Title: Asociación Iberoamericana de Víctimas Contra la Violencia Vial (FICVI)
LinkTitle: Asociación Iberoamericana de Víctimas Contra la Violencia Vial (FICVI)
NSAObjectives:
Nuestro Objetivo es "Cero víctimas en el tránsito"

Para ello nos enfocamos en: 

1. Proponer, defender y llevar a cabo, acciones que promuevan la seguridad vial, dirigidas a la opinión pública, a las fuerzas sociales y políticas, así como a los grupos empresariales y organismos del sector privado.

2. Promover la investigación y el desarrollo de metodologías de atención a víctimas de siniestros de tránsito y sus familiares, tanto en emergencias como en atención secundaria.

3.  Apoyar el fortalecimiento institucional de las asociaciones miembros.

Para ello actuamos en:

Generar un espacio de encuentro y comunicación para compartir informaciones, experiencias y conocimiento sobre la siniestralidad vial y su prevención.

Impulsar la interlocución entre las asociaciones miembros y las instituciones públicas y privadas, que favorezcan el desarrollo de políticas públicas que eviten la ocurrencia de siniestros y/o disminuyan su letalidad en caso de presentarse.

Contribuir a la formación de profesionales en la preparación y gestión de proyectos de seguridad vial, capacitación, apoyo organizativo y desarrollo técnico.

Promover y apoyar la realización de estudios e investigaciones en distintos temas relacionados con la seguridad vial 
 

Reivindicaciones y Exigencias:

1. Una política de estado prioritaria.

2. La creación de Observatorios Nacionales de Seguridad Vial.

3. La educación vial en todos los niveles de enseñanza.

4. Un sistema riguroso y estricto de otorgamiento de licencias de conducir.

5. Que las autoridades controlen y castiguen con severidad a los conductores temerarios.

6. Que se contemple en el código penal los delitos cometidos contra la seguridad vial.

7. Que destinen los recursos económicos para garantizar el derecho a una movilidad segura y sostenible.

8. De nuestros gobiernos un protocolo eficaz de atención sanitaria inmediata y post hospitalaria.

9. Que mejoren la seguridad de las infraestructuras, y regulen las condiciones técnicas que deben cumplir los vehículos.

10. Compromisos mutuos porque todos somos parte de la solución.
NSAWorkFields:
ATENCIÓN A VÍCTIMAS DE SINIESTROS VIALES. Publicamos  la Guía Iberoamericana de Atención integral a Víctimas de Siniestros Viales con el fin de crear Unidades de Atención a Víctimas de Siniestros de Tránsito, así como un Manual de formación dirigido al personal de las UAVT, y un Protocolo de actuación que sirva de orientación a las autoridades de gobierno y los profesionales, para apoyar a las víctimas y sus familias en la defensa de sus derechos.

MOVILIDAD SEGURA Y SOSTENIBLE con objetivos claros, entre otros: 
Configurar un modelo de transporte público más eficiente para mejorar la competitividad del sistema productivo.
Mejorar la integración social de los ciudadanos aportando una accesibilidad más universal.
No comprometer las condiciones de salud de los ciudadanos.

VEHICULOS MÁS SEGUROS somos miembros de LatinNcap y apoyamos el programa de evaluación de los vehículos nuevos para América latina y damos información a los consumidores sobre los niveles de seguridad de los vehículos

Perez Nunez, Ricardo (WDC) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 10/30/2025 12:39:57 PM. Comments: Muchas gracias por la consulta. Si bien no ha habido acuerdos formales firmados en el pasado entre la OPS y FICVI, si ha habido una larga lista de colaboración en general, incluyendo proyectos y actividades conjuntas que dan cuenta de la estrecha colaboración entre ambas organizaciones. Hago notar, que la colaboración ha sido a nivel global (OMS, UNRSC), regional (con la Oficina Regional de OPS/OMS) y nacional (tanto FICVI como sus miembros han venido trabajando de la mano con distintas Oficinas de OPS/OMS en distintos países). Global: FICVI es miembro de la UN Road Safety Collaboration (https://www.who.int/groups/united-nations-road-safety-collaboration/unrsc-membership), prácticamente desde el inicio, apoyando el trabajo de OPS y OMS para impulsar la agenda de seguridad vial. Regional: Como parte del proyecto (en proceso de revisión corporativa) del UNRSF titulado “Strengthening legal frameworks to promote motorcycle safety in 5 countries of the Americas”, FICVI participó en el diseño y será miembro/aliado clave para trabajar en su implementación en los países participantes. De la mano de APASIT, la Oficina de OPS/OMS en Guatemala, está en el proceso de realizar una Evaluación de la Atención de Emergencias y Cuidados Críticos empleando la herramienta ECCSA de OMS. Esto, como parte del proyecto de la Oficina Regional titulado “Strengthening Emergency Care Systems in the Americas”. Esto es el resultado de una serie de reuniones virtuales/presenciales en las que hemos identificado estrategias específicas para impulsar esta importante agenda de forma conjunta. Este año, la OPS/OMS recibió el invaluable apoyo de FICVI para llevar a cabo el Taller Regional sobre Periodismo de Soluciones para Periodistas Latinoamericanos y Oficiales de Comunicación del 22 al 24 de julio (https://www.paho.org/en/news/28-7-2025-pahowho-convenes-journalists-reshape-how-road-safety-covered-latin-america). Tanto FICVI como OPS somos parte del Comité Internacional de Cooperación del OISEVI (organismo que es parte de la SEGIB, con quien OPS recién ha firmado un Acuerdo Marco de Colaboración Técnica). También en este espacio trabajamos de la mano para promover conjuntamente la implementación de las Recomendaciones del Plan Mundial para el Segundo Decenio de Acción para la Seguridad Vial 2021-2030 en los países de la Región. Ejemplos de colaboración a nivel nacional: “Perspectivas sobre la Seguridad de Niños y Adolescentes en el Tráfico”, promovido por la Fundación Thiago de Moraes Gonzaga de Brasil, miembro de FICVI, se celebró el 10 de mayo de 2022 y tuvo lugar en Brasilia, con el objetivo de contribuir al fortalecimiento del recién creado Frente Parlamentario para la Seguridad de Niños y Adolescentes en el Tránsito (FPSCAT). El encuentro contó con el importante apoyo de la Organización Panamericana de la Salud (OPS/OMS), reforzando el compromiso internacional con la prevención de lesiones y muertes por accidentes de tránsito entre niños y adolescentes. La OPS estuvo representada por Victor Pavarino, que participó en la mesa redonda junto a autoridades y expertos en seguridad vial. La presencia de la organización resaltó la relevancia del tema en la agenda de salud pública y su alineación con los esfuerzos globales por ciudades más seguras. El evento también incluyó una dinámica interactiva que reunió a representantes de diversos sectores de la sociedad para discutir las prioridades y estrategias de acción de la FPSCAT, con especial énfasis en la protección de los usuarios vulnerables en las diferentes realidades regionales del país. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uL3WNxMP9oc • OPS apoyó el desarrollo de esta publicación de la Fundación Thiago de Moraes Gonzaga: Perda Sem Nome (2012): https://www3.paho.org/hq/dmdocuments/2013/Paho-sde-perda-sem-nome.pdf • Y su actualización en 2023: https://iris.paho.org/bitstream/handle/10665.2/57468/9789275727294_por.pdf?sequence=1&isAllowed=y o El 15 de agosto de 2022, la Fundación Thiago de Moraes Gonzaga (FTMG) lanzó, en colaboración con la Organización Panamericana de la Salud (OPS), el libro electrónico «Pérdida sin nombre - Cómo aprender a vivir con la ausencia de los seres queridos», en la Asamblea Legislativa de RS. La asociación con la OPS refuerza el reconocimiento internacional de la labor de la Fundación en la ayuda a las familias en duelo por siniestros viales. El representante de la OPS participó virtualmente en el acto, que conmemoraba el décimo aniversario de la primera edición del libro. La nueva versión actualiza las experiencias vividas durante la pandemia de Covid-19 y comparte historias del Grupo de Apoyo a Padres, coordinado por psicólogos de la FTMG. El libro, basado en la experiencia de Diza Gonzaga, fundadora de la Fundación, busca apoyar el duelo de madres y padres, ofreciendo formas de seguir viviendo con la ausencia de hijos e hijas víctimas de accidentes de tránsito. La asociación con la OPS refuerza el alcance y la relevancia de esta iniciativa, que promueve la salud mental y la valoración de la vida. • CONECTA Vida Urgente | Olhares para a Mobilidade (La movilidad en el punto de mira), es una iniciativa de la Fundación Thiago Moraes de Gonzaga que promueve el debate sobre la seguridad vial y la preservación de la vida, reuniendo diferentes perspectivas sobre el tráfico en Brasil. En su edición del 26 de abril de 2022, realizada online, el evento destacó la importancia de la protección de niños y adolescentes en el tránsito y el papel del país en el esfuerzo mundial por carreteras más seguras. La asociación con la Oficina de OPS/OMS en Brasil fue uno de los elementos más destacados de la noche. La presencia de la OPS refuerza la relevancia internacional de la agenda y fortalece el papel de la Fundación Thiago Gonzaga en la construcción de un sistema de tránsito más humano y seguro. https://vidaurgente.org.br/conecta-vida-urgente-recebe-especialistas-em-edicao-especial/ La Fundación Reconocer, de Panamá recibió apoyo económico de OPS para la filmación y edición de un video para promocionar el foro Repensemos la Movilidad celebrado el 23 de mayo de 2024, organizado por Fundación Reconocer y el Consejo Nacional de Tránsito y Seguridad Vial, durante la Semana Mundial de la Seguridad Vial. Link al video: https://www.instagram.com/p/CsZ82USOGtA/ En 2024, OPS hizo un llamado para colaborar con una campaña preventiva antes de los Carnavales. Fundación Reconocer participó activamente e hizo una propuesta escrita formal con el lema CALMA como idea central de la campaña, basado en buenas prácticas internacionales que resaltan el calmado de velocidades como medida efectiva para evitar siniestros. Además, se propuso la idea principal para los guiones de los videos, inspirados por una campaña de la DGT en España. Todos los colaboradores de la campaña aceptaron la propuesta. La campaña fue promocionada a través de las redes de la Autoridad de Tránsito y Transporte Terrestre de Panamá, la Policía Nacional, el Consejo Nacional de Tránsito y Seguridad Vial de Panamá, Fundación Reconocer, Fundación Mónica Licona y otros colaboradores. También se hizo una conferencia de prensa, a la cual asistieron los medios. VIVIAC, parte de FICVI, fue socio local clave para el impulso de la Ley de Movilidad en Jalisco (en 2012) y miembro de la Coalición de Movilidad Segura que, de la mano de la Oficina de OPS/OMS en México, impulsó la Ley de Movilidad Segura en México en 2023. Esto, como parte de la iniciativa BIGRS de Bloomberg. https://www.paho.org/es/noticias/1-5-2022-nueva-ley-general-movilidad-seguridad-vial-mexico-podria-cambiar-reglas-juego Espero esta información contribuya a la revisión. Atento por si fuera de utilidad ampliar esta información o brindar información adicional.

Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as LEG Reviewer on 10/30/2025 10:55:11 AM. Comments: Estimado Ricardo, En el marco del análisis que estamos realizando y conforme a los criterios establecidos en FENSA, hemos verificado que las interacciones identificadas entre la OPS y la Federación Iberoamericana de Víctimas contra la Violencia Vial (FICVI) se han dado principalmente a través de su participación en reuniones y eventos organizados por la Organización. Al respecto, el marco regulatorio nos pide verificar que entre las partes se hayan mantenido al menos dos años de interacción sistemática, documentada y evaluada por ambas partes como mutuamente beneficiosa y no solamente reuniones. Es por lo anterior que necesitamos verificar la existencia de una colaboración sistemática que cumpla plenamente con los requisitos establecidos por FENSA para el reconocimiento de relaciones oficiales. En ese sentido, agradeceríamos su apoyo para confirmar si existen otros tipos de interacciones o formas de colaboración más estrechas —tales como proyectos conjuntos, actividades de cooperación técnica o acuerdos formales— que debamos considerar en esta evaluación. Gracias

TitleENG: Ibero-American Association of Victims Against Road Violence (FICVI)
TitleENGSPA: Asociación Iberoamericana de Víctimas Contra la Violencia Vial (FICVI)
NSAObjetivesENG:
Our goal is "Zero victims in traffic."

To achieve this, we focus on:

1. Proposing, defending, and implementing actions that promote road safety, aimed at public opinion, social and political forces, as well as business groups and private sector organizations.

2. Promoting research and the development of methodologies for assisting victims of traffic accidents and their families, both in emergencies and in secondary care.

3. Supporting the institutional strengthening of member associations.

To this end, we act by:

Creating a space for meeting and communication to share information, experiences, and knowledge about road traffic accidents and their prevention.

Encouraging dialogue between member associations and public and private institutions to foster the development of public policies that prevent accidents and/or reduce their lethality when they occur.

Contributing to the training of professionals in the preparation and management of road safety projects, training, organizational support, and technical development.

Promoting and supporting the conduct of studies and research on various topics related to road safety.

### Claims and Demands:

1. A priority state policy.

2. The creation of National Road Safety Observatories.

3. Road safety education at all levels of education.

4. A rigorous and strict system for granting driver's licenses.

5. That authorities control and severely punish reckless drivers.

6. That crimes against road safety be included in the penal code.

7. That economic resources be allocated to guarantee the right to safe and sustainable mobility.

8. An effective protocol from our governments for immediate and post-hospital healthcare.

9. Improvements in the safety of infrastructure and regulations on the technical conditions that vehicles must meet.

10. Mutual commitments because we are all part of the solution.

NSAWorkFieldsENG:
### Attention to Victims of Traffic Accidents

We publish the Ibero-American Guide for Comprehensive Care of Victims of Traffic Accidents in order to create Units for the Care of Traffic Accident Victims, as well as a Training Manual aimed at the staff of the UAVT, and a Protocol of Action that serves as guidance for government authorities and professionals, to support victims and their families in defending their rights.

### Safe and Sustainable Mobility

With clear objectives, among others:
- To configure a more efficient public transport model to improve the competitiveness of the productive system.
- To enhance the social integration of citizens by providing more universal accessibility.
- To not compromise the health conditions of citizens.

### Safer Vehicles

We are members of LatinNcap and support the program for evaluating new vehicles for Latin America, providing consumers with information about the safety levels of vehicles.

TitleFRE: Association ibéro-américaine des victimes contre la violence routière (FICVI)
TitlePOR: Associação Ibero-Americana de Vítimas Contra a Violência Viária (FICVI)
NSAObjectivesFRE:
### Traduction

Notre objectif est "Zéro victimes sur la route"

Pour cela, nous nous concentrons sur :

1. Proposer, défendre et mettre en œuvre des actions qui promeuvent la sécurité routière, destinées à l'opinion publique, aux forces sociales et politiques, ainsi qu'aux groupes d'entreprises et organismes du secteur privé.

2. Promouvoir la recherche et le développement de méthodologies d'assistance aux victimes d'accidents de la route et à leurs familles, tant en cas d'urgence qu'en soins secondaires.

3. Soutenir le renforcement institutionnel des associations membres.

Pour cela, nous agissons en :

Générant un espace de rencontre et de communication pour partager des informations, des expériences et des connaissances sur l'accidentalité routière et sa prévention.

Encourageant le dialogue entre les associations membres et les institutions publiques et privées, favorisant le développement de politiques publiques qui évitent la survenue d'accidents et/ou diminuent leur létalité en cas de survenance.

Contribuant à la formation de professionnels dans la préparation et la gestion de projets de sécurité routière, la formation, le soutien organisationnel et le développement technique.

Promouvant et soutenant la réalisation d'études et de recherches sur divers sujets liés à la sécurité routière.

### Revendications et Exigences :

1. Une politique d'État prioritaire.

2. La création d'Observatoires Nationaux de Sécurité Routière.

3. L'éducation routière à tous les niveaux d'enseignement.

4. Un système rigoureux et strict d'octroi de permis de conduire.

5. Que les autorités contrôlent et punissent sévèrement les conducteurs imprudents.

6. Que le code pénal prenne en compte les délits commis contre la sécurité routière.

7. Que des ressources économiques soient allouées pour garantir le droit à une mobilité sûre et durable.

8. De nos gouvernements, un protocole efficace d'assistance sanitaire immédiate et post-hospitalière.

9. Que la sécurité des infrastructures soit améliorée et que les conditions techniques que doivent respecter les véhicules soient réglementées.

10. Des engagements mutuels car nous faisons tous partie de la solution.
NSAObjectivesPOR:
Nosso objetivo é "Zero vítimas no trânsito"

Para isso, nos concentramos em:

1. Propor, defender e realizar ações que promovam a segurança viária, direcionadas à opinião pública, às forças sociais e políticas, assim como aos grupos empresariais e organismos do setor privado.

2. Promover a pesquisa e o desenvolvimento de metodologias de atendimento a vítimas de acidentes de trânsito e seus familiares, tanto em emergências quanto em atendimento secundário.

3. Apoiar o fortalecimento institucional das associações membros.

Para isso, atuamos em:

Gerar um espaço de encontro e comunicação para compartilhar informações, experiências e conhecimentos sobre a sinistralidade viária e sua prevenção.

Impulsionar a interlocução entre as associações membros e as instituições públicas e privadas, que favoreçam o desenvolvimento de políticas públicas que evitem a ocorrência de acidentes e/ou diminuam sua letalidade em caso de acontecerem.

Contribuir para a formação de profissionais na preparação e gestão de projetos de segurança viária, capacitação, apoio organizacional e desenvolvimento técnico.

Promover e apoiar a realização de estudos e pesquisas em diferentes temas relacionados à segurança viária.

Reivindicações e Exigências:

1. Uma política de estado prioritária.

2. A criação de Observatórios Nacionais de Segurança Viária.

3. A educação viária em todos os níveis de ensino.

4. Um sistema rigoroso e estrito de concessão de licenças de condução.

5. Que as autoridades controlem e punam severamente os condutores temerários.

6. Que sejam contemplados no código penal os crimes cometidos contra a segurança viária.

7. Que destinem recursos econômicos para garantir o direito a uma mobilidade segura e sustentável.

8. De nossos governos, um protocolo eficaz de atendimento sanitário imediato e pós-hospitalar.

9. Que melhorem a segurança das infraestruturas e regulem as condições técnicas que os veículos devem cumprir.

10. Compromissos mútuos porque todos somos parte da solução.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
ATENTION AUX VICTIMES D'ACCIDENTS DE LA ROUTE. Nous publions le Guide ibéro-américain d'Attention intégrale aux Victimes d'Accidents de la Route afin de créer des Unités d'Attention aux Victimes d'Accidents de la Circulation, ainsi qu'un Manuel de formation destiné au personnel des UAVT, et un Protocole d'action qui serve d'orientation aux autorités gouvernementales et aux professionnels, pour soutenir les victimes et leurs familles dans la défense de leurs droits.

MOBILITÉ SÛRE ET DURABLE avec des objectifs clairs, parmi lesquels : 
Configurer un modèle de transport public plus efficace pour améliorer la compétitivité du système productif.
Améliorer l'intégration sociale des citoyens en offrant une accessibilité plus universelle.
Ne pas compromettre les conditions de santé des citoyens.

VÉHICULES PLUS SÉCURISÉS, nous sommes membres de LatinNcap et soutenons le programme d'évaluation des nouveaux véhicules pour l'Amérique latine et fournissons des informations aux consommateurs sur les niveaux de sécurité des véhicules.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
ATENÇÃO A VÍTIMAS DE ACIDENTES DE TRÂNSITO. Publicamos a Guia Ibero-Americana de Atendimento Integral a Vítimas de Acidentes de Trânsito com o objetivo de criar Unidades de Atendimento a Vítimas de Acidentes de Trânsito, assim como um Manual de formação dirigido ao pessoal das UAVT, e um Protocolo de atuação que sirva de orientação às autoridades governamentais e aos profissionais, para apoiar as vítimas e suas famílias na defesa de seus direitos.

MOBILIDADE SEGURA E SUSTENTÁVEL com objetivos claros, entre outros: 
Configurar um modelo de transporte público mais eficiente para melhorar a competitividade do sistema produtivo.
Melhorar a integração social dos cidadãos, proporcionando uma acessibilidade mais universal.
Não comprometer as condições de saúde dos cidadãos.

VEÍCULOS MAIS SEGUROS somos membros do LatinNcap e apoiamos o programa de avaliação dos veículos novos para a América Latina e fornecemos informações aos consumidores sobre os níveis de segurança dos veículos.

Title: Clean Air Institute (CAI)
NSAObjectives: The Clean Air Institute (CAI) is organized exclusively for charitable, scientific and education purposes. The Institute´s mission is to promote policies, strategies, and technologies that can reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve air quality to protect public health and promote a sustainable environment.
NSAWorkFields:
- Integrated air quality management: Ensuring healthy air quality is vital for public health, environmental well-being, and achieving the SDGs. The Clean Air Institute (CAI) has extensive experience and achievements in the LAC region.
- Environmental health: CAI strengthens health, environmental, and related sectors through tools such as health impact assessments and risk communication, while engaging communities to protect and improve public health.
- Sustainable mobility and transport: It promotes cleaner, more accessible mobility, supporting governments, businesses, and communities in adopting Integrated Environmental Strategies that reduce emissions, improve quality of life, and expand opportunities.
- Low-carbon smart development: CAI empowers companies with knowledge and tools to cut emissions, enhance performance, and gain social recognition, helping them achieve competitiveness while reducing their environmental footprint.
NSABoardMembers:
•	Dr. Alan Loyd, is the Chairman of the Board of Directors at Clean Air Institute. 
Affiliation: President of the International Council on Clean Transportation, ICCT, and Research Associate; Center for Electromechanics, Cockrell School of Engineering, The University of Texas at Austin.
•	Dr. Dayana Agudelo Castañeda
Affiliation: Researcher and professor at the Universidad del Norte in Barranquilla, Colombia.
•	Dr. Pablo Cicero Fernández
Affiliation: Air Pollution Specialist at the California Air Resources Board in the United States.

History:
Luna, Cristina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 12/19/2025 04:10:19 PM. Comments: ERP recommends proceeding with the designation as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. In addition, the FENSA review and risk analysis conducted by LEG has also been carefully considered. While ERP supports moving forward with the designation based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 12/4/2025 01:41:19 PM. Comments: Dear Juan: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed the background of The Clean Air Institute, which is applying to be in official relations with PAHO with a work plan centered in environmental health issues. LEG recommendation is to proceed with the designation, as long as DHE believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO carries out due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-state actors. Based on the documentation provided by The Clean Air Institute and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments: 1. For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to The Clean Air Institute the policies and procedures applicable to a non-governmental organization (NGO). 2. Legal status: U.S. non-profit corporation (501(c)(3)) incorporated in Washington, D.C. in 2006 to continue the World Bank’s Clean Air Initiative for Latin American Cities. 3. Mission/objectives: To improve air quality and mitigate climate change in Latin America through technical cooperation, research, and capacity building in urban air-quality management and sustainable transport. 4. Main income source: Program-service revenue from international donors and multilateral projects. The major donor is the Clean Air Fund. 5. Governance: Board of Directors includes Dr. Alan Lloyd (Chair), Dr. Dayana Agudelo-Castañeda, Mario Molina, and Dr. Pablo Cicero-Fernández. Executive Director is Sergio Sanchez. All members were screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List and no matches were found. 6. The Clean Air Institute declared no links with tobacco and arms industries on September 4, 2025; and no links were uncovered by LEG either. The risk analysis consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of participating in this collaboration, while protecting and preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. In our searches, we found no evidence of financial, ethical, or reputational concerns. The Institute’s profile and activities are consistent with PAHO’s Strategic Plan and its collaboration history with PAHO is positive. Therefore, LEG recommendation is to proceed with the designation, as long as ERP believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Please do not hesitate to contact LEG should you require further assistance. Sincerely, LEG

NSAObjetivesENG: The Clean Air Institute (CAI) is organized exclusively for charitable, scientific, and educational purposes. The Institute's mission is to promote policies, strategies, and technologies that can reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve air quality to protect public health and promote a sustainable environment.
NSAObjetivesSPA: El Instituto del Aire Limpio (CAI) está organizado exclusivamente con fines benéficos, científicos y educativos. La misión del Instituto es promover políticas, estrategias y tecnologías que puedan reducir las emisiones de gases de efecto invernadero y mejorar la calidad del aire para proteger la salud pública y promover un ambiente sostenible.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
- Integrated air quality management: Ensuring healthy air quality is vital for public health, environmental well-being, and achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The Clean Air Institute (CAI) has extensive experience and achievements in the Latin America and Caribbean (LAC) region.
- Environmental health: CAI strengthens health, environmental, and related sectors through tools such as health impact assessments and risk communication, while engaging communities to protect and improve public health.
- Sustainable mobility and transport: It promotes cleaner, more accessible mobility, supporting governments, businesses, and communities in adopting Integrated Environmental Strategies that reduce emissions, improve quality of life, and expand opportunities.
- Low-carbon smart development: CAI empowers companies with knowledge and tools to cut emissions, enhance performance, and gain social recognition, helping them achieve competitiveness while reducing their environmental footprint.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
- Gestión integrada de la calidad del aire: Asegurar una calidad del aire saludable es vital para la salud pública, el bienestar ambiental y el logro de los ODS. El Instituto del Aire Limpio (CAI) tiene una amplia experiencia y logros en la región de LAC.
- Salud ambiental: El CAI fortalece los sectores de salud, medio ambiente y relacionados a través de herramientas como evaluaciones de impacto en la salud y comunicación de riesgos, al mismo tiempo que involucra a las comunidades para proteger y mejorar la salud pública.
- Movilidad y transporte sostenibles: Promueve una movilidad más limpia y accesible, apoyando a gobiernos, empresas y comunidades en la adopción de Estrategias Ambientales Integradas que reduzcan las emisiones, mejoren la calidad de vida y amplíen las oportunidades.
- Desarrollo inteligente y bajo en carbono: El CAI empodera a las empresas con conocimientos y herramientas para reducir emisiones, mejorar el rendimiento y obtener reconocimiento social, ayudándolas a lograr competitividad mientras reducen su huella ambiental.

NSAObjectivesFRE: L'Institut de l'Air Pur (IAP) est organisé exclusivement à des fins caritatives, scientifiques et éducatives. La mission de l'Institut est de promouvoir des politiques, des stratégies et des technologies qui peuvent réduire les émissions de gaz à effet de serre et améliorer la qualité de l'air afin de protéger la santé publique et de favoriser un environnement durable.
NSAObjectivesPOR: O Instituto do Ar Limpo (IAL) é organizado exclusivamente para fins de caridade, científicos e educacionais. A missão do Instituto é promover políticas, estratégias e tecnologias que possam reduzir as emissões de gases de efeito estufa e melhorar a qualidade do ar para proteger a saúde pública e promover um ambiente sustentável.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
- Gestion intégrée de la qualité de l'air : Assurer une qualité de l'air saine est vital pour la santé publique, le bien-être environnemental et l'atteinte des ODD. L'Institut de l'air pur (IAP) possède une vaste expérience et des réalisations dans la région LAC.
- Santé environnementale : L'IAP renforce les secteurs de la santé, de l'environnement et des secteurs connexes grâce à des outils tels que les évaluations d'impact sur la santé et la communication des risques, tout en engageant les communautés à protéger et améliorer la santé publique.
- Mobilité et transport durables : Il promeut une mobilité plus propre et plus accessible, soutenant les gouvernements, les entreprises et les communautés dans l'adoption de stratégies environnementales intégrées qui réduisent les émissions, améliorent la qualité de vie et élargissent les opportunités.
- Développement intelligent à faible émission de carbone : L'IAP donne aux entreprises les connaissances et les outils nécessaires pour réduire les émissions, améliorer les performances et obtenir une reconnaissance sociale, les aidant à atteindre la compétitivité tout en réduisant leur empreinte environnementale.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
- Gestão integrada da qualidade do ar: Garantir uma qualidade do ar saudável é vital para a saúde pública, o bem-estar ambiental e a realização dos ODS. O Clean Air Institute (CAI) possui ampla experiência e conquistas na região da América Latina e Caribe (LAC).
- Saúde ambiental: O CAI fortalece os setores de saúde, meio ambiente e relacionados por meio de ferramentas como avaliações de impacto na saúde e comunicação de riscos, ao mesmo tempo em que envolve as comunidades na proteção e melhoria da saúde pública.
- Mobilidade e transporte sustentáveis: Promove uma mobilidade mais limpa e acessível, apoiando governos, empresas e comunidades na adoção de Estratégias Ambientais Integradas que reduzem emissões, melhoram a qualidade de vida e expandem oportunidades.
- Desenvolvimento inteligente de baixo carbono: O CAI capacita as empresas com conhecimento e ferramentas para reduzir emissões, melhorar o desempenho e ganhar reconhecimento social, ajudando-as a alcançar competitividade enquanto reduzem sua pegada ambiental.

Title: American Society for Microbiology (ASM)
NSAObjectives:
The American Society for Microbiology is a professional life science organization composed of more than 37,000 scientists, educators and health professionals who are dedicated to promoting and advancing microbial sciences around the world.

We strive to make the microbial sciences the most welcoming field in STEM by elevating and embodying Progress, Impact, Access and Accountability. ASM works to create a worldwide community and culture where microbiologists can come together, connect, learn and recharge for the future.

ASM Promotes and Supports the Future of the Microbial Sciences. It is our responsibility to empower and connect microbial scientists and stakeholders around the world to shape the future of the field. ASM embraces this responsibility, leveraging fundamental and applied science to positively impact life on, and the sustainability of, our planet.
NSAWorkFields:
1. Convene experts and members at ASM meetings (https://asm.org/events)
2. Publish innovative science (https://journals.asm.org/)
3. Advocate for science (https://asm.org/advocacy)
4. Build microbiology laboratory capacity (https://asm.org/global-health)
NSABoardMembers: The ASM Board of Directors (https://asm.org/asm-governance-committees/board-of-directors-overview), supported by the Committees of the Board (https://asm.org/asm-governance-committees/board-of-directors-overview), provides strategic direction to our Society. It is responsible for the organizational, business, legal, financial and governance matters of our organization. The Board of Directors is chaired by the president. Annual elections to the board begin in the late summer each year with a call for nominations from ASM membership.
NSAOrganizationBodies:
United Nations 
World Health Organization
African Society for Laboratory Medicine

Campa Sole, Regina (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 1/9/2026 04:23:24 PM. Comments: 
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 1/9/2026 04:21:54 PM. Comments:Dear colleagues: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed your FENSA request for the American Society for Microbiology (ASM) to renew their status as non-state actor in official relations with PAHO.  Subject to the considerations indicated below, LEG recommendation is to proceed with the renewal of the status, as long as the Focal Point believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO.  
Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO carries out due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-state actors. Based on the documentation provided by the ASM and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments:
For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to ASM the policies and procedures applicable to non-governmental organizations.
ASM is a non-profit professional and scientific society incorporated in the United States of America (Washington, D.C.), founded in 1899 and registered as a 501(c)(3) organization.
Its mission is to advance the microbial sciences through research, education, publications, and capacity development, with the goal of improving health and safeguarding the environment.
Its main sources of income are membership dues, scientific publishing, conference fees, and corporate sponsorship via an ASM Corporate Council composed largely of diagnostics and pharmaceutical companies.
ASM is governed by a Board of Directors and Council on Microbial Sciences. Current officers include a President, President-Elect, Treasurer, and Chief Executive Officer. Members screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List – no matches were found.
The AMS declared no links with tobacco and arms industries on 10 September 2025; and no links were uncovered by LEG either.
Other industries (pharma, alcohol, chemicals, food & beverage, medical services): ASM maintains a Corporate Council that includes pharmaceutical and diagnostic manufacturers. LEG notes that such sponsorship could pose a potential perceived conflict of interest and should be managed through strict firewalls and transparency measures.
The risk analysis consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of participating in this collaboration, while protecting and preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. In our searches, we found that ASM is a well-established, reputable scientific organization whose mission and activities are aligned with PAHO’s public-health objectives, particularly in laboratory quality management, biosafety, and antimicrobial resistance (AMR). However, the existence of corporate sponsorships requires that any joint activity be carefully structured to ensure independence and avoid perception of endorsement.
Subject to the considerations indicated above, ASM’s renewal may proceed, as long as the  collaborations with PAHO are not earmarked or directly financed by potentially problematic industries (including but not limited to diagnostics, pharmaceutical, alcohol, or food and beverage sectors) and do not create a perceived or actual conflict of interest. Under these conditions, the potential risks associated with ASM’s external links are considered adequately mitigated.
Sincerely,

Looking ahead to the next work plan, activities will include participation in webinars, provision of technical support during country missions, and delivery of training sessions as needed. With best regards

Luna, Cristina (Consultant WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as ERP Reviewer on 11/6/2025 12:22:34 PM. Comments: Dear Pilar, Thank you for clearing the ASM renewal form. After reviewing it in ERP, we identified points that need clarification to ensure consistency with the 2023–2025 approved work plan and to clarify the proposed 2026–2028 plan. Please review the observations below and share any additional details through the system. If needed, we can consult with the NSA for further clarification. Past Activities (2023–2025) The approved plan included two lines of collaboration (I am sending the 2023 background paper with the full plan by email): Standardization of AST protocols for macrolides and submission to CLSI/EUCAST for review and dissemination in the Region. Dissemination of recommended methodologies for detection, diagnosis, and surveillance of Bordetella pertussis in Brazil and Mexico, aiming to expand the network and transfer capacities to other countries. Reported activities (policy conference, ReLAVRA webinar, Mexico workshop) are aligned with the overall objective but do not clearly indicate progress on these components. Could you clarify whether actions were taken and provide details on outcomes and PAHO–ASM collaboration? If additional activities were implemented, please add them in a separate section. Proposed Work Plan (2026–2028) Please provide: More details on planned collaborative activities, including expected actions and contributions from PAHO and ASM. Clarification on whether these initiatives are based on results from the previous cycle (2023–2025). If so, please include this linkage. Thank you for your collaboration and support. Best regards,

En cuanto a las actividades propuestas, les agradeceriamos si pudieran aclarar si dichas iniciativas estaran basadas en los resultados de las actividades realizadas en el anterior ciclo. Muchas gracias
Ramon-Pardo, Pilar (WDC) submitted request as PAHO Focal Point for review on 9/26/2025 11:35:07 AM. Comments: The American Society for Microbiology (ASM) offers significant value to the AMR Special Program at PAHO by serving as a key partner in advancing microbiological capacity, research, and education across the Americas. ASM’s global expertise in laboratory standards, antimicrobial susceptibility testing, and microbial surveillance directly supports PAHO’s efforts to strengthen national AMR surveillance systems and laboratory networks. Their commitment to innovation and equity aligns with PAHO’s mission to combat AMR through multisectoral and sustainable approaches, therefore we strongly support the renewal of this official relation with ASM.

NSAObjetivesENG:
The American Society for Microbiology is a professional life science organization made up of over 37,000 scientists, educators, and health professionals dedicated to promoting and advancing microbial sciences globally.

We aim to make the microbial sciences the most inclusive field in STEM by emphasizing and embodying Progress, Impact, Access, and Accountability. ASM works to foster a global community and culture where microbiologists can unite, connect, learn, and recharge for the future.

ASM promotes and supports the future of microbial sciences. It is our duty to empower and connect microbial scientists and stakeholders worldwide to shape the future of the field. ASM embraces this responsibility, utilizing fundamental and applied science to positively influence life on our planet and its sustainability.
NSAObjetivesSPA:
La Sociedad Americana de Microbiología es una organización profesional de ciencias de la vida compuesta por más de 37,000 científicos, educadores y profesionales de la salud que están dedicados a promover y avanzar en las ciencias microbianas en todo el mundo.

Nos esforzamos por hacer de las ciencias microbianas el campo más acogedor en STEM al elevar y encarnar el Progreso, el Impacto, el Acceso y la Responsabilidad. ASM trabaja para crear una comunidad y una cultura mundial donde los microbiólogos puedan reunirse, conectarse, aprender y recargar energías para el futuro.

ASM promueve y apoya el futuro de las ciencias microbianas. Es nuestra responsabilidad empoderar y conectar a los científicos microbianos y a las partes interesadas de todo el mundo para dar forma al futuro del campo. ASM abraza esta responsabilidad, aprovechando la ciencia fundamental y aplicada para impactar positivamente la vida en nuestro planeta y la sostenibilidad del mismo.

NSAObjectivesFRE:
La Société Américaine de Microbiologie est une organisation professionnelle des sciences de la vie composée de plus de 37 000 scientifiques, éducateurs et professionnels de la santé qui se consacrent à la promotion et à l'avancement des sciences microbiennes à travers le monde.

Nous nous efforçons de faire des sciences microbiennes le domaine le plus accueillant dans les STEM en élevant et en incarnant le Progrès, l'Impact, l'Accès et la Responsabilité. L'ASM travaille à créer une communauté et une culture mondiales où les microbiologistes peuvent se rassembler, se connecter, apprendre et se ressourcer pour l'avenir.

L'ASM promeut et soutient l'avenir des sciences microbiennes. Il est de notre responsabilité d'autonomiser et de connecter les scientifiques microbiologiques et les parties prenantes du monde entier pour façonner l'avenir du domaine. L'ASM embrasse cette responsabilité, en s'appuyant sur la science fondamentale et appliquée pour avoir un impact positif sur la vie sur notre planète et sa durabilité.
NSAObjectivesPOR:
A Sociedade Americana de Microbiologia é uma organização profissional de ciências biológicas composta por mais de 37.000 cientistas, educadores e profissionais de saúde dedicados a promover e avançar as ciências microbianas em todo o mundo.

Nos esforçamos para tornar as ciências microbianas o campo mais acolhedor em STEM, elevando e incorporando Progresso, Impacto, Acesso e Responsabilidade. A ASM trabalha para criar uma comunidade e cultura mundial onde os microbiologistas possam se reunir, conectar, aprender e recarregar as energias para o futuro.

A ASM promove e apoia o futuro das ciências microbianas. É nossa responsabilidade capacitar e conectar cientistas microbianos e partes interessadas em todo o mundo para moldar o futuro do campo. A ASM abraça essa responsabilidade, aproveitando a ciência fundamental e aplicada para impactar positivamente a vida em nosso planeta e a sustentabilidade do mesmo.

NSAObjetivesENG:
The American Society for Microbiology is a professional life science organization made up of over 37,000 scientists, educators, and health professionals dedicated to promoting and advancing microbial sciences globally.

We aim to make the microbial sciences the most inclusive field in STEM by emphasizing and embodying Progress, Impact, Access, and Accountability. ASM works to foster a global community and culture where microbiologists can unite, connect, learn, and recharge for the future.

ASM promotes and supports the future of microbial sciences. It is our duty to empower and connect microbial scientists and stakeholders worldwide to shape the future of the field. ASM embraces this responsibility, utilizing fundamental and applied science to positively influence life on our planet and its sustainability.
NSAObjetivesSPA:
La Sociedad Americana de Microbiología es una organización profesional de ciencias de la vida compuesta por más de 37,000 científicos, educadores y profesionales de la salud que están dedicados a promover y avanzar en las ciencias microbianas en todo el mundo.

Nos esforzamos por hacer de las ciencias microbianas el campo más acogedor en STEM al elevar y encarnar el Progreso, el Impacto, el Acceso y la Responsabilidad. ASM trabaja para crear una comunidad y una cultura mundial donde los microbiólogos puedan reunirse, conectarse, aprender y recargar energías para el futuro.

ASM promueve y apoya el futuro de las ciencias microbianas. Es nuestra responsabilidad empoderar y conectar a los científicos microbianos y a las partes interesadas de todo el mundo para dar forma al futuro del campo. ASM abraza esta responsabilidad, aprovechando la ciencia fundamental y aplicada para impactar positivamente la vida en nuestro planeta y la sostenibilidad del mismo.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
1. Convene experts and members at ASM meetings (https://asm.org/events)  
2. Publish innovative science (https://journals.asm.org/)  
3. Advocate for science (https://asm.org/advocacy)  
4. Build microbiology laboratory capacity (https://asm.org/global-health)
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
1. Convocar a expertos y miembros en las reuniones de ASM (https://asm.org/events)  
2. Publicar ciencia innovadora (https://journals.asm.org/)  
3. Abogar por la ciencia (https://asm.org/advocacy)  
4. Desarrollar la capacidad de los laboratorios de microbiología (https://asm.org/global-health)
NSABoardMembersENG: The ASM Board of Directors, supported by the Committees of the Board, provides strategic direction to our Society. It is responsible for the organizational, business, legal, financial, and governance matters of our organization. The Board of Directors is chaired by the president. Annual elections to the board begin in late summer each year with a call for nominations from ASM membership.

Es responsable de los asuntos organizativos, comerciales, legales, financieros y de gobernanza de nuestra organización. La Junta Directiva es presidida por el presidente. Las elecciones anuales para la junta comienzan a finales del verano de cada año con una convocatoria de nominaciones de los miembros de la ASM.

Title: Coalition for Americas’ Health
NSAObjectives:
MISSSION: To prevent and control chronic diseases, also called non-communicable diseases or NCDs, in the region of the Americas by promoting an environment conducive to health, well-being, social and economic development.
This mission is accomplished by increasing awareness and education of the public, the media, authorities, and other sectors of society about the prevention and control of NCDs, their risk factors, determinants, and relation to infectious diseases such as COVID-19.
NSAWorkFields:
1. Promoting healthy & sustainable food systems to reduce obesity and overweight, which impacts almost all NCDs:  front-of-package warning labels, promoting healthy food in schools, fiscal policies regarding sweetened beverages, regulating advertising/promotion/sponsorship of unhealthy food and drink, elimination of trans fats from the food supply and sodium reduction
2. Promoting reductions in the consumption of tobacco, nicotine, and alcohol, such as accelerating the implementation of the FCTC through warning labels on packaging, fiscal policies, smokefree environments, smoking cessation, and controlling illicit trade of tobacco products. Supporting SAFER to reduce the consumption of alcohol
3. Addressing the prevention & treatment of hypertension & diabetes by improving access to healthcare & medicines and increasing public awareness
4. Prevention & control of NCDs, encouraging government action
5. Strengthen civil society organizations through training, planning, exchange of info
NSABoardMembers:
Board of Directors representing Membership: Oscar Cabrera, Esq, Ernesto Sebrie, MD, PhD, Beatriz Champagne, PhD .  Staff group manages operations.
Members are non-government organizations dedicated to the prevention and control of Non-communicable Diseases or NCDs, risk factors and other related topics.  Members complete an application form that is reviewed by a Membership Committee, which in turn makes a recommendation to the Board of Directors for acceptance or rejection.
Principal members include: IDEC; ACT Saude Publica Brazil; FIC Argentina; FUNDEPS; SANAR; Red PaPaz Colombia; Bolivia Saludable; Costa Rica Saludable; Movimiento Alimentación Saludable; Salud Justa México; El Poder del Consumidor MX; OMIS Uruguay; CUUM Ecuador, PROESA Colombia
NSAOrganizationBodies:
PAHO official status; FCTC observer status; Application ECOSOC; Application CODEX; WHO Civil Society Working Group on NCDs; WHO Youth Council; UNICEF

NCD Alliance; World Obesity Federation WOF; Global Alliance for Tobacco Control GATC; Global Alcohol Policy Alliance  (GAPA); World Heart Federation, Movendi International, Forut; Climate & Health, SLAN, ALSANNA, INWAT; Together Women Rise; Corporate Accountability International; INCAP; COLANSA; Nicotine Addition and Tobacco Treatment Initiative NATTI;

History:
Luna, Cristina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 12/23/2025 11:00:57 AM. Comments: ERP recommends proceeding to renew the status as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. In addition, the FENSA review and risk analysis conducted by LEG has also been carefully considered. While ERP supports moving forward with the renewal based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 12/9/2025 04:41:58 PM. Comments: Dear Fabio: We have reviewed the background of Coalition for America’s Health (CLAS), which is applying to be in official relations with PAHO. LEG recommendation is to proceed with the designation, as long as NMH believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO carries out due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-state actors. Based on the documentation provided by CLAS and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments: 1. For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to CLAS the policies and procedures applicable to a non-governmental organization (NGO). 2. CLAS is a non-profit entity incorporated in the State of Texas, United States of America (entity name: Coalition for Americas’ Health, Inc., EIN 85-3425179, company number 0803787496). It functions as a regional civil-society coalition for NCD prevention and health promotion across the Americas. 3. CLAS seeks to prevent and control non-communicable diseases by promoting environments conducive to health and well-being through evidence-based advocacy, awareness, and policy translation on tobacco, alcohol, nutrition, and physical activity. 4. The main sources of income are contributions and grants. The major donors are Bloomberg Philanthropies and Resolve to Save Lives, both entities in official relations with WHO. 5. The Executive Director of CLAS is Beatriz M. Champagne; and the Board of Directors includes Oscar Cabrera and Ernesto Sebrie. Their names were screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List, and no matches were found. 6. CLAS declared no links with the tobacco and arms industries on September 24, 2025; and no links were uncovered by LEG either. The risk analysis consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of participating in this collaboration, while protecting and preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. In our searches, we found no evidence of financial, ethical, or reputational concerns. CLAS’ profile and activities are consistent with PAHO’s Strategic Plan and its collaboration history with PAHO is positive. Best regards,
Beatriz Champagne responded with additional information as NSA Originator on 12/6/2025 03:00:29 PM. Comments: Donors that provided more than 10% of our revenues, there are only two: 1. Bloomberg Philanthropies, Food Policy Program 300,000 usd 2. Resolve to Save Lives 69,957 usd Please see attached the FINAL Audit report for 2024 and the form 990 sent to the IRS
Da Silva Gomes, Fabio (WDC) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 12/4/2025 01:00:10 PM. Comments: I am not able to edit the text in the system. So I am pasting the original and amending it here to address the comments. As for the documentation requested. I cannot upload it to the system, so I will forward it by email. Activity 1 Three of 4 countries in Central America that CLAS has jointly supported with PAHO now have legislative proposals on Front of Package Warning Labels tabled. CLAS co-organized with PAHO in-person workshops in Costa Rica (2023), Guatemala (2024) and Panama (2025). In addition to workshops, PAHO and CLAS have coordinated capacity building sessions with legislators and advisors of the legislators leading the bills in those countries to prepare them for debates in the parliament. Those activities included discussion about legislative strategies to advance bills, discussion of technical, legal, political and economic arguments and counter-arguments to prepare legislators to defend the bills in line with PAHO's recommendations and best practices. These activities took place either in person or virtually. 2. Three of 3 countries where CLAS jointly worked with PAHO to establish elimination policies for industrially-produced trans fatty acids, now have legislative proposals tabled: Bolivia, Ecuador, and Panama. The work involved engagement of civil society in advocating for bills that are aligned with PAHO's recommendations and best practices for the elimination of industrially produced trans fatty acids. That also included follow up of legislative process so that amendments to the bill would not weaken those away from the recommendations and best practices. 3. Initiated efforts to advocate for mandatory sodium reduction targets in Costa Rica and Panama guided by and in collaboration with PAHO. This included social mobilization to gather support for such measures in those countries with campaigns in social media, and events involving civil society, academia, and national health authorities, and PAHO. 4. With Colansa, provided financial & technical support to 4 subgrants on agroecology, school environments, the regulation of marketing of UPF, and coalition building. The implementation of such support is done collaboratively with PAHO to support its efforts in advancing the adoption of PAHO's recommendations and best practices. 5. Organized ALASA, a regional alliance to reduce alcohol consumption in LA supporting PAHO's work on this matter. With funding from a Norwegian NGO. Now helping Uruguay and Costa Rica to organize their own national alliances in collaboration with PAHO. Activity 2 CLAS and Colansa developed and launched SaludableSaberlo and collaborated with PAHO on expanding and integrating it with PAHO’s communication platforms, to improve the reach of scientifically based information to the public, media, and decision makers. Scientific evidence based material for media and public engagement has been jointly developed by CLAS and PAHO, and CLAS, COLANSA, and PAHO have co-designed and disseminated communication campaigns through their channels. SaludableSaberlos is better integrating national and regional communications and traditional and social media, creating synergies and empowering local advocates in the region of the Americas. With the collaboration of PAHO SaludableSaberlo held webinars on journalism, addressing conflict of interest, digital marketing and other topics for media. Best read articles are compiled and sent via email in a newsletter monthly. On Sept 2025 SaludableSaberlo with the collaboration of PAHO held a journalist workshop with 40+ participants from 10 different countries. Reach has increased greatly since its launch in Jan 2024.

Luna, Cristina (Consultant WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as ERP Reviewer on 11/11/2025 12:42:41 PM. Comments: Dear Fabio, Thank you for clearing the CLAS renewal form. After reviewing it in ERP, we identified specific points that need clarification. Please review the observations below and share any additional details through the system. If needed, we can consult with the NSA for further clarification. Reported Activities (2023–2025) The reported activities are aligned with the approved plan; however, we noted that the collaborative approach with PAHO is not clearly described. • In Activity 1, PAHO’s support is mentioned in the mentoring initiative in Panama, but it would be helpful to clarify PAHO’s role and contributions in this and other capacity-building efforts. • In Activity 2, the report notes that SaludableSaberlo was launched, but please elaborate on PAHO’s involvement (e.g., co-design, technical input, dissemination). Could you please expand on these points to ensure the report reflects the joint nature of the work? Additionally, we adjusted the description of Activity 2 in the system to align with the wording of the approved plan, while keeping the essence of what was reported. Proposed Plan (2026–2028) To reflect the collaborative nature of the work, could you please: • For Activity 1, provide more details on planned collaborative activities, including specific actions and expected contributions from PAHO and CLAS. For example: • Co-develop and implement joint capacity-building initiatives (e.g., workshops, webinars). • Collaborate on the development of advocacy tools and stakeholder mapping. • Coordinate joint efforts to monitor industry practices and regulatory changes. • For Activity 2, we suggest using the wording approved in the previous plan or adjusting it to focus on collaborative actions. For example: • Co-design and disseminate communication campaigns through PAHO and CLAS channels. • Jointly develop narratives based on scientific evidence for media and public engagement. • Collaborate on expanding SaludableSaberlo.org and integrating it with PAHO’s communication platforms. This additional information will help ensure the plan reflects concrete actions and a clear collaborative approach. Thank you for your support.

CollabWPActHealthAgenda_txt: Goal 9: Noncommunicable Diseases
NSAObjetivesENG: MISSION: To prevent and control chronic diseases, also known as non-communicable diseases or NCDs, in the Americas by promoting an environment conducive to health, well-being, and social and economic development. This mission is achieved by raising awareness and educating the public, the media, authorities, and other sectors of society about the prevention and control of NCDs, their risk factors, determinants, and their relationship to infectious diseases such as COVID-19.
NSAObjetivesSPA: MISIÓN: Prevenir y controlar las enfermedades crónicas, también llamadas enfermedades no transmisibles o ENT, en la región de las Américas promoviendo un entorno propicio para la salud, el bienestar, el desarrollo social y económico. Esta misión se logra aumentando la concienciación y la educación del público, los medios, las autoridades y otros sectores de la sociedad sobre la prevención y el control de las ENT, sus factores de riesgo, determinantes y su relación con enfermedades infecciosas como la COVID-19.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
1. Promoting healthy and sustainable food systems to reduce obesity and overweight, which affect nearly all non-communicable diseases (NCDs): implementing front-of-package warning labels, promoting healthy food in schools, enacting fiscal policies regarding sugary beverages, regulating the advertising, promotion, and sponsorship of unhealthy food and drinks, eliminating trans fats from the food supply, and reducing sodium intake.
2. Promoting reductions in the consumption of tobacco, nicotine, and alcohol, such as accelerating the implementation of the Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC) through warning labels on packaging, fiscal policies, creating smoke-free environments, supporting smoking cessation, and controlling the illicit trade of tobacco products. Supporting the SAFER initiative to reduce alcohol consumption.
3. Addressing the prevention and treatment of hypertension and diabetes by improving access to healthcare and medicines and increasing public awareness.
4. Prevention and control of non-communicable diseases (NCDs), encouraging government action.
5. Strengthening civil society organizations through training, planning, and the exchange of information.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
1. Promover sistemas alimentarios saludables y sostenibles para reducir la obesidad y el sobrepeso, que impactan casi todas las enfermedades no transmisibles (ENT): etiquetas de advertencia en el frente del paquete, promoción de alimentos saludables en las escuelas, políticas fiscales respecto a las bebidas azucaradas, regulación de la publicidad/promoción/patrocinio de alimentos y bebidas no saludables, eliminación de grasas trans del suministro alimentario y reducción de sodio.
2. Promover reducciones en el consumo de tabaco, nicotina y alcohol, como acelerar la implementación del Tratado Marco de la OMS para el Control del Tabaco (FCTC) a través de etiquetas de advertencia en el empaque, políticas fiscales, entornos libres de humo, cesación del tabaquismo y control del comercio ilícito de productos de tabaco. Apoyar SAFER para reducir el consumo de alcohol.
3. Abordar la prevención y el tratamiento de la hipertensión y la diabetes mejorando el acceso a la atención médica y a los medicamentos, y aumentando la concienciación pública.
4. Prevención y control de las enfermedades no transmisibles, fomentando la acción gubernamental.
5. Fortalecer las organizaciones de la sociedad civil a través de capacitación, planificación e intercambio de información.
NSABoardMembersENG: Board of Directors representing Membership: Oscar Cabrera, Esq, Ernesto Sebrie, MD, PhD, Beatriz Champagne, PhD. The staff group manages operations. Members are non-governmental organizations dedicated to the prevention and control of Non-communicable Diseases (NCDs), risk factors, and other related topics. Members complete an application form that is reviewed by a Membership Committee, which then makes a recommendation to the Board of Directors for acceptance or rejection. Principal members include: IDEC; ACT Saude Publica Brazil; FIC Argentina; FUNDEPS; SANAR; Red PaPaz Colombia; Bolivia Saludable; Costa Rica Saludable; Movimiento Alimentación Saludable; Salud Justa México; El Poder del Consumidor MX; OMIS Uruguay; CUUM Ecuador; PROESA Colombia.
NSABoardMembersSPA:
### Traducción

Junta Directiva que representa a los Miembros: Oscar Cabrera, Esq, Ernesto Sebrie, MD, PhD, Beatriz Champagne, PhD. El grupo de personal gestiona las operaciones. Los miembros son organizaciones no gubernamentales dedicadas a la prevención y control de Enfermedades No Transmisibles (ENT), factores de riesgo y otros temas relacionados. Los miembros completan un formulario de solicitud que es revisado por un Comité de Membresía, que a su vez hace una recomendación a la Junta Directiva para su aceptación o rechazo. 

Los miembros principales incluyen: IDEC; ACT Salud Pública Brasil; FIC Argentina; FUNDEPS; SANAR; Red PaPaz Colombia; Bolivia Saludable; Costa Rica Saludable; Movimiento Alimentación Saludable; Salud Justa México; El Poder del Consumidor MX; OMIS Uruguay; CUUM Ecuador, PROESA Colombia.

TitleFRE: Coalition pour la santé des Amériques
NSAObjectivesFRE: MISSION : Prévenir et contrôler les maladies chroniques, également appelées maladies non transmissibles ou MNT, dans la région des Amériques en promouvant un environnement propice à la santé, au bien-être, au développement social et économique. Cette mission est accomplie en augmentant la sensibilisation et l'éducation du public, des médias, des autorités et d'autres secteurs de la société sur la prévention et le contrôle des MNT, leurs facteurs de risque, leurs déterminants et leur relation avec les maladies infectieuses telles que la COVID-19.
NSAObjectivesPOR: MISSÃO: Prevenir e controlar doenças crônicas, também chamadas de doenças não transmissíveis ou DNTs, na região das Américas, promovendo um ambiente propício à saúde, bem-estar, desenvolvimento social e econômico. Essa missão é realizada por meio do aumento da conscientização e educação do público, da mídia, das autoridades e de outros setores da sociedade sobre a prevenção e controle das DNTs, seus fatores de risco, determinantes e relação com doenças infecciosas como a COVID-19.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
1. Promouvoir des systèmes alimentaires sains et durables pour réduire l'obésité et le surpoids, qui impactent presque toutes les MNT : étiquettes d'avertissement sur le devant des emballages, promotion des aliments sains dans les écoles, politiques fiscales concernant les boissons sucrées, réglementation de la publicité/promotion/sponsoring des aliments et boissons malsains, élimination des acides gras trans de l'approvisionnement alimentaire et réduction du sodium.
2. Promouvoir la réduction de la consommation de tabac, de nicotine et d'alcool, comme l'accélération de la mise en œuvre de la FCTC par le biais d'étiquettes d'avertissement sur les emballages, de politiques fiscales, d'environnements sans fumée, d'aide à l'arrêt du tabac et de contrôle du commerce illicite des produits du tabac. Soutenir SAFER pour réduire la consommation d'alcool.
3. Aborder la prévention et le traitement de l'hypertension et du diabète en améliorant l'accès aux soins de santé et aux médicaments et en augmentant la sensibilisation du public.
4. Prévention et contrôle des MNT, en encourageant l'action gouvernementale.
5. Renforcer les organisations de la société civile par la formation, la planification et l'échange d'informations.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
1. Promover sistemas alimentares saudáveis e sustentáveis para reduzir a obesidade e o sobrepeso, que impactam quase todas as DCNTs: rótulos de advertência na parte frontal da embalagem, promoção de alimentos saudáveis nas escolas, políticas fiscais em relação a bebidas adoçadas, regulamentação da publicidade/promoção/patrocínio de alimentos e bebidas não saudáveis, eliminação de gorduras trans do suprimento alimentar e redução de sódio.
2. Promover a redução do consumo de tabaco, nicotina e álcool, como acelerar a implementação da FCTC por meio de rótulos de advertência nas embalagens, políticas fiscais, ambientes livres de fumaça, cessação do tabagismo e controle do comércio ilícito de produtos de tabaco. Apoiar o SAFER para reduzir o consumo de álcool.
3. Abordar a prevenção e o tratamento da hipertensão e diabetes, melhorando o acesso a cuidados de saúde e medicamentos e aumentando a conscientização pública.
4. Prevenção e controle das DCNTs, incentivando a ação do governo.
5. Fortalecer as organizações da sociedade civil por meio de treinamento, planejamento e intercâmbio de informações.
NSABoardMembersFRE:
Conseil d'administration représentant les membres : Oscar Cabrera, Esq, Ernesto Sebrie, MD, PhD, Beatriz Champagne, PhD. Le groupe de personnel gère les opérations. Les membres sont des organisations non gouvernementales dédiées à la prévention et au contrôle des maladies non transmissibles (MNT), des facteurs de risque et d'autres sujets connexes. Les membres remplissent un formulaire de candidature qui est examiné par un comité d'adhésion, qui à son tour fait une recommandation au conseil d'administration pour acceptation ou rejet. 

Les membres principaux incluent : IDEC ; ACT Saude Publica Brésil ; FIC Argentine ; FUNDEPS ; SANAR ; Red PaPaz Colombie ; Bolivia Saludable ; Costa Rica Saludable ; Movimiento Alimentación Saludable ; Salud Justa Mexique ; El Poder del Consumidor MX ; OMIS Uruguay ; CUUM Équateur, PROESA Colombie.

CollabWPActHealthAgenda_txt: Goal 10: Communicable Diseases
NSAObjetivesENG: MISSION: To prevent and control chronic diseases, also known as non-communicable diseases or NCDs, in the Americas by promoting an environment conducive to health, well-being, and social and economic development. This mission is achieved by raising awareness and educating the public, the media, authorities, and other sectors of society about the prevention and control of NCDs, their risk factors, determinants, and their relationship to infectious diseases such as COVID-19.
NSAObjetivesSPA: MISIÓN: Prevenir y controlar las enfermedades crónicas, también llamadas enfermedades no transmisibles o ENT, en la región de las Américas promoviendo un entorno propicio para la salud, el bienestar, el desarrollo social y económico. Esta misión se logra aumentando la concienciación y la educación del público, los medios, las autoridades y otros sectores de la sociedad sobre la prevención y el control de las ENT, sus factores de riesgo, determinantes y su relación con enfermedades infecciosas como la COVID-19.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
1. Promoting healthy and sustainable food systems to reduce obesity and overweight, which affect nearly all non-communicable diseases (NCDs): implementing front-of-package warning labels, promoting healthy food in schools, enacting fiscal policies regarding sugary beverages, regulating the advertising, promotion, and sponsorship of unhealthy food and drinks, eliminating trans fats from the food supply, and reducing sodium intake.
2. Promoting reductions in the consumption of tobacco, nicotine, and alcohol, such as accelerating the implementation of the Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC) through warning labels on packaging, fiscal policies, creating smoke-free environments, supporting smoking cessation, and controlling the illicit trade of tobacco products. Supporting the SAFER initiative to reduce alcohol consumption.
3. Addressing the prevention and treatment of hypertension and diabetes by improving access to healthcare and medicines and increasing public awareness.
4. Prevention and control of non-communicable diseases (NCDs), encouraging government action.
5. Strengthening civil society organizations through training, planning, and the exchange of information.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
1. Promover sistemas alimentarios saludables y sostenibles para reducir la obesidad y el sobrepeso, que impactan casi todas las enfermedades no transmisibles (ENT): etiquetas de advertencia en el frente del paquete, promoción de alimentos saludables en las escuelas, políticas fiscales respecto a las bebidas azucaradas, regulación de la publicidad/promoción/patrocinio de alimentos y bebidas no saludables, eliminación de grasas trans del suministro alimentario y reducción de sodio.
2. Promover reducciones en el consumo de tabaco, nicotina y alcohol, como acelerar la implementación del Tratado Marco de la OMS para el Control del Tabaco (FCTC) a través de etiquetas de advertencia en el empaque, políticas fiscales, entornos libres de humo, cesación del tabaquismo y control del comercio ilícito de productos de tabaco. Apoyar SAFER para reducir el consumo de alcohol.
3. Abordar la prevención y el tratamiento de la hipertensión y la diabetes mejorando el acceso a la atención médica y a los medicamentos, y aumentando la concienciación pública.
4. Prevención y control de las enfermedades no transmisibles, fomentando la acción gubernamental.
5. Fortalecer las organizaciones de la sociedad civil a través de capacitación, planificación e intercambio de información.

Members are non-governmental organizations dedicated to the prevention and control of Non-communicable Diseases (NCDs), risk factors, and other related topics. Title: United States Pharmacopeial Convention (USP)
NSAObjectives:
The United States Pharmacopeial Convention ﴾USP﴿ is a scientific nonprofit organization that sets standards for the
identity, strength, quality, and purity of medicines, food ingredients, and dietary supplements manufactured, distributed and consumed worldwide. USP’s drug standards are enforceable in the United States by the Food and Drug
Administration ﴾FDA﴿, and these standards are used in more than 140 countries. USP’s mission is to improve global health through public standards and related programs that help ensure the quality, safety, and benefit of medicines and foods. 

The mission of the United States Pharmacopeia ﴾USP﴿ is to improve global health through public standards and related
programs that help ensure the quality, safety, and benefit of medicines and foods. Areas of work include quality standards for chemical medicines, biologics, compounding standards, dietary supplements and herbal medicines, excipients, and food safety and integrity. Training and capacity programs support global health initiatives, standards harmonization, healthcare quality and safety, research and innovation, and reference standards.
NSAWorkFields:
• Standards‐setting and related activities
• Documentary standards for medicines, dietary supplements, and food ingredients
• Reference standards for use in USP compendial tests and methods
• Standards promoting patient safety and healthcare quality
• Meetings, including symposia, workshops, and other events that engage global stakeholders on issues critical to USP’s
mission
• Allied compendial programs supporting USP’s standards‐setting activities
• Verification services for dietary supplements, dietary ingredients, and pharmaceutical ingredients
• Pharmacopeial Education Global Health Programs that are aimed to help under‐resourced countries build their capacity to effectively regulate the quality of their medicines
• Official Medicines Control Laboratory ﴾OMCL﴿ Networks
NSABoardMembers:
The USP Convention consists of over 501 member organizations representing academic institutions; health practitioner and scientific associations; consumer organizations; manufacturer and trade associations; government bodies and associations; and nongovernmental standards‐setting and conformity assessment bodies.

Members vote on resolutions that guide USP's policies and initiatives, and elect the USP Convention officers, trustees, and the Council of Experts. Members must be invited and approved by the Council of the USP Convention and the Board of Trustees.
NSAOrganizationBodies: World Health Organization ﴾WHO﴿; United Nations Economic and Social Council ﴾ECOSOC﴿
