Luna, Cristina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 12/23/2025 11:40:15 AM. Comments: ERP recommends proceeding to renew the status as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. In addition, the FENSA review and risk analysis conducted by LEG has also been carefully considered. While ERP supports moving forward with the renewal based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Sagastuy Artaloitia, Begoña (WDC) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 12/2/2025 06:39:44 PM. Comments: We acknowledge receipt and take note of ERP's comments.
Luna, Cristina (WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as ERP Reviewer on 11/20/2025 12:11:48 PM. Comments: Dear Begoña, We are sending you this message before completing the ERP review to confirm that you are receiving it as the new focal point for USP. Here are our comments following the review: Based on the ERP review, the activities reported by USP were implemented as planned under the approved collaboration framework for the 2023–2025 period. The report adequately reflects the expected outcomes, maintaining alignment with the agreed lines of work and demonstrating a sustained and effective partnership between USP and PAHO in strengthening regulatory capacities and supporting regional harmonization efforts. Regarding the proposed plan, the collaboration period has been updated for clarity to January 2026 – December 2028 (originally indicated as 2025–2027). The plan maintains consistency with the previously approved lines of collaboration and builds on prior achievements. It is well-defined, aligned with PAHO’s institutional objectives, and emphasizes collaborative activities. No substantive observations are identified regarding the proposed content. Please let us know if you have any questions or need further clarification.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 11/19/2025 02:50:26 PM. Comments: We have reviewed the documents accompanying the request for renewal of the United States Pharmacopeial Convention (USP) of its status as a non-state actor in official relations with PAHO. Based on the information provided LEG recommendation is to proceed with the renewal of this membership. LEG conducted the due diligence and risk assessment and based on that we provide the following comments: During the review of documents and investigation of public records, LEG found no criminal, reputational or corruption allegations, or litigation involving USP. LEG noted a perception of risk related to its technical engagement with the food and beverage industry due to the nature of its activity to develop standards for quality assurance in the production of medicines for. However, none of them were identified as a conflict of interest or a direct commercial interest over USP activities. LEG highlights that USP maintains official relations with WHO and PAHO since 1997, collaborating on medicine-quality and regulatory harmonization initiatives. LEG notes the acquisition of Pharmatech Associates (a consultancy serving pharmaceutical manufacturers) and the majority purchase of Stratix Labs (microbiological products). As per the information provided, these acquisitions will support USPC’s mission commitments to expand the global availability of and access to quality and assured medicines, help solve pervasive and emerging quality challenges and strengthen USPC’s standard’s portfolio. LEG understands that both are positioned as mission-supporting and non-marketing ventures. LEG will continue to review USP acquisitions moving forward to verify that such activities do not change the FENSA categorization of USP from NGO to “private sector.” As LEG noted in previous assessments, USP as a nongovernmental organization plays a unique role in global public health by working closely with regulators and providing standards that are legally required in several countries. Most Member States in the Region use USP standards in some form, which supports harmonization of pharmaceutical quality across the Americas. USP’s collaboration with PAHO advances the Organization’s Strategic Plan by promoting harmonized standards, strengthening quality assurance and control systems, and supporting capacity-building through technical cooperation, trainings, and expert participation. Although there is a real risk that official relations could be perceived as endorsing USP and its standards, this risk is mitigated by its long-standing official relations with WHO and PAHO since 1997. Overall, the benefits of continued engagement with USP, particularly in fostering harmonization and improving medicine quality, outweigh the potential risks.
Pombo, Maria Luz (WDC) submitted request as PAHO Focal Point for review on 9/27/2025 01:21:17 PM. Comments: The Department of Innovation, Access to Medicines and Health Technologies confirms that collaboration with USP is essential to sustaining technical cooperation in key areas, including the strengthening of regulatory capacities, the development of harmonized regulatory systems, and the enhancement of quality assurance mechanisms to prevent, detect, and mitigate the risks of substandard and falsified medical products. The proposed work plan to renew USP’s status as a non-state actor in official relations with PAHO was jointly agreed upon with the IMT Department, focusing on critical areas where both institutions can collaborate to advance regional technical cooperation. Therefore, we recommend maintaining the existing collaboration between PAHO and USP. IMT suggest that changes are made in PAHO focal point to monitor the progress of this collaboration, by including Begoña Sagastuy as the PAHO counterpart.

TitleENGSPA: Convención de la Farmacopea de Estados Unidos
NSAObjetivesENG:
The United States Pharmacopeial Convention (USP) is a scientific nonprofit organization that establishes standards for the identity, strength, quality, and purity of medicines, food ingredients, and dietary supplements that are manufactured, distributed, and consumed worldwide. USP's drug standards are enforceable in the United States by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA), and these standards are utilized in over 140 countries. USP's mission is to enhance global health through public standards and related programs that help ensure the quality, safety, and efficacy of medicines and foods.

The mission of the United States Pharmacopeia (USP) is to improve global health through public standards and related programs that help ensure the quality, safety, and efficacy of medicines and foods. Areas of focus include quality standards for chemical medicines, biologics, compounding standards, dietary supplements and herbal medicines, excipients, and food safety and integrity. Training and capacity-building programs support global health initiatives, standards harmonization, healthcare quality and safety, research and innovation, and reference standards.
NSAObjetivesSPA:
La Convención Farmacopea de los Estados Unidos (USP) es una organización científica sin fines de lucro que establece estándares para la identidad, potencia, calidad y pureza de los medicamentos, ingredientes alimentarios y suplementos dietéticos fabricados, distribuidos y consumidos en todo el mundo. Los estándares de medicamentos de la USP son exigibles en los Estados Unidos por la Administración de Alimentos y Medicamentos (FDA), y estos estándares se utilizan en más de 140 países. La misión de la USP es mejorar la salud global a través de estándares públicos y programas relacionados que ayudan a garantizar la calidad, seguridad y beneficios de los medicamentos y alimentos.

La misión de la Farmacopea de los Estados Unidos (USP) es mejorar la salud global a través de estándares públicos y programas relacionados que ayudan a garantizar la calidad, seguridad y beneficios de los medicamentos y alimentos. Las áreas de trabajo incluyen estándares de calidad para medicamentos químicos, biológicos, estándares de preparación, suplementos dietéticos y medicamentos herbales, excipientes, y seguridad e integridad alimentaria. Los programas de capacitación y capacidad apoyan iniciativas de salud global, armonización de estándares, calidad y seguridad en la atención médica, investigación e innovación, y estándares de referencia.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
- Standards-setting and related activities
- Documentary standards for medicines, dietary supplements, and food ingredients
- Reference standards for use in USP compendial tests and methods
- Standards promoting patient safety and healthcare quality
- Meetings, including symposia, workshops, and other events that engage global stakeholders on issues critical to USP’s mission
- Allied compendial programs supporting USP’s standards-setting activities
- Verification services for dietary supplements, dietary ingredients, and pharmaceutical ingredients
- Pharmacopeial Education Global Health Programs aimed at helping under-resourced countries build their capacity to effectively regulate the quality of their medicines
- Official Medicines Control Laboratory (OMCL) Networks
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
• Actividades de establecimiento de normas y relacionadas  
• Normas documentales para medicamentos, suplementos dietéticos e ingredientes alimentarios  
• Normas de referencia para su uso en pruebas y métodos de la Farmacopea de los Estados Unidos (USP)  
• Normas que promueven la seguridad del paciente y la calidad de la atención médica  
• Reuniones, incluidos simposios, talleres y otros eventos que involucran a partes interesadas globales en temas críticos para la misión de la USP  
• Programas compendiales aliados que apoyan las actividades de establecimiento de normas de la USP  
• Servicios de verificación para suplementos dietéticos, ingredientes dietéticos e ingredientes farmacéuticos  
• Programas de Educación Farmacopea y Salud Global que tienen como objetivo ayudar a los países con pocos recursos a desarrollar su capacidad para regular eficazmente la calidad de sus medicamentos  
• Redes de Laboratorios Oficiales de Control de Medicamentos (OMCL)
NSABoardMembersENG:
The USP Convention consists of over 501 member organizations representing academic institutions; health practitioner and scientific associations; consumer organizations; manufacturer and trade associations; government bodies and associations; and nongovernmental standards-setting and conformity assessment bodies.

Members vote on resolutions that guide USP's policies and initiatives, and elect the USP Convention officers, trustees, and the Council of Experts. Members must be invited and approved by the Council of the USP Convention and the Board of Trustees.
NSABoardMembersSPA:
La Convención de USP está compuesta por más de 501 organizaciones miembros que representan instituciones académicas; asociaciones de profesionales de la salud y científicas; organizaciones de consumidores; asociaciones de fabricantes y comercio; organismos gubernamentales y asociaciones; y organismos no gubernamentales de establecimiento de normas y evaluación de la conformidad.

Los miembros votan sobre resoluciones que guían las políticas e iniciativas de USP, y eligen a los oficiales de la Convención de USP, a los fideicomisarios y al Consejo de Expertos. Los miembros deben ser invitados y aprobados por el Consejo de la Convención de USP y la Junta de Fideicomisarios.
NSAOrganizationBodiesENG: World Health Organization (WHO); United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC)
NSAOrganizationBodiesSPA: Organización Mundial de la Salud (OMS); Consejo Económico y Social de las Naciones Unidas (ECOSOC)

NSAObjectivesFRE:
La Convention pharmacopeique des États-Unis (USP) est une organisation scientifique à but non lucratif qui établit des normes pour l'identité, la force, la qualité et la pureté des médicaments, des ingrédients alimentaires et des suppléments diététiques fabriqués, distribués et consommés dans le monde entier. Les normes pharmaceutiques de l'USP sont applicables aux États-Unis par la Food and Drug Administration (FDA), et ces normes sont utilisées dans plus de 140 pays. La mission de l'USP est d'améliorer la santé mondiale grâce à des normes publiques et à des programmes connexes qui aident à garantir la qualité, la sécurité et l'efficacité des médicaments et des aliments.

La mission de la Pharmacopeia des États-Unis (USP) est d'améliorer la santé mondiale grâce à des normes publiques et à des programmes connexes qui aident à garantir la qualité, la sécurité et l'efficacité des médicaments et des aliments. Les domaines de travail incluent les normes de qualité pour les médicaments chimiques, les produits biologiques, les normes de préparation, les suppléments diététiques et les médicaments à base de plantes, les excipients, ainsi que la sécurité et l'intégrité alimentaires. Des programmes de formation et de renforcement des capacités soutiennent les initiatives de santé mondiale, l'harmonisation des normes, la qualité et la sécurité des soins de santé, la recherche et l'innovation, ainsi que les normes de référence.
NSAObjectivesPOR:
A Convenção Farmacopeica dos Estados Unidos (USP) é uma organização científica sem fins lucrativos que estabelece padrões para a identidade, força, qualidade e pureza de medicamentos, ingredientes alimentares e suplementos dietéticos fabricados, distribuídos e consumidos em todo o mundo. Os padrões de medicamentos da USP são aplicáveis nos Estados Unidos pela Administração de Alimentos e Medicamentos (FDA), e esses padrões são utilizados em mais de 140 países. A missão da USP é melhorar a saúde global por meio de padrões públicos e programas relacionados que ajudam a garantir a qualidade, segurança e benefícios de medicamentos e alimentos.

A missão da Farmacopeia dos Estados Unidos (USP) é melhorar a saúde global por meio de padrões públicos e programas relacionados que ajudam a garantir a qualidade, segurança e benefícios de medicamentos e alimentos. As áreas de trabalho incluem padrões de qualidade para medicamentos químicos, biológicos, padrões de manipulação, suplementos dietéticos e medicamentos à base de plantas, excipientes e segurança e integridade alimentar. Programas de treinamento e capacitação apoiam iniciativas de saúde global, harmonização de padrões, qualidade e segurança na saúde, pesquisa e inovação, e padrões de referência.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
• Activités d'élaboration de normes et activités connexes  
• Normes documentaires pour les médicaments, les compléments alimentaires et les ingrédients alimentaires  
• Normes de référence à utiliser dans les tests et méthodes de l'USP  
• Normes favorisant la sécurité des patients et la qualité des soins de santé  
• Réunions, y compris des symposiums, des ateliers et d'autres événements engageant les parties prenantes mondiales sur des questions critiques pour la mission de l'USP  
• Programmes compendiaux alliés soutenant les activités d'élaboration de normes de l'USP  
• Services de vérification pour les compléments alimentaires, les ingrédients diététiques et les ingrédients pharmaceutiques  
• Programmes d'éducation pharmacopeique pour la santé mondiale visant à aider les pays sous-dotés à renforcer leur capacité à réglementer efficacement la qualité de leurs médicaments  
• Réseaux de laboratoires de contrôle des médicaments officiels (OMCL)
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
• Atividades de definição de padrões e relacionadas  
• Padrões documentais para medicamentos, suplementos dietéticos e ingredientes alimentares  
• Padrões de referência para uso em testes e métodos compendiais da USP  
• Padrões que promovem a segurança do paciente e a qualidade da assistência à saúde  
• Reuniões, incluindo simpósios, oficinas e outros eventos que envolvem partes interessadas globais em questões críticas para a missão da USP  
• Programas compendiais aliados que apoiam as atividades de definição de padrões da USP  
• Serviços de verificação para suplementos dietéticos, ingredientes dietéticos e ingredientes farmacêuticos  
• Programas de Educação Farmacopeial em Saúde Global que visam ajudar países com poucos recursos a desenvolver sua capacidade de regular efetivamente a qualidade de seus medicamentos  
• Redes de Laboratórios Oficiais de Controle de Medicamentos (OMCL)
NSABoardMembersFRE:
La Convention USP se compose de plus de 501 organisations membres représentant des institutions académiques ; des associations de praticiens de la santé et scientifiques ; des organisations de consommateurs ; des associations de fabricants et de commerce ; des organismes gouvernementaux et des associations ; ainsi que des organismes non gouvernementaux d'élaboration de normes et d'évaluation de la conformité.

Les membres votent sur des résolutions qui guident les politiques et initiatives de l'USP, et élisent les dirigeants, les administrateurs et le Conseil des experts de la Convention USP. Les membres doivent être invités et approuvés par le Conseil de la Convention USP et le Conseil d'administration.
NSABoardMembersPOR:
A Convenção USP é composta por mais de 501 organizações membros que representam instituições acadêmicas; associações de profissionais de saúde e científicas; organizações de consumidores; associações de fabricantes e comércio; órgãos governamentais e associações; e entidades não governamentais de definição de normas e avaliação de conformidade.

Os membros votam em resoluções que orientam as políticas e iniciativas da USP e elegem os oficiais da Convenção USP, os curadores e o Conselho de Especialistas. Os membros devem ser convidados e aprovados pelo Conselho da Convenção USP e pelo Conselho de Curadores.
NSAOrganizationBodiesFRE: Organisation mondiale de la santé (OMS) ; Conseil économique et social des Nations Unies (ECOSOC)
NSAOrganizationBodiesPOR: Organização Mundial da Saúde (OMS); Conselho Econômico e Social das Nações Unidas (ECOSOC)

Title: International Diabetes Federation (IDF)
NSAObjectives:
IDF is the global voice of the diabetes community. We are a non-profit umbrella organisation of 251 national diabetes associations in 158 countries and territories, working together to improve and empower the lives of the estimated 590 million people living with diabetes and prevent diabetes in those at risk.

IDF is an international non-profit association established in accordance with Title III of the Belgian law of 27 June 1921, now replaced by the Belgian Code on Companies and Associations. 

Our vision is to guarantee access to affordable, quality diabetes care and education worldwide.

Our mission is to improve the lives of people living with diabetes and prevent diabetes in those at risk.
NSAWorkFields:
IDF advocates for increased diabetes prevention, education and the best possible care for anyone who lives with diabetes. 

In close collaboration with its member associations, IDF works to increase access to and improve the quality of care currently available. It does this through advocacy, education for people with diabetes to help them manage their condition, public awareness and health improvement campaigns, and the promotion of the free exchange of diabetes knowledge.
NSABoardMembers:
The Board of Directors is the elected management body of the International Diabetes Federation (IDF). More information on its members affiliations can be found at ww.idf.org.

Professor Peter Schwarz, President.

Dr Niti Pall, President-Elect. 

Ms Erum Ghafoor, Vice President. 

Dr Hermelinda Pedrosa, Vice President. 

Dr Jackie Maalouf, Vice President. 

Prof Jacko Abodo, Vice President. 

Ms Jane Giles, Vice President. 

Mr Kyle Jacques Rose, Vice President. 

Ms Elizabeth Denyoh, Chair of the IDF Africa Region.

Prof Tadej Battelino, Chair of the IDF Europe Region. 

Dr Mohamed Eltom, Chair of the IDF Middle-East and North Africa Region. 

Ms Angelika Namdar, Chair of the IDF North America and Caribbean.

Dr Pablo Arias, Chair of the IDF South and Central America Region. 

Dr Banshi Saboo, Chair of the IDF South-East Asia.

Prof Alicia Jenkins, Chair of the IDF Western Pacific Region.
NSAOrganizationBodies: IDF is in official relations with the World Health Organization and a founding member of the NCD Alliance. It is part of the Global Coalition for Circulatory Health and collaborates frequently with the global leading organisations in other NCDs.
NSAFocalpointRole: Senior Manager, Advocacy and Community Engagement

Comments: ERP recommends proceeding to renew the status as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. In addition, the FENSA review and risk analysis conducted by LEG has also been carefully considered. While ERP supports moving forward with the renewal based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 12/4/2025 02:08:59 PM. Comments: Dear Carmen: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed the background of the International Diabetes Federation (IDF), which is applying to be in official relations with PAHO. LEG recommendation is to proceed with the designation, as long as ERP believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO carries out due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-state actors. Based on the documentation provided by IDF and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments: 1. For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to IDF the policies and procedures applicable to a non-governmental organization (NGO). 2. Legal status: International non-profit association (AISBL) incorporated in Belgium (Registered No. BE 0433.674.528); headquarters at Avenue Herrmann-Debroux 54, B-1160 Brussels. IDF is a federation of national diabetes associations operating in over 150 countries. 3. Mission/objectives: To improve the lives of people living with diabetes and prevent diabetes in those at risk; to advocate for access to quality, affordable diabetes care worldwide and support Member Associations through education and policy leadership. To improve air quality and mitigate climate change in Latin America through technical cooperation, research, and capacity building in urban air-quality management and sustainable transport. 4. Main income source: Project funding and membership fees. The only contributor that represents more than 10% of the revenue is Sanofi. 5. Governance: (2025–27) headed by Prof. Peter Schwarz (President) and Dr. Niti Pall (President-Elect), with Vice-Presidents Erum Ghafoor, Hermelinda Pedrosa, Jackie Maalouf, Jacko Abodo, Jane Giles, and Kyle Jacques Rose; Regional Chairs include Elizabeth Denyoh (Africa) and Tadej Battelino (Europe). All board members and officers were screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List and none appears there. 6. IDF declared no links with tobacco and arms industries on September 10, 2025; and no links were uncovered by LEG either. The risk analysis consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of participating in this collaboration, while protecting and preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. In our searches, we found no evidence of financial, ethical, or reputational concerns. IDF’s profile and activities are consistent with PAHO’s Strategic Plan and its collaboration history with PAHO is positive. Therefore, LEG recommendation is to proceed with the designation, as long as NMH believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Please do not hesitate to contact LEG should you require further assistance. Sincerely, LEG

Luna, Cristina (Consultant WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as ERP Reviewer on 10/31/2025 01:36:19 PM. Comments: Dear Carmen, Thank you for clearing this form. After reviewing it in ERP, we made minor edits and added comments to ensure alignment with the 2023–2025 collaboration work plan approved by Member States. We also adjusted language in the Proposed Workplan 2026–2028 for consistency with PAHO Governing Bodies documents. Kindly confirm if you agree with these adjustments. Please review the observations below and share any additional details or comments through the system. If needed, we can consult NSA for further guidance. Activity 2: Appears partially aligned with the approved line “Joint webinar and education materials on diabetes”, specifically the dissemination of PAHO public education materials. However, the reported results are very general. Could you provide brief examples (e.g., webinar titles, types of materials shared, or campaigns supported) to strengthen the evidence of collaboration? Activity 3: The development of infographics is not explicitly in the approved plan but could complement the public education efforts under the same line. Please confirm if these infographics were developed jointly with PAHO or with input from PAHO technical team. Activities 4 & 5. Appears to be aligned with the approved line “Joint webinar and education materials on diabetes”. We consolidated both under this approved activity for clarity. Please confirm if you agree with the proposed change. Final Comment The following approved collaboration lines/activities in the 2023-2025 Workplan were not reported in the current submission. Could you clarify whether any actions were taken under these lines? 1. Quality of care indicators and chronic care passport o Advocacy for national monitoring and reporting of diabetes quality of care indicators. o Survey on the use of the quality-of-care model and Chronic Care Passport in the Region. 2. Access to diagnosis and medicines, including insulin o Development of a methodology to gather national data on access and affordability of insulin. o Promotion of the Strategic Fund at the national level (partially reported). We appreciate your collaboration and look forward to your feedback.

NSAObjetivesENG:
The IDF is the global voice of the diabetes community. We are a non-profit umbrella organization representing 251 national diabetes associations across 158 countries and territories, collaborating to enhance and empower the lives of the estimated 590 million individuals living with diabetes and to prevent diabetes in those at risk.

The IDF is an international non-profit association established in accordance with Title III of the Belgian law of June 27, 1921, which has since been replaced by the Belgian Code on Companies and Associations.

Our vision is to ensure access to affordable, quality diabetes care and education worldwide.

Our mission is to improve the lives of people living with diabetes and to prevent diabetes in those at risk.
NSAObjetivesSPA:
La IDF es la voz global de la comunidad de diabetes. Somos una organización no lucrativa que agrupa a 251 asociaciones nacionales de diabetes en 158 países y territorios, trabajando juntos para mejorar y empoderar la vida de las aproximadamente 590 millones de personas que viven con diabetes y prevenir la diabetes en aquellos que están en riesgo.

La IDF es una asociación internacional sin fines de lucro establecida de acuerdo con el Título III de la ley belga del 27 de junio de 1921, ahora reemplazada por el Código belga de Sociedades y Asociaciones.

Nuestra visión es garantizar el acceso a una atención y educación sobre diabetes asequibles y de calidad en todo el mundo.

Nuestra misión es mejorar la vida de las personas que viven con diabetes y prevenir la diabetes en aquellos que están en riesgo.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
The IDF advocates for enhanced diabetes prevention, education, and the highest quality of care for all individuals living with diabetes.

In close collaboration with its member associations, the IDF strives to increase access to and improve the quality of care currently available. It achieves this through advocacy, education for people with diabetes to assist them in managing their condition, public awareness and health improvement campaigns, and the promotion of the free exchange of diabetes knowledge.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
La IDF aboga por un aumento en la prevención de la diabetes, la educación y la mejor atención posible para cualquier persona que viva con diabetes.

En estrecha colaboración con sus asociaciones miembros, la IDF trabaja para aumentar el acceso y mejorar la calidad de la atención actualmente disponible. Lo hace a través de la defensa, la educación para las personas con diabetes para ayudarles a manejar su condición, campañas de concienciación pública y mejora de la salud, y la promoción del intercambio libre de conocimientos sobre la diabetes.

NSAObjectivesFRE:
L'IDF est la voix mondiale de la communauté diabétique. Nous sommes une organisation non lucrative qui regroupe 251 associations nationales de diabète dans 158 pays et territoires, travaillant ensemble pour améliorer et autonomiser la vie des 590 millions de personnes vivant avec le diabète et prévenir le diabète chez ceux qui sont à risque.

L'IDF est une association internationale à but non lucratif établie conformément au Titre III de la loi belge du 27 juin 1921, désormais remplacée par le Code belge des sociétés et des associations.

Notre vision est de garantir l'accès à des soins et à une éducation sur le diabète de qualité et abordables dans le monde entier.

Notre mission est d'améliorer la vie des personnes vivant avec le diabète et de prévenir le diabète chez ceux qui sont à risque.
NSAObjectivesPOR:
A IDF é a voz global da comunidade diabética. Somos uma organização não governamental que reúne 251 associações nacionais de diabetes em 158 países e territórios, trabalhando juntos para melhorar e capacitar a vida das aproximadamente 590 milhões de pessoas que vivem com diabetes e prevenir a diabetes em aqueles que estão em risco.

A IDF é uma associação internacional sem fins lucrativos estabelecida de acordo com o Título III da lei belga de 27 de junho de 1921, agora substituída pelo Código Belga de Empresas e Associações.

Nossa visão é garantir o acesso a cuidados e educação sobre diabetes acessíveis e de qualidade em todo o mundo.

Nossa missão é melhorar a vida das pessoas que vivem com diabetes e prevenir a diabetes em aqueles que estão em risco.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
L'IDF plaide en faveur d'une augmentation de la prévention du diabète, de l'éducation et des meilleurs soins possibles pour toute personne vivant avec le diabète.

En étroite collaboration avec ses associations membres, l'IDF s'efforce d'accroître l'accès aux soins et d'améliorer la qualité des soins actuellement disponibles. Elle le fait par le biais de plaidoyer, d'éducation pour les personnes diabétiques afin de les aider à gérer leur condition, de campagnes de sensibilisation du public et d'amélioration de la santé, ainsi que de la promotion de l'échange libre de connaissances sur le diabète.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
A IDF defende o aumento da prevenção do diabetes, educação e o melhor cuidado possível para todos que vivem com diabetes.

Em estreita colaboração com suas associações membros, a IDF trabalha para aumentar o acesso e melhorar a qualidade do atendimento atualmente disponível. Isso é feito por meio de advocacy, educação para pessoas com diabetes para ajudá-las a gerenciar sua condição, campanhas de conscientização pública e melhoria da saúde, e a promoção da livre troca de conhecimento sobre diabetes.
NSABoardMembersFRE:
Le Conseil d'Administration est l'organe de gestion élu de la Fédération Internationale du Diabète (FID). Plus d'informations sur les affiliations de ses membres peuvent être trouvées sur www.idf.org.

Ade Torrent, Maria de la Paz (WDC) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 12/9/2025 05:17:02 PM. Comments: In agreement to delete activity #3, since is a PATH independent activity, supporting efforts for better access to G6PD testing for a safer radical cure treatment. For activity #4, original activity FLAMME paused and specifically PAHO not involved on it, but instead PATH will support MOH with chemoprevention activities in Colombia following PAHO/WHO recommendations, and for that we are coordinating together. So perhaps we can delete the FLAMME specifically and just leave as: Through the PMI REACH project, PATH will directly support the introduction of chemoprevention interventions in Colombia, providing technical and operational support in planning, implementation, and evaluation of a chemoprevention strategy for Plasmodium falciparum. Thank you
Luna, Cristina (WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as ERP Reviewer on 12/9/2025 04:42:52 PM. Por favor, confirma si estás de acuerdo para proceder con el ajuste.

NSAObjetivesENG:
The IDF is the global voice of the diabetes community. We are a non-profit umbrella organization representing 251 national diabetes associations across 158 countries and territories, collaborating to enhance and empower the lives of the estimated 590 million individuals living with diabetes and to prevent diabetes in those at risk.

The IDF is an international non-profit association established in accordance with Title III of the Belgian law of June 27, 1921, which has since been replaced by the Belgian Code on Companies and Associations.

Our vision is to ensure access to affordable, quality diabetes care and education worldwide.

Our mission is to improve the lives of people living with diabetes and to prevent diabetes in those at risk.
NSAObjetivesSPA:
La IDF es la voz global de la comunidad de diabetes. Somos una organización no lucrativa que agrupa a 251 asociaciones nacionales de diabetes en 158 países y territorios, trabajando juntos para mejorar y empoderar la vida de las aproximadamente 590 millones de personas que viven con diabetes y prevenir la diabetes en aquellos que están en riesgo.

La IDF es una asociación internacional sin fines de lucro establecida de acuerdo con el Título III de la ley belga del 27 de junio de 1921, ahora reemplazada por el Código belga de Sociedades y Asociaciones.

Nuestra visión es garantizar el acceso a una atención y educación sobre diabetes asequibles y de calidad en todo el mundo.

Nuestra misión es mejorar la vida de las personas que viven con diabetes y prevenir la diabetes en aquellos que están en riesgo.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
The IDF advocates for enhanced diabetes prevention, education, and the highest quality of care for all individuals living with diabetes.

In close collaboration with its member associations, the IDF strives to increase access to and improve the quality of care currently available. It achieves this through advocacy, education for people with diabetes to assist them in managing their condition, public awareness and health improvement campaigns, and the promotion of the free exchange of diabetes knowledge.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
La IDF aboga por un aumento en la prevención de la diabetes, la educación y la mejor atención posible para cualquier persona que viva con diabetes.

En estrecha colaboración con sus asociaciones miembros, la IDF trabaja para aumentar el acceso y mejorar la calidad de la atención actualmente disponible. Lo hace a través de la defensa, la educación para las personas con diabetes para ayudarles a manejar su condición, campañas de concienciación pública y mejora de la salud, y la promoción del intercambio libre de conocimientos sobre la diabetes.

NSAObjectivesFRE:
L'IDF est la voix mondiale de la communauté diabétique. Nous sommes une organisation non lucrative qui regroupe 251 associations nationales de diabète dans 158 pays et territoires, travaillant ensemble pour améliorer et autonomiser la vie des 590 millions de personnes vivant avec le diabète et prévenir le diabète chez ceux qui sont à risque.

L'IDF est une association internationale à but non lucratif établie conformément au Titre III de la loi belge du 27 juin 1921, désormais remplacée par le Code belge des sociétés et des associations.

Notre vision est de garantir l'accès à des soins et à une éducation sur le diabète de qualité et abordables dans le monde entier.

Notre mission est d'améliorer la vie des personnes vivant avec le diabète et de prévenir le diabète chez ceux qui sont à risque.
NSAObjectivesPOR:
A IDF é a voz global da comunidade diabética. Somos uma organização não governamental que reúne 251 associações nacionais de diabetes em 158 países e territórios, trabalhando juntos para melhorar e capacitar a vida das aproximadamente 590 milhões de pessoas que vivem com diabetes e prevenir a diabetes em aqueles que estão em risco.

A IDF é uma associação internacional sem fins lucrativos estabelecida de acordo com o Título III da lei belga de 27 de junho de 1921, agora substituída pelo Código Belga de Empresas e Associações.

Nossa visão é garantir o acesso a cuidados e educação sobre diabetes acessíveis e de qualidade em todo o mundo.

Nossa missão é melhorar a vida das pessoas que vivem com diabetes e prevenir a diabetes em aqueles que estão em risco.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
L'IDF plaide en faveur d'une augmentation de la prévention du diabète, de l'éducation et des meilleurs soins possibles pour toute personne vivant avec le diabète.

En étroite collaboration avec ses associations membres, l'IDF s'efforce d'accroître l'accès aux soins et d'améliorer la qualité des soins actuellement disponibles. Elle le fait par le biais de plaidoyer, d'éducation pour les personnes diabétiques afin de les aider à gérer leur condition, de campagnes de sensibilisation du public et d'amélioration de la santé, ainsi que de la promotion de l'échange libre de connaissances sur le diabète.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
A IDF defende o aumento da prevenção do diabetes, educação e o melhor cuidado possível para todos que vivem com diabetes.

Em estreita colaboração com suas associações membros, a IDF trabalha para aumentar o acesso e melhorar a qualidade do atendimento atualmente disponível. Isso é feito por meio de advocacy, educação para pessoas com diabetes para ajudá-las a gerenciar sua condição, campanhas de conscientização pública e melhoria da saúde, e a promoção da livre troca de conhecimento sobre diabetes.
NSABoardMembersFRE:
Le Conseil d'Administration est l'organe de gestion élu de la Fédération Internationale du Diabète (FID). Plus d'informations sur les affiliations de ses membres peuvent être trouvées sur www.idf.org.

Title: March of Dimes
NSAObjectives:
Nearly 90 years ago, what began with President Franklin D. Roosevelt's personal struggle with polio led to the creation of the National Foundation for Infantile Paralysis, better known as March of Dimes. We pioneered the vaccine research
leading to the eradication of polio in the U.S., and then we shifted focus to address some of the biggest health threats to moms and babies with innovations like folic acid, newborn screening and surfactant therapy. Today we educate medical professionals and the public about best practices for moms and babies; we support lifesaving research; we provide comfort and support to families during their newborn's NICU stay; and we promote policy and legislation at the federal and state level to promote the health and well‐being of moms, babies and families. For the past many decades, March of Dimes has helped millions of babies survive and thrive. Now, we're building on that legacy to level the playing field for all moms and babies regardless of their age, socio‐economic background or demographics. We support moms throughout their pregnancy and we support radical improvements to the care they receive all along the pregnancy journey. March of Dimes leads the fight for the health of all moms and babies and we are working to end preventable maternal and infant deaths.
NSAWorkFields:
March of Dimes leads the fight for the health of all moms and babies. We support research, lead programs, and provide
education and advocacy so that every family can have the best possible start. Building on a successful 87‐year legacy, we support every pregnant person and every family. We imagine a world where every mom and baby is healthy regardless of wealth, race, gender or geography. Organizational goals: End preventable maternal and infant death  through three main pillars: improving access to care, reducing hypertensive disorders of pregnancy, and prevent preterm birth.
NSABoardMembers:
The National Board of Trustees manages all affairs of the organization and is the main decision making body. It is
comprised of 16 members, all with the right to vote. The Trustees serve for four years and do not receive remuneration.

Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 11/14/2025 11:46:23 AM. Comments: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed your FENSA request for March of Dimes (“MoD”) for the purpose of renewing official relations with the entity. Subject to the considerations indicated below, LEG recommendation is to proceed with the collaboration, as long as the Focal Point believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Based on the documentation provided by March of Dimes and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments: March of Dimes is a U.S.-incorporated non-profit public charity (501(c)(3)), headquartered in Arlington, Virginia, registered as a legal person in the United States. Its mission is “to lead the fight for the health of all moms and babies,” focusing on ending preventable maternal health risks and deaths, preventing preterm birth and infant mortality, and closing the health-equity gap through advocacy, research, and community programs. Its main income sources are contributions and gifts, through fundraising events and other grants. Governance is vested in a Board of Trustees, who manage and control all affairs, and the executive management exercises all powers of the Board between meetings and advise officers. Members of the Board and Executive Committee were screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List and no matches were identified. March of Dimes declared no links with tobacco and arms industries on 19/9/2025 and no links were uncovered by LEG either. For the purposes of FENSA, LEG considers MoD to be a non-governmental organization (NGO). With regard to the risk assessment process, this process consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of participating in the proposed collaboration, protecting while preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. In that context, for the proposed activities on maternal and infant health, LEG does not identify risks or conflicts of interest that would affect PAHO's integrity, independence, credibility, or reputation and, from the point of view of FENSA, LEG considers that this collaboration can proceed as the proposed collaboration is in the interest of the Region and aligns with PAHO's objectives, contributing to improve maternal and infant health. However, LEG notes that MoD has disclosed ties to the food and beverages, healthcare, and pharmaceutical sectors. In our searches, we found that MoD receives funding from various private companies like Publix, Merck, Pfizer, and many others. While MoD has several private sector donors, most of its funding is derived from the March of Babies, a charitable walking event sponsored by the March of Dimes that commenced in 1970, and other fundraising events. 55th annual March for Babies raised more than $15 million in August 2025. We noted no evidence of financial or governance conflicts with restricted sectors under FENSA. In past FENSA assessments, concerns have been raised regarding asset deficiency (in 2017 and 2018). However, as indicated in past assessments and from the financial statements submitted, it is clear that MoD has gained financial stability over the years. MoD has been in operation for 75 years and in official relations with PAHO/WHO since 2020. Notwithstanding the above, before proceeding with any collaboration with MoD, please have them ensure that the funds for the activities being implemented with PAHO are not earmarked or linked to any food and beverage or pharmaceutical company. From a FENSA perspective, LEG recommends renewing MoD’s status as NSA in official relations. Please do not hesitate to contact LEG should you require further assistance.
Luna, Cristina (Consultant WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as ERP Reviewer on 11/6/2025 04:51:30 PM. Comments: Dear Pablo, Thank you for reviewing the MoD renewal form. After reviewing it in ERP, we would also like to share some observations on the reported activities to ensure consistency with the approved 2023–2025 work plan. Please note that the approved plan for 2023–2025 included three specific lines of collaboration/activities (I am sending the 2023 background paper with the full plan by email): 1. Organization of the 10th and 11th International Conferences on Birth Defects and Disabilities in the Developing World (2023 in Chile and 2025 in a location to be determined), organized by MoD in collaboration with PAHO and with support from CDC. Expected outcome: Provide a platform to strengthen birth defects surveillance, prevention, screening, and care in lower- and middle-income countries of the Region. 2. Advocacy for prevention, detection, and management of birth defects and preterm birth during World Birth Defects Day (March 3) and World Prematurity Day (November 17). Expected outcome: Collaborative activities such as webinars, social media engagement, and communication materials to raise awareness in Latin American countries. 3. Facilitate experience sharing and dissemination of data on birth defects and other perinatal outcomes to support decision-making and interventions aimed at reducing disease burden in Latin America and the Caribbean. Expected outcome: Increased use of data on birth defects for decision-making in the Region. Observations: The reported actions (webinars, reports, campaigns) do not mention the international conferences or advocacy activities during the commemorative days, nor is there a clear reference to experience sharing on birth defects surveillance. Some activities appear to be MoD-led initiatives rather than collaborations with PAHO as outlined in the approved plan. Suggestions: -It would be helpful to indicate how the reported activities relate to the approved activities and describe PAHO’s role or mutual contributions in these collaborations. -If additional activities were implemented beyond the approved plan, please keep them in separate boxes as complementary initiatives. Regarding the proposed 2026–2028 plan: We have reviewed the recommendations provided by LEG and align with them. We look forward to seeing these incorporated to further strengthen the action plan. If a brief call is arranged, we will be happy to participate and discuss these suggestions further. Thank you for your collaboration and support.

Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as LEG Reviewer on 11/6/2025 02:42:18 PM. Comments: Dear Pablo; Thank you for your clarification and comments. They were very helpful in understanding the context of the proposed activities. With respect to activities 4 and 6, please note that under the FENSA policy all proposed engagements must demonstrate a clear benefit to public health and conform with PAHO’s Constitution, mandate and Strategic Plan. When describing the proposed activities, it is important to clearly articulate how each activity aligns with PAHO’s mandate and Strategic Plan, and to specify the nature of PAHO’s engagement with each non-State actor. This ensures that the activity is reviewed in accordance with FENSA principles and the applicable due diligence and risk assessment process conducted by LEG. Please also note that if an activity does not constitute a PAHO activity, it should not be proposed as such. In accordance with FENSA, PAHO should avoid engagements that fall outside the scope of its mandate or where the Organization has no defined role, in order to safeguard PAHO’s independence, credibility, and reputation. Please let us know if you have any questions. Thank you.
Duran, Pablo (URY) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 11/6/2025 09:39:02 AM. Comments: Dear Maria Florencia and colleagues, thank you for the review and comments. Regarding the clarifications requested, my comments/clarifications are: Activity 2: The series of webinars that MOD is organizing is alaigned with the PAHO initiative and Call for action toward reducing preventable maternal deaths. Within this initiative, PAHO (HSS/WH) is working on advocating, promoting implementation of evidence based interventions and evaluate results in this regard. The topic mentioned is one of the interventions included and the webinar represents an excellent opportunity to advicate and present PAHO leadership. With "PAHO will providea global expert" may be miswrited but the plan was to identify a PAHO expert that provide a presentation on this topic and show what PAHO recommendas and is working on. Based on preliminary discussions with responsibles form MOD and WH, the presenter may be the UNit Chief r the consultant leading maternal mortality at PAHO. Regarding Activity 3: The workplan mentions webinars to be coordinated by PAHO. Could you elaborate on MoD’s role in this activity? It appears to be a separate activity that PAHO conducts: It refers to regular webinars that PAHO corodinates on key topics/days as World Prematurity Day, World Birth Defects Day and World´s Safety Patients day. MOD has a large experience and strong Global, Regional and Domestic (US) ledership in social movilization and communication on these topics. Based on that, MOD was invited to present its experience and contribute to the call to strengthen patient´s safety during the Webinar organized and leaded by PAHO. As previous years, we are planning to invite MOD to bring also its experience to the network of preterm groups of parents, contributing to rise the voices of families and to promote person and family centereed care. Finally, we expect to involve MOD in the same way during the conmemorative activitis around the World Births Defects Day. Regarding Activity 4: Please clarify how MoD’s annual report requires collaboration from PAHO and how it supports PAHO’s strategic plan, as this seems to be an activity MoD undertakes in its own capacity: MOD asked PAHO to review the cards in order to aligned the content to PAHO´s recommendations and link their messages to PAHO´s. But it can be refrased or deleted as it is not a primarily PAHO activity. Regarding Activity 5: Could you elaborate on what is meant by “Promotion of Born Too Report,” including how PAHO will collaborate or contribute, and how this activity aligns with PAHO’s strategic objectives?: This activity involves presenting the results, facts and recommendations included in the second edition of the technical document Born too soon. PAHO, along with WHO, other partners and MOD, has contributed to this report. It includes experiences and lessons learned from the Americas and is aligned with Global and Rwgional frameworks as UN, WHO and PAHO resolutions. At different technical sessions MOD contributes on diseminating this information and reinforce the recommendations considered in PAHO SP. Regarding Activity 6: Please explain how MoD’s participation in a group for designing clinical guidelines relates to PAHO’s work: PAHO is working on the clinical guideline on newborn screening. MOD will provide inputs and recommendations as representatives of civil society. PAHO´s product will be the clinical guideline. MOD output will be recommendations and inputs that will improve the content of the guidelin. Should we state the product as a technical document with the results of the review and recommendations on the clinical guideline on newborn screening devveloped by PAHO? Or is ti better to eliminate this activity. We, from the technical and participation perspective, consider it relevant. I hope these comments clarify the questions and concerns. Please let me know if aditional clarifications or changes are needed. Thank you and best regards

NSAObjetivesENG: Nearly 90 years ago, what began with President Franklin D. Roosevelt's personal struggle with polio led to the creation of the National Foundation for Infantile Paralysis, better known as March of Dimes. We pioneered the vaccine research that led to the eradication of polio in the U.S., and then we shifted our focus to address some of the biggest health threats to mothers and babies with innovations like folic acid, newborn screening, and surfactant therapy. Today, we educate medical professionals and the public about best practices for mothers and babies; we support lifesaving research; we provide comfort and support to families during their newborn's NICU stay; and we promote policy and legislation at the federal and state levels to enhance the health and well-being of mothers, babies, and families. For many decades, March of Dimes has helped millions of babies survive and thrive. Now, we're building on that legacy to level the playing field for all mothers and babies, regardless of their age, socio-economic background, or demographics. We support mothers throughout their pregnancy and advocate for radical improvements in the care they receive throughout the pregnancy journey. March of Dimes leads the fight for the health of all mothers and babies, and we are working to end preventable maternal and infant deaths.
NSAObjetivesSPA: Casi hace 90 años, lo que comenzó con la lucha personal del presidente Franklin D. Roosevelt contra la poliomielitis llevó a la creación de la Fundación Nacional para la Parálisis Infantil, más conocida como March of Dimes. Pioneros en la investigación de vacunas que condujo a la erradicación de la poliomielitis en los EE. UU., luego cambiamos nuestro enfoque para abordar algunas de las mayores amenazas a la salud de las mamás y los bebés con innovaciones como el ácido fólico, el tamizaje neonatal y la terapia con surfactante. Hoy educamos a los profesionales médicos y al público sobre las mejores prácticas para mamás y bebés; apoyamos investigaciones que salvan vidas; brindamos consuelo y apoyo a las familias durante la estancia de su recién nacido en la UCI neonatal; y promovemos políticas y legislación a nivel federal y estatal para fomentar la salud y el bienestar de mamás, bebés y familias. Durante las últimas décadas, March of Dimes ha ayudado a millones de bebés a sobrevivir y prosperar. Ahora, estamos construyendo sobre ese legado para nivelar el campo de juego para todas las mamás y los bebés, independientemente de su edad, antecedentes socioeconómicos o demográficos. Apoyamos a las mamás a lo largo de su embarazo y apoyamos mejoras radicales en la atención que reciben durante todo el viaje del embarazo. March of Dimes lidera la lucha por la salud de todas las mamás y los bebés y estamos trabajando para poner fin a las muertes maternas e infantiles prevenibles.
NSAWorkFieldsENG: March of Dimes leads the fight for the health of all mothers and babies. We support research, lead programs, and provide education and advocacy so that every family can have the best possible start. Building on a successful 87-year legacy, we support every pregnant person and every family. We envision a world where every mother and baby is healthy, regardless of wealth, race, gender, or geography. Our organizational goals are to end preventable maternal and infant deaths through three main pillars: improving access to care, reducing hypertensive disorders of pregnancy, and preventing preterm birth.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA: March of Dimes lidera la lucha por la salud de todas las mamás y los bebés. Apoyamos la investigación, dirigimos programas y proporcionamos educación y defensa para que cada familia pueda tener el mejor comienzo posible. Basándonos en un legado exitoso de 87 años, apoyamos a cada persona embarazada y a cada familia. Imaginamos un mundo donde cada mamá y cada bebé sean saludables, independientemente de la riqueza, la raza, el género o la geografía. Objetivos organizacionales: poner fin a la muerte materna e infantil prevenible a través de tres pilares principales: mejorar el acceso a la atención, reducir los trastornos hipertensivos del embarazo y prevenir el nacimiento prematuro.
NSABoardMembersENG: The National Board of Trustees oversees all matters of the organization and serves as the primary decision-making body. It consists of 16 members, all of whom have the right to vote. The Trustees serve a term of four years and do not receive any compensation.
NSABoardMembersSPA: La Junta Nacional de Fideicomisarios gestiona todos los asuntos de la organización y es el principal órgano de toma de decisiones. Está compuesta por 16 miembros, todos con derecho a voto. Los Fideicomisarios sirven por un período de cuatro años y no reciben remuneración.
NSAOrganizationBodiesENG: PAHO/WHO stands for the Pan American Health Organization/World Health Organization.
NSAOrganizationBodiesSPA: OPS/OMS
NSAFocalpointRoleENG: Senior Vice President, Chief Impact Officer

TitlePOR: March of Dimes in Portuguese is "Março dos Dimes".
NSAObjectivesFRE: Il y a près de 90 ans, ce qui a commencé par la lutte personnelle du président Franklin D. Roosevelt contre la polio a conduit à la création de la Fondation nationale pour la paralysie infantile, mieux connue sous le nom de March of Dimes. Nous avons été les pionniers de la recherche sur les vaccins qui a conduit à l'éradication de la polio aux États-Unis, puis nous avons réorienté nos efforts pour répondre à certaines des plus grandes menaces sanitaires pour les mères et les bébés avec des innovations telles que l'acide folique, le dépistage des nouveau-nés et la thérapie par surfactant. Aujourd'hui, nous éduquons les professionnels de la santé et le public sur les meilleures pratiques pour les mères et les bébés ; nous soutenons la recherche qui sauve des vies ; nous apportons réconfort et soutien aux familles pendant le séjour de leur nouveau-né en unité de soins intensifs néonatals (USIN) ; et nous promouvons des politiques et des législations au niveau fédéral et étatique pour favoriser la santé et le bien-être des mères, des bébés et des familles. Depuis de nombreuses décennies, March of Dimes a aidé des millions de bébés à survivre et à s'épanouir. Maintenant, nous bâtissons sur cet héritage pour égaliser les chances pour toutes les mères et tous les bébés, quel que soit leur âge, leur origine socio-économique ou leurs caractéristiques démographiques. Nous soutenons les mères tout au long de leur grossesse et nous soutenons des améliorations radicales des soins qu'elles reçoivent tout au long de ce parcours. March of Dimes mène le combat pour la santé de toutes les mères et de tous les bébés et nous travaillons à mettre fin aux décès maternels et infantiles évitables.
NSAObjectivesPOR: Quase 90 anos atrás, o que começou com a luta pessoal do presidente Franklin D. Roosevelt contra a poliomielite levou à criação da Fundação Nacional para a Paralisia Infantil, mais conhecida como March of Dimes. Nós pioneiramos a pesquisa de vacinas que levou à erradicação da poliomielite nos EUA, e então mudamos o foco para enfrentar algumas das maiores ameaças à saúde de mães e bebês com inovações como ácido fólico, triagem de recém-nascidos e terapia com surfactante. Hoje, educamos profissionais de saúde e o público sobre as melhores práticas para mães e bebês; apoiamos pesquisas que salvam vidas; fornecemos conforto e apoio às famílias durante a estadia de seus recém-nascidos na UTI neonatal; e promovemos políticas e legislações em nível federal e estadual para promover a saúde e o bem-estar de mães, bebês e famílias. Nas últimas décadas, a March of Dimes ajudou milhões de bebês a sobreviver e prosperar. Agora, estamos construindo sobre esse legado para nivelar o campo de jogo para todas as mães e bebês, independentemente de sua idade, origem socioeconômica ou demografia. Apoiamo mães durante toda a sua gravidez e apoiamos melhorias radicais nos cuidados que recebem ao longo de toda a jornada da gravidez. A March of Dimes lidera a luta pela saúde de todas as mães e bebês e estamos trabalhando para acabar com as mortes maternas e infantis evitáveis.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE: March of Dimes mène le combat pour la santé de toutes les mamans et de tous les bébés. Nous soutenons la recherche, dirigeons des programmes et fournissons éducation et plaidoyer afin que chaque famille puisse bénéficier du meilleur départ possible. S'appuyant sur un héritage réussi de 87 ans, nous soutenons chaque personne enceinte et chaque famille. Nous imaginons un monde où chaque maman et chaque bébé est en bonne santé, indépendamment de la richesse, de la race, du genre ou de la géographie. Objectifs organisationnels : Mettre fin aux décès maternels et infantiles évitables à travers trois piliers principaux : améliorer l'accès aux soins, réduire les troubles hypertensifs de la grossesse et prévenir la naissance prématurée.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR: A March of Dimes lidera a luta pela saúde de todas as mães e bebês. Nós apoiamos pesquisas, lideramos programas e fornecemos educação e defesa para que cada família possa ter o melhor começo possível. Baseando-se em um legado de 87 anos de sucesso, apoiamos cada pessoa grávida e cada família. Imaginamos um mundo onde cada mãe e bebê sejam saudáveis, independentemente de riqueza, raça, gênero ou geografia. Objetivos organizacionais: acabar com a morte materna e infantil evitável por meio de três pilares principais: melhorar o acesso ao cuidado, reduzir os distúrbios hipertensivos na gravidez e prevenir o nascimento prematuro.
NSABoardMembersFRE: Le Conseil national des administrateurs gère toutes les affaires de l'organisation et est l'organe principal de prise de décision. Il est composé de 16 membres, tous ayant le droit de vote. Les administrateurs servent pendant quatre ans et ne reçoivent pas de rémunération.
NSABoardMembersPOR: O Conselho Nacional de Curadores gerencia todos os assuntos da organização e é o principal órgão de tomada de decisões. É composto por 16 membros, todos com direito a voto. Os Curadores servem por quatro anos e não recebem remuneração.

TitleENGSPA: Asociación Americana del Habla, Lenguaje y Audición
NSAObjetivesENG: Nearly 90 years ago, what began with President Franklin D. Roosevelt's personal struggle with polio led to the creation of the National Foundation for Infantile Paralysis, better known as March of Dimes. We pioneered the vaccine research that led to the eradication of polio in the U.S., and then we shifted our focus to address some of the biggest health threats to mothers and babies with innovations like folic acid, newborn screening, and surfactant therapy. Today, we educate medical professionals and the public about best practices for mothers and babies; we support lifesaving research; we provide comfort and support to families during their newborn's NICU stay; and we promote policy and legislation at the federal and state levels to enhance the health and well-being of mothers, babies, and families. For many decades, March of Dimes has helped millions of babies survive and thrive. Now, we're building on that legacy to level the playing field for all mothers and babies, regardless of their age, socio-economic background, or demographics. We support mothers throughout their pregnancy and advocate for radical improvements in the care they receive throughout the pregnancy journey. March of Dimes leads the fight for the health of all mothers and babies, and we are working to end preventable maternal and infant deaths.
NSAObjetivesSPA: Casi hace 90 años, lo que comenzó con la lucha personal del presidente Franklin D. Roosevelt contra la poliomielitis llevó a la creación de la Fundación Nacional para la Parálisis Infantil, más conocida como March of Dimes. Pioneros en la investigación de vacunas que condujo a la erradicación de la poliomielitis en los EE. UU., luego cambiamos nuestro enfoque para abordar algunas de las mayores amenazas a la salud de las mamás y los bebés con innovaciones como el ácido fólico, el tamizaje neonatal y la terapia con surfactante. Hoy educamos a los profesionales médicos y al público sobre las mejores prácticas para mamás y bebés; apoyamos investigaciones que salvan vidas; brindamos consuelo y apoyo a las familias durante la estancia de su recién nacido en la UCI neonatal; y promovemos políticas y legislación a nivel federal y estatal para fomentar la salud y el bienestar de mamás, bebés y familias. Durante las últimas décadas, March of Dimes ha ayudado a millones de bebés a sobrevivir y prosperar. Ahora, estamos construyendo sobre ese legado para nivelar el campo de juego para todas las mamás y los bebés, independientemente de su edad, antecedentes socioeconómicos o demográficos. Apoyamos a las mamás a lo largo de su embarazo y apoyamos mejoras radicales en la atención que reciben durante todo el viaje del embarazo. March of Dimes lidera la lucha por la salud de todas las mamás y los bebés y estamos trabajando para poner fin a las muertes maternas e infantiles prevenibles.
NSAWorkFieldsENG: March of Dimes leads the fight for the health of all mothers and babies. We support research, lead programs, and provide education and advocacy so that every family can have the best possible start. Building on a successful 87-year legacy, we support every pregnant person and every family. We envision a world where every mother and baby is healthy, regardless of wealth, race, gender, or geography. Our organizational goals are to end preventable maternal and infant deaths through three main pillars: improving access to care, reducing hypertensive disorders of pregnancy, and preventing preterm birth.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA: March of Dimes lidera la lucha por la salud de todas las mamás y los bebés. Apoyamos la investigación, dirigimos programas y proporcionamos educación y defensa para que cada familia pueda tener el mejor comienzo posible. Basándonos en un legado exitoso de 87 años, apoyamos a cada persona embarazada y a cada familia. Imaginamos un mundo donde cada mamá y cada bebé sean saludables, independientemente de la riqueza, la raza, el género o la geografía. Objetivos organizacionales: poner fin a la muerte materna e infantil prevenible a través de tres pilares principales: mejorar el acceso a la atención, reducir los trastornos hipertensivos del embarazo y prevenir el nacimiento prematuro.
NSABoardMembersENG: The National Board of Trustees oversees all matters of the organization and serves as the primary decision-making body. It consists of 16 members, all of whom have the right to vote. The Trustees serve a term of four years and do not receive any compensation.
NSABoardMembersSPA: La Junta Nacional de Fideicomisarios gestiona todos los asuntos de la organización y es el principal órgano de toma de decisiones. Está compuesta por 16 miembros, todos con derecho a voto. Los Fideicomisarios sirven por un período de cuatro años y no reciben remuneración.
NSAOrganizationBodiesENG: PAHO/WHO stands for the Pan American Health Organization/World Health Organization.

NSAObjectivesFRE: Il y a près de 90 ans, ce qui a commencé par la lutte personnelle du président Franklin D. Roosevelt contre la polio a conduit à la création de la Fondation nationale pour la paralysie infantile, mieux connue sous le nom de March of Dimes. Nous avons été les pionniers de la recherche sur les vaccins qui a conduit à l'éradication de la polio aux États-Unis, puis nous avons réorienté nos efforts pour répondre à certaines des plus grandes menaces sanitaires pour les mères et les bébés avec des innovations telles que l'acide folique, le dépistage des nouveau-nés et la thérapie par surfactant. Aujourd'hui, nous éduquons les professionnels de la santé et le public sur les meilleures pratiques pour les mères et les bébés ; nous soutenons la recherche qui sauve des vies ; nous apportons réconfort et soutien aux familles pendant le séjour de leur nouveau-né en unité de soins intensifs néonatals (USIN) ; et nous promouvons des politiques et des législations au niveau fédéral et étatique pour favoriser la santé et le bien-être des mères, des bébés et des familles. Depuis de nombreuses décennies, March of Dimes a aidé des millions de bébés à survivre et à s'épanouir. Maintenant, nous bâtissons sur cet héritage pour égaliser les chances pour toutes les mères et tous les bébés, quel que soit leur âge, leur origine socio-économique ou leurs caractéristiques démographiques. Nous soutenons les mères tout au long de leur grossesse et nous soutenons des améliorations radicales des soins qu'elles reçoivent tout au long de ce parcours. March of Dimes mène le combat pour la santé de toutes les mères et de tous les bébés et nous travaillons à mettre fin aux décès maternels et infantiles évitables.
NSAObjectivesPOR: Quase 90 anos atrás, o que começou com a luta pessoal do presidente Franklin D. Roosevelt contra a poliomielite levou à criação da Fundação Nacional para a Paralisia Infantil, mais conhecida como March of Dimes. Nós pioneiramos a pesquisa de vacinas que levou à erradicação da poliomielite nos EUA, e então mudamos o foco para enfrentar algumas das maiores ameaças à saúde de mães e bebês com inovações como ácido fólico, triagem de recém-nascidos e terapia com surfactante. Hoje, educamos profissionais de saúde e o público sobre as melhores práticas para mães e bebês; apoiamos pesquisas que salvam vidas; fornecemos conforto e apoio às famílias durante a estadia de seus recém-nascidos na UTI neonatal; e promovemos políticas e legislações em nível federal e estadual para promover a saúde e o bem-estar de mães, bebês e famílias. Nas últimas décadas, a March of Dimes ajudou milhões de bebês a sobreviver e prosperar. Agora, estamos construindo sobre esse legado para nivelar o campo de jogo para todas as mães e bebês, independentemente de sua idade, origem socioeconômica ou demografia. Apoiamo mães durante toda a sua gravidez e apoiamos melhorias radicais nos cuidados que recebem ao longo de toda a jornada da gravidez. A March of Dimes lidera a luta pela saúde de todas as mães e bebês e estamos trabalhando para acabar com as mortes maternas e infantis evitáveis.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE: March of Dimes mène le combat pour la santé de toutes les mamans et de tous les bébés. Nous soutenons la recherche, dirigeons des programmes et fournissons éducation et plaidoyer afin que chaque famille puisse bénéficier du meilleur départ possible. S'appuyant sur un héritage réussi de 87 ans, nous soutenons chaque personne enceinte et chaque famille. Nous imaginons un monde où chaque maman et chaque bébé est en bonne santé, indépendamment de la richesse, de la race, du genre ou de la géographie. Objectifs organisationnels : Mettre fin aux décès maternels et infantiles évitables à travers trois piliers principaux : améliorer l'accès aux soins, réduire les troubles hypertensifs de la grossesse et prévenir la naissance prématurée.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR: A March of Dimes lidera a luta pela saúde de todas as mães e bebês. Nós apoiamos pesquisas, lideramos programas e fornecemos educação e defesa para que cada família possa ter o melhor começo possível. Baseando-se em um legado de 87 anos de sucesso, apoiamos cada pessoa grávida e cada família. Imaginamos um mundo onde cada mãe e bebê sejam saudáveis, independentemente de riqueza, raça, gênero ou geografia. Objetivos organizacionais: acabar com a morte materna e infantil evitável por meio de três pilares principais: melhorar o acesso ao cuidado, reduzir os distúrbios hipertensivos na gravidez e prevenir o nascimento prematuro.
NSABoardMembersFRE: Le Conseil national des administrateurs gère toutes les affaires de l'organisation et est l'organe principal de prise de décision. Il est composé de 16 membres, tous ayant le droit de vote. Les administrateurs servent pendant quatre ans et ne reçoivent pas de rémunération.
NSABoardMembersPOR: O Conselho Nacional de Curadores gerencia todos os assuntos da organização e é o principal órgão de tomada de decisões. É composto por 16 membros, todos com direito a voto. Os Curadores servem por quatro anos e não recebem remuneração.

Title: World Association for Sexual Health (WAS)
NSAObjectives: The purpose of WAS is to advance international cooperation in the field of Sexual Health and to coordinate activities designed to increase research and knowledge in sexual health and sexual rights, including sexuality education, sexual medicine, psycho-sexology, policy-making and advocacy.
NSAWorkFields:
The mission of WAS is carried out through:
Promoting sexual rights for all.
Developing the field of sexuality, sexual health, and sexology.
Facilitating the dissemination and exchange of information, ideas, and experiences.

Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 12/18/2025 10:58:44 AM. Comments: Dear Rodolfo Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed your FENSA request for the World Association for Sexual Health (“WAS”) for the purpose of renewing official relations with the entity. Subject to the considerations indicated below, LEG recommendation is to proceed with the renewal of the non-state actor (“NSA”) status, as long as the focal point believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Based on the documentation provided by WAS, and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments: WAS is an international confederation comprising federations, international and national societies, associations, institutes, and individual supporting members. Its members work professionally in the areas of sexual health, rights, well-being, research, education, public policy, law, and advocacy related to human relationships and personal well-being. WAS promotes health and rights across the lifespan and worldwide by advancing professional knowledge and practice, supporting research and evidence-based education, and strengthening clinical care and related services. LEG did not have access to WAS financial statements, but from publicly available information, we understand the main income sources are Membership fees and grants from philanthropic foundations. WAS is governed by three principal bodies: General Assembly, Advisory Committee, and Executive Committee. WAS’s President is the chairperson of the Executive Committee, the Advisory Committee and of the General Assembly. Members of the Executive Committee were screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List and no matches were identified. WAS declared no links with tobacco and arms industries on 23/10/2025 and no links were uncovered by LEG either. WAS disclosed links with the healthcare sectors. No associations with other relevant industries were identified. For the purposes of FENSA, LEG considers WAS to be a non-governmental organization. With regard to the risk assessment process, this process consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of participating in the proposed collaboration, protecting while preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. WAS has disclosed ties to the healthcare sector. In our search we found that various industry partners like Lawley Pharmaceuticals, BTL Industries and other public health non-governmental organizations have sponsored the WAS Congress in 2025, a major international gathering of professionals in sexual health, sexology and sexual rights hosted by WAS. As such, these don’t create a conflict of interest since these indicate links in context of the event and do not represent ongoing institutional funding or affiliation. No other evidence of ties to commercial or high-risk industries, nor was any adverse legal or reputational information found. WAS has previously engaged with the PAHO/WHO and has been in official relations since 2020. LEG understands that the proposed collaboration seeks to increase the quality of training for health workers at the primary care level and furthers the Sustainable Health Agenda for the Americas 2018-2030 and the PAHO Strategic Plan 2020-2025, thus proposed activities align with PAHO’s mandate. From a FENSA perspective, LEG has determined that the NSA status can be renewed. We remain available should you have any questions. Thank you.
