History:
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 1/5/2026 11:52:36 AM. Comments: LEG has reviewed the renewal application submitted by the American Public Health Association (APHA) and considers that its renewal for official relations with PAHO may be approved, based on and limited to the information to which LEG had access during the review process. Overall, APHA has maintained a long-standing, close, and constructive collaboration with PAHO since its official recognition in 2014. The partnership has focused on scientific and technical cooperation, including the co-hosting of annual scientific sessions at APHA Annual Meetings (2022–2025), a joint PAHO–APHA exhibit at the Public Health Expo, and joint participation in the Global Public Health Film Festival. The organizations have also collaborated through the American Journal of Public Health (AJPH) and the Pan American Journal of Public Health (PAJPH) on the translation and dissemination of peer-reviewed research. APHA has worked with PAHO on activities related to National Public Health Week, World Health Day, and Wellness Week, supported the Alliance of Public Health Associations of the Americas (APHA-AASPA), and coordinated communications to share regional public health opportunities and resources. Based on the documentation reviewed, LEG did not identify any conflict of interest or reputational risk that could compromise the integrity, independence, or reputation of PAHO. APHA demonstrates sound institutional governance and financial transparency, with regularly audited financial statements and consistent reporting practices as a U.S. nonprofit organization registered under section 501(c)(3). Independent verification did not identify any associations with tobacco or arms, nor any evidence of corruption or financial irregularities. With the information available to LEG, the APHA 2025 Annual Report lists several grant and organizational supporters from, or adjacent to, regulated or commercial health sectors, including Pfizer, Otsuka Cares (a philanthropic initiative of Otsuka America Pharmaceuticals), Blue Cross Blue Shield, and Kaiser EastBay. These references do not, in themselves, imply any conflict of interest or direct corporate influence on APHA’s governance or technical work. The documentation reviewed suggests sporadic and project-specific contributions, which, on their face, would not preclude PAHO from continuing to implement the proposed work plan. LEG notes that the requested clarification regarding the links between APHA and the pharmaceutical industry was not provided. Accordingly, LEG’s assessment is necessarily limited to the information available at the time of review. On this basis, and subject to the limitations noted above, LEG does not object to recommending the renewal of APHA’s status in official relations with PAHO.
Luna, Cristina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 12/19/2025 03:50:27 PM. Comments: ERP recommends proceeding to renew the status as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. However, ERP notices that LEG is conducting the FENSA review and risk analysis, and ERP final recommendation is conditional to its completion and clearance. While ERP supports moving forward with the renewal based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "NSA Originator" as LEG Reviewer on 12/16/2025 11:44:46 AM. Comments: Dear APHA colleagues, this is a friendly reminder that PAHO is awaiting on the requested clarifications regarding BHI's links with the pharmaceutical industry. Please note that the deadline is on December 18 COB. Otherwise, we will not be able to complete the evaluation for the upcoming cycle. Best,
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "NSA Originator" as LEG Reviewer on 12/1/2025 04:07:55 PM. Comments: This is a friendly follow-up to our message from November 14. Please note that the process cannot be completed until we receive the pending informaiton. Thank you,
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "NSA Originator" as LEG Reviewer on 11/14/2025 10:49:04 AM. Comments: Dear Vina Hulamm; We thank you for the information provided in the Information Requested from Non-State Actors form submitted as part of the renewal process for non-state actors in official relations with PAHO. We noted that in the declaration, APHA indicated no formal associations or links with the pharmaceutical or health care sectors. However, our independent review of the APHA 2025 Annual Report indicates that the organization listed several grant and organizational supporters from, or adjacent to, regulated or commercial health sectors, including Pfizer, Otsuka Cares (a philanthropic initiative of Otsuka America Pharmaceuticals), Blue Cross Blue Shield, and Kaiser EastBay. We are therefore reaching out to kindly request clarification and additional details on this aspect of the declaration. For our records, it would be helpful if APHA could briefly describe the nature and scope of any collaborations or funding relationships with these or similar entities, including their role—if any—in APHA’s activities, events, or publications. We would appreciate receiving this clarification at your earliest convenience so that we may ensure that the information contained in your file is accurate and fully aligned with the internal policies. Thank you very much for your cooperation and continued collaboration with PAHO.

NSAObjetivesENG: APHA’s mission is to improve public health and achieve health equity. The organization's vision is to create the healthiest nation within a generation. Its values reflect the beliefs of its members from various public health disciplines and over 40 countries. APHA publishes the American Journal of Public Health and The Nation's Health newspaper. At the Annual Meeting, thousands of individuals share the latest research in public health. APHA also leads public awareness campaigns such as Get Ready and National Public Health Week.
NSAObjetivesSPA: La misión de APHA es mejorar la salud del público y lograr la equidad en el estado de salud. La visión de la organización es crear la nación más saludable en una generación. Los valores de la organización reflejan las creencias de sus miembros de todas las disciplinas de la salud pública y de más de 40 países. APHA publica el American Journal of Public Health y el periódico The Nation's Health. En la Reunión Anual, miles de personas comparten la investigación más reciente en salud pública. APHA lidera campañas de concienciación pública como Get Ready y la Semana Nacional de la Salud Pública.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
APHA works to improve access to care, promote health equity, and support public health infrastructure. APHA also addresses emerging health challenges that we face today.  
The main areas of focus include health reform, emergency preparedness, health in all policies, climate change, environmental health, chronic diseases, healthy community design, transportation, tobacco, gun violence, injury prevention, and reproductive health, among others.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
La APHA trabaja para mejorar el acceso a la atención, lograr la equidad en salud y apoyar la infraestructura de salud pública. La APHA también se centra en los desafíos de salud emergentes que enfrentamos en la actualidad.  
Los principales campos de trabajo son: reforma de la salud, preparación para emergencias, salud en todas las políticas, cambio climático, salud ambiental, enfermedades crónicas, diseño de comunidades saludables, transporte, tabaco, violencia armada, prevención de lesiones y salud reproductiva, por nombrar algunos.
NSABoardMembersENG: APHA is governed by elected and appointed members. The Governing Council elects the Executive Board, the officers of the Association, and honorary members. Members of the Governing Council serve staggered two-year terms. The Executive Board carries out work in five areas: policy, management, program, membership, and deployment. The Board is a 24-member body that serves on behalf of the Governing Council. Members of the Executive Board serve staggered four-year terms among the voting members of the Board. Other boards and committees oversee publications, awards, science, advocacy, and more.
NSABoardMembersSPA: APHA está gobernada por miembros elegidos y designados. El Consejo de Gobierno elige a la Junta Ejecutiva, a los oficiales de la Asociación y a los miembros honorarios. Los miembros del Consejo de Gobierno sirven mandatos escalonados de dos años. La Junta Ejecutiva lleva a cabo su trabajo en cinco áreas: política, gestión, programas, membresía y despliegue. La Junta es un cuerpo de 24 miembros que actúa en nombre del Consejo de Gobierno. Los miembros de la Junta Ejecutiva sirven en mandatos escalonados de cuatro años entre los miembros votantes de la Junta. Otras juntas y comités supervisan publicaciones, premios, ciencia, defensa y más.
NSAOrganizationBodiesENG: The Partnership for Maternal, Newborn and Child Health (PMNCH), Global Health Council, Cuban Society of Public Health, Alliance of Public Health Associations of the Americas, World Federation of Public Health Associations
NSAOrganizationBodiesSPA: La Alianza para la Salud Materna, Neonatal y Infantil (PMNCH), Consejo de Salud Global, Sociedad Cubana de Salud Pública, Alianza de Asociaciones de Salud Pública de las Américas, Federación Mundial de Asociaciones de Salud Pública.

NSAObjectivesFRE: La mission de l'APHA est d'améliorer la santé du public et d'atteindre l'équité en matière de santé. La vision de l'organisation est de créer la nation la plus saine en une génération. Les valeurs de l'organisation reflètent les croyances de ses membres provenant de toutes les disciplines de la santé publique et de plus de 40 pays. L'APHA publie l'American Journal of Public Health et le journal The Nation's Health. Lors de la Réunion Annuelle, des milliers de personnes partagent les dernières recherches en santé publique. L'APHA mène des campagnes de sensibilisation telles que Get Ready et la Semaine Nationale de la Santé Publique.
NSAObjectivesPOR: A missão da APHA é melhorar a saúde da população e alcançar a equidade no estado de saúde. A visão da organização é criar a nação mais saudável em uma geração. Os valores da organização refletem as crenças de seus membros de todas as disciplinas da saúde pública e de mais de 40 países. A APHA publica o American Journal of Public Health e o jornal The Nation's Health. Na Reunião Anual, milhares de pessoas compartilham as mais recentes pesquisas em saúde pública. A APHA lidera campanhas de conscientização pública, como Get Ready e a Semana Nacional de Saúde Pública.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
L'APHA travaille à améliorer l'accès aux soins, à promouvoir l'équité en santé et à soutenir l'infrastructure de santé publique. L'APHA se concentre également sur les défis de santé émergents auxquels nous sommes confrontés à notre époque.  
Les principaux domaines de travail sont : la réforme de la santé, la préparation aux urgences, la santé dans toutes les politiques, le changement climatique, la santé environnementale, les maladies chroniques, la conception de communautés saines, les transports, le tabac, la violence armée, la prévention des blessures et la santé reproductive, pour n'en nommer que quelques-uns.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
A APHA trabalha para melhorar o acesso aos cuidados, promover a equidade em saúde e apoiar a infraestrutura de saúde pública. A APHA também se concentra nos desafios de saúde emergentes enfrentados em nosso tempo atual.  
As principais áreas de atuação são: reforma da saúde, preparação para emergências, saúde em todas as políticas, mudanças climáticas, saúde ambiental, doenças crônicas, design de comunidades saudáveis, transporte, tabaco, violência armada, prevenção de lesões e saúde reprodutiva, para citar algumas.
NSABoardMembersFRE: L'APHA est dirigée par des membres élus et nommés. Le Conseil d'administration élit le Bureau exécutif, les responsables de l'Association et les membres honoraires. Les membres du Conseil d'administration exercent des mandats échelonnés de deux ans. Le Bureau exécutif travaille dans cinq domaines : politique, gestion, programme, adhésion et déploiement. Le Conseil est un organe de 24 membres qui agit au nom du Conseil d'administration. Les membres du Bureau exécutif exercent des mandats échelonnés de quatre ans parmi les membres votants du Conseil. D'autres conseils et comités supervisent les publications, les prix, la science, le plaidoyer et plus encore.

NSAObjetivesENG: APHA’s mission is to improve public health and achieve health equity. The organization's vision is to create the healthiest nation within a generation. Its values reflect the beliefs of its members from various public health disciplines and over 40 countries. APHA publishes the American Journal of Public Health and The Nation's Health newspaper. At the Annual Meeting, thousands of individuals share the latest research in public health. APHA also leads public awareness campaigns such as Get Ready and National Public Health Week.
NSAObjetivesSPA: La misión de APHA es mejorar la salud del público y lograr la equidad en el estado de salud. La visión de la organización es crear la nación más saludable en una generación. Los valores de la organización reflejan las creencias de sus miembros de todas las disciplinas de la salud pública y de más de 40 países. APHA publica el American Journal of Public Health y el periódico The Nation's Health. En la Reunión Anual, miles de personas comparten la investigación más reciente en salud pública. APHA lidera campañas de concienciación pública como Get Ready y la Semana Nacional de la Salud Pública.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
APHA works to improve access to care, promote health equity, and support public health infrastructure. APHA also addresses emerging health challenges that we face today.  
The main areas of focus include health reform, emergency preparedness, health in all policies, climate change, environmental health, chronic diseases, healthy community design, transportation, tobacco, gun violence, injury prevention, and reproductive health, among others.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
La APHA trabaja para mejorar el acceso a la atención, lograr la equidad en salud y apoyar la infraestructura de salud pública. La APHA también se centra en los desafíos de salud emergentes que enfrentamos en la actualidad.  
Los principales campos de trabajo son: reforma de la salud, preparación para emergencias, salud en todas las políticas, cambio climático, salud ambiental, enfermedades crónicas, diseño de comunidades saludables, transporte, tabaco, violencia armada, prevención de lesiones y salud reproductiva, por nombrar algunos.
NSABoardMembersENG: APHA is governed by elected and appointed members. The Governing Council elects the Executive Board, the officers of the Association, and honorary members. Members of the Governing Council serve staggered two-year terms. The Executive Board carries out work in five areas: policy, management, program, membership, and deployment. The Board is a 24-member body that serves on behalf of the Governing Council. Members of the Executive Board serve staggered four-year terms among the voting members of the Board. Other boards and committees oversee publications, awards, science, advocacy, and more.
NSABoardMembersSPA: APHA está gobernada por miembros elegidos y designados. El Consejo de Gobierno elige a la Junta Ejecutiva, a los oficiales de la Asociación y a los miembros honorarios. Los miembros del Consejo de Gobierno sirven mandatos escalonados de dos años. La Junta Ejecutiva lleva a cabo su trabajo en cinco áreas: política, gestión, programas, membresía y despliegue. La Junta es un cuerpo de 24 miembros que actúa en nombre del Consejo de Gobierno. Los miembros de la Junta Ejecutiva sirven en mandatos escalonados de cuatro años entre los miembros votantes de la Junta. Otras juntas y comités supervisan publicaciones, premios, ciencia, defensa y más.
NSAOrganizationBodiesENG: The Partnership for Maternal, Newborn and Child Health (PMNCH), Global Health Council, Cuban Society of Public Health, Alliance of Public Health Associations of the Americas, World Federation of Public Health Associations
NSAOrganizationBodiesSPA: La Alianza para la Salud Materna, Neonatal y Infantil (PMNCH), Consejo de Salud Global, Sociedad Cubana de Salud Pública, Alianza de Asociaciones de Salud Pública de las Américas, Federación Mundial de Asociaciones de Salud Pública.

NSAObjectivesFRE: La mission de l'APHA est d'améliorer la santé du public et d'atteindre l'équité en matière de santé. La vision de l'organisation est de créer la nation la plus saine en une génération. Les valeurs de l'organisation reflètent les croyances de ses membres provenant de toutes les disciplines de la santé publique et de plus de 40 pays. L'APHA publie l'American Journal of Public Health et le journal The Nation's Health. Lors de la Réunion Annuelle, des milliers de personnes partagent les dernières recherches en santé publique. L'APHA mène des campagnes de sensibilisation telles que Get Ready et la Semaine Nationale de la Santé Publique.
NSAObjectivesPOR: A missão da APHA é melhorar a saúde da população e alcançar a equidade no estado de saúde. A visão da organização é criar a nação mais saudável em uma geração. Os valores da organização refletem as crenças de seus membros de todas as disciplinas da saúde pública e de mais de 40 países. A APHA publica o American Journal of Public Health e o jornal The Nation's Health. Na Reunião Anual, milhares de pessoas compartilham as mais recentes pesquisas em saúde pública. A APHA lidera campanhas de conscientização pública, como Get Ready e a Semana Nacional de Saúde Pública.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:

Les principaux domaines de travail sont : la réforme de la santé, la préparation aux urgences, la santé dans toutes les politiques, le changement climatique, la santé environnementale, les maladies chroniques, la conception de communautés saines, les transports, le tabac, la violence armée, la prévention des blessures et la santé reproductive, pour n'en nommer que quelques-uns.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:

As principais áreas de atuação são: reforma da saúde, preparação para emergências, saúde em todas as políticas, mudanças climáticas, saúde ambiental, doenças crônicas, design de comunidades saudáveis, transporte, tabaco, violência armada, prevenção de lesões e saúde reprodutiva, para citar algumas.

Title: Global Oncology (GO)
NSAObjectives: Global Oncology collaborates across geographic, professional and academic borders to build capacity of health systems; to educate patients, caregivers, and the general public; and to mobilize the global cancer community to stem the growing tide of cancer in low- and middle-income countries.
NSAWorkFields:
Capacity building: Train clinical staff in cancer care delivery; design clinical operations and patient navigation systems
Education: Design cancer education tools for patients, caregivers, and the general public
Technical cooperation: Participate in national cancer control planning with key stakeholders
NSABoardMembers:

Dr. Franklin Huang, MD PhD, Asst. Prof. of Medicine, UCSF

History:
Luna, Cristina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 12/23/2025 11:21:10 AM. Comments: ERP recommends proceeding to renew the status as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. In addition, the FENSA review and risk analysis conducted by LEG has also been carefully considered. While ERP supports moving forward with the renewal based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 12/16/2025 10:02:30 AM. Comments: LEG has reviewed the renewal application submitted by Global Oncology and considers that its renewal for official relations with PAHO/WHO may be approved. During the due diligence and risk assessment, LEG did not identify any conflict of interest or reputational risk that could compromise the integrity or reputation of the Organization. LEG reviewed the financial information provided by Global Oncology and noted that, although some recent fiscal years showed negative operating results, the organization maintains an overall stable financial structure, and no material risks to its institutional continuity were observed. It was also verified that contributions received from the private sector have been limited. All board members, donors, and collaborators were screened through independent searches and cross-checked against the United Nations Security Council Consolidated Sanctions List, with no adverse findings. Overall, Global Oncology has maintained close and sustained collaboration with PAHO in implementing the workplan activities for the 2023–2025 period. LEG reviewed the additional explanatory comments provided and considers that they demonstrate a substantial and mutually beneficial engagement. For the proposed workplan for 2026–2028, LEG similarly reviewed the additional comments submitted and did not identify risks or conflicts of interest associated with the proposed activities, which clearly outline PAHO’s role and the expected outcomes. Therefore, LEG does not object to recommending that Global Oncology renew its status in official relations with PAHO.
Nicole Kim responded with additional information as NSA Originator on 12/3/2025 08:17:47 PM. Comments: Additional information about 2023-2025 activities: 1. Trainings on medical oncology Description: Conduct didactic and hands-on trainings on medical oncology for clinical staff at emerging and/or established public oncology programs in selected countries in Latin America and the Caribbean. Design clinical operations and patient navigation systems that allow real-time tracking of patients throughout their cancer journeys. Expected result/outcome: Staff show demonstrable increase in knowledge in selected topic areas of medical oncology. 2. Workshops on cancer patient navigation principles Description: Conduct workshops on the principles of cancer patient navigation, drawing on a detailed case study of a novel patient navigation program developed by GO in Belize. Expected result/outcome: Participants understand the principles of cancer patient navigation and a detailed case study of the GO-DOC patient navigation program in Belize. Results: 6 permanent staff and additional rotating nurses have begun training on nurse navigation using the GO-DOC framework, with monthly virtual mentorship and semi-annual to quarterly in-person training. Official data for the GO-DOC patient navigation program from 2023-2025 has been collected and will be published as a report. 3. Technical assistance in national cancer control planning Description: Provide technical assistance to national cancer control planning efforts in selected countries in the Region, and guidance on developing clinical operations and patient navigation systems to enhance diagnosis and treatment of cancer patients. Expected result/outcome: Key national stakeholders from public, private, and civil sectors have identified gaps, challenges, and opportunities in cancer prevention and control and developed strategic and operational plans for addressing this disease burden. Results: Gaps in care that have been identified in collaboration with the Belizean Ministry of Health, Social Security Board, and KHMH Oncology unit are 1. Accessible radiation treatments 2. Pediatric oncology care 3. Country-wide screening services for most cancer types including breast and cervical cancers (the most common in Belize). Global Oncology has worked to engage local stakeholders to understand current needs and has advised on strategic plans for each of these points and are awaiting updates and requests for additional work from national stakeholders. GO has also shared with PAHO and MoH the framework for the hospital-based cancer navigation system that has been implemented in the Oncology unit by GO and KHMH and offered to collaborate to provide data on cancer cases seen at the hospital. Additional information about 2026-2028 proposed workplan: 1. Continued training and operational development for nurses, pharmacists, and oncology clinic staff : Ongoing hands-on and didactic oncology training for nurses, pharmacists, and clinic staff, along with support for developing and strengthening clinical operations. GO role: Global Oncology will provide technical expertise to develop and deliver comprehensive training for nurses, pharmacists, and oncology clinic staff. This includes designing curricula, offering hands-on and didactic sessions, and supporting operational systems to strengthen workflows, patient and staff safety, and patient care delivery. PAHO role: PAHO will coordinate with MoH to ensure that the trainings are aligned with national priorities and regional competency frameworks. They also support integration of the training programs into the national health system to promote sustainability. Collaboration type: This is a joint implementation effort, with GO leading the technical content and training delivery, while PAHO ensures alignment with national and regional standards and facilitates coordination with health authorities. Supports PAHO Strategic Plan: This activity strengthens the oncology workforce, improves the quality of patient care, and advances equitable access to cancer services in line with PAHO’s goals for health workforce development and regional health system strengthening. 2. Continued support for medical oncology through tumor boards and gynecologic oncology surgery : Expansion of specialist oncology support through regular multidisciplinary tumor boards and gynecologic oncology surgical capacity building. GO role: Global Oncology provides ongoing clinical support by facilitating multidisciplinary tumor boards and offering guidance for gynecologic oncology surgeries. GO experts share case-based knowledge, provide mentorship, and support decision-making for complex patient cases. PAHO role: PAHO liaises with the Ministry of Health to ensure these clinical support activities are embedded within national cancer care programs. They help align the efforts with regional standards and provide visibility to successful practices that can be replicated in other countries. Collaboration type: This is a joint technical support effort, where GO leads the clinical guidance and tumor board facilitation, while PAHO ensures coordination with national stakeholders and supports integration into broader regional cancer care strategies. Supports PAHO Strategic Plan: This activity enhances clinical decision-making, increases access to specialized oncology care, and promotes evidence-based treatment pathways, contributing to PAHO’s objectives of improving quality of care and strengthening health services for cancer patients. 3. Breast cancer prevention education through development and distribution of an educational comic book: Development, updating, and distribution of a culturally relevant breast cancer prevention comic book for community education based in Belize. GO role: Global Oncology leads the creation, updating, and distribution of a culturally appropriate comic book that educates communities about breast cancer prevention, early detection, and health-promoting behaviors. GO ensures the content is evidence-based, accessible, and tailored to local populations. PAHO role: PAHO provides technical guidance to verify the accuracy of health information, ensure alignment with regional health communication standards, and support the dissemination of the educational materials through national and regional networks. Collaboration type: The partnership involves joint technical review and coordinated dissemination, with GO producing and distributing the materials while PAHO facilitates reach and ensures consistency with public health messaging. Supports PAHO Strategic Plan: This initiative advances early detection of breast cancer, improves health literacy, and reduces inequities in cancer prevention, aligning with PAHO’s strategic goals for promoting preventive care and community health education. 4. Collaboration with the Belizean Ministry of Health & Wellness and Belize Cancer Society for breast and cervical cancer prevention outreach: Coordinated outreach campaigns and community education on breast and cervical cancer prevention and screening such as booth at Pink Power Health Expo with Belize Cancer Society. GO role: Global Oncology supports community outreach by providing technical expertise, educational materials, and guidance on linking women to screening and prevention services. GO helps design campaigns and coordinates community engagement efforts. PAHO role: PAHO ensures that outreach activities align with national health priorities and regional prevention strategies. They coordinate with the Ministry of Health and civil society partners to facilitate implementation and provide oversight to maintain quality and consistency. Collaboration type: This is a joint implementation activity, with GO and PAHO sharing responsibilities for planning, technical guidance, and execution of outreach campaigns. Supports PAHO Strategic Plan: This collaboration promotes women’s health, increases equitable access to breast and cervical cancer screening, and strengthens community-level engagement in preventive care, directly contributing to PAHO’s strategic objectives for cancer prevention and health equity. Requested financial information for 2023 and 2024 are uploaded in the mandatory documents section.

Luna, Cristina (Consultant WDC) requested additional information from "NSA Originator" as ERP Reviewer on 11/6/2025 10:47:14 AM. Comments: Dear GO colleagues, Thank you for the timely submission of the renewal form. After reviewing it, we identified some points that require clarification to ensure consistency with the 2023–2025 approved work plan and also regarding the proposed 2026–2028 work plan. Please review the observations below and share any additional details through the system. If needed, you can discuss with the PAHO focal point for further guidance. Past Activities (2023–2025) The approved work plan 2023-2025 included three distinct collaboration lines/activities: 1. Trainings on medical oncology Description:Conduct didactic and hands-on trainings on medical oncology for clinical staff at emerging and/or established public oncology programs in selected countries in Latin America and the Caribbean. Design clinical operations and patient navigation systems that allow real-time tracking of patients throughout their cancer journeys. Expected result/outcome: Staff show demonstrable increase in knowledge in selected topic areas of medical oncology. 2. Workshops on cancer patient navigation principles Description: Conduct workshops on the principles of cancer patient navigation, drawing on a detailed case study of a novel patient navigation program developed by GO in Belize. Expected result/outcome: Participants understand the principles of cancer patient navigation and a detailed case study of the GO-DOC patient navigation program in Belize. 3. Technical assistance in national cancer control planning Description: Provide technical assistance to national cancer control planning efforts in selected countries in the Region, and guidance on developing clinical operations and patient navigation systems to enhance diagnosis and treatment of cancer patients. Expected result/outcome: Key national stakeholders from public, private, and civil sectors have identified gaps, challenges, and opportunities in cancer prevention and control and developed strategic and operational plans for addressing this disease burden. From what is reported, the training of oncology nurses and pharmacists in Belize seems partially aligned with the first approved line (Trainings on medical oncology). However, there is no clear reference to activities under the other two lines. Could you clarify whether any actions were taken under these lines and include them in this report? If additional activities were implemented, please add them in a separate box for this reporting period. Also, please expand the description of reported actions to explain scope/objectives, outcomes, and the collaborative role and contributions of GO-PAHO in these activities. Proposed Work Plan (2026–2028) The proposed activities are currently presented as one block. For clarity and to allow for more detailed information on each line of collaboration, we suggest separating them into distinct boxes. Also please expand the description to better explain the specific role and type of collaboration with PAHO for each activity (e.g., technical support, joint implementation, etc.), and the mutual contributions of PAHO and GO and how these activities support PAHO’s Strategic Plan. Thank you for your collaboration and support. Please let us know if you have any questions or need further clarification.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as LEG Reviewer on 10/21/2025 05:32:31 PM. NSAObjectivesFRE: L'Oncologie Mondiale collabore au-delà des frontières géographiques, professionnelles et académiques pour renforcer les capacités des systèmes de santé ; éduquer les patients, les aidants et le grand public ; et mobiliser la communauté mondiale du cancer pour endiguer la marée croissante du cancer dans les pays à revenu faible et intermédiaire.
NSAObjectivesPOR: A Oncologia Global colabora além das fronteiras geográficas, profissionais e acadêmicas para fortalecer a capacidade dos sistemas de saúde; educar pacientes, cuidadores e o público em geral; e mobilizar a comunidade global de câncer para conter a crescente onda de câncer em países de baixa e média renda.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
Renforcement des capacités : Former le personnel clinique à la prestation de soins en oncologie ; concevoir des opérations cliniques et des systèmes de navigation pour les patients  
Éducation : Concevoir des outils d'éducation sur le cancer pour les patients, les aidants et le grand public  
Coopération technique : Participer à la planification nationale de la lutte contre le cancer avec les principales parties prenantes
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
Capacitação: Treinar a equipe clínica na prestação de cuidados oncológicos; projetar operações clínicas e sistemas de navegação para pacientes.  
Educação: Desenvolver ferramentas de educação sobre câncer para pacientes, cuidadores e o público em geral.  
Cooperação técnica: Participar do planejamento nacional de controle do câncer com partes interessadas chave.

NSAObjetivesENG: Global Oncology collaborates across geographic, professional, and academic boundaries to strengthen health system capacities; to educate patients, caregivers, and the general public; and to mobilize the global cancer community to combat the increasing prevalence of cancer in low- and middle-income countries.
NSAObjetivesSPA: La Oncología Global colabora a través de fronteras geográficas, profesionales y académicas para fortalecer la capacidad de los sistemas de salud; educar a pacientes, cuidadores y al público en general; y movilizar a la comunidad global del cáncer para frenar la creciente ola de cáncer en los países de ingresos bajos y medianos.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
Capacity building: Train clinical staff in cancer care delivery; design clinical operations and patient navigation systems.  
Education: Design cancer education tools for patients, caregivers, and the general public.  
Technical cooperation: Participate in national cancer control planning with key stakeholders.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
Desarrollo de capacidades: Capacitar al personal clínico en la atención del cáncer; diseñar operaciones clínicas y sistemas de navegación para pacientes.  
Educación: Diseñar herramientas de educación sobre el cáncer para pacientes, cuidadores y el público en general.  
Cooperación técnica: Participar en la planificación nacional de control del cáncer con las partes interesadas clave.

Title: Task Force for Global Health (TFGH)
NSAFocalpointRole: Director of Global and Federal Affairs

CollabWPActStrategicPlan.Label: OCM 17: Elimination of communicable diseases

History:
Luna, Cristina (WDC) completed the review as the ERP Officer on 12/11/2025 12:18:31 PM. Comments: El informe de progreso ha sido revisado por ERP. No se requiere información adicional.
Saboya, Martha (WDC) submitted request as PAHO Focal Point for review on 10/2/2025 05:40:12 PM. Comments: The report has been completed, and it is in accordance with the established plan of work. PAHO recognizes the value of this partnership and the great opportunities for countries in our Region and for PAHO to advance the Elimination Initiative agenda.
econron re-submitted request as NSA Originator on 10/2/2025 04:08:58 PM. Comments: Added TEPHINET update to section on increasing access to comprehensive and quality health services for disease elimination, as suggested.
Bascolo, Ernesto (WDC) returned submission to NSA Originator as PAHO Focal Point on 9/26/2025 01:37:19 PM. Comments: Thank you, colleagues, for clearly presenting the collaboration we have developed throughout the year. This has been a very valuable partnership that highlights the importance and significance of our joint work, offering great opportunities for countries in our Region and for PAHO to advance the Elimination Initiative agenda. Regarding the activity related to the FETP, I would suggest moving it under the section on increasing access to comprehensive and quality health services for disease elimination. This activity is essentially about training human resources under the One Health approach, which makes it even more consistent to include it in that area, since the framework agreement does not foresee a specific activity on building the technical and operational capacity of the health workforce in PAHO countries for pandemic preparedness, response, and other public health emergencies.
econron has submitted a progress report on 9/25/2025 02:57:10 PM.

History:
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 1/5/2026 12:07:10 PM. Comments: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed the background information for the Federación Panamericana de Asociaciones de Facultades y Escuelas de Medicina (FEPAFEM / PAFAMS), which is applying to be in official relations with PAHO. LEG takes note that, for the purposes of completing the FENSA review, additional information was requested—namely: (i) a complete copy of the audited financial statements, and (ii) a list of donors or funding sources representing 10% or more of total income, including clarification as to whether any funds originate from the pharmaceutical industry. We further note that this information will not be provided. Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO conducts due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-State actors. Accordingly, LEG’s assessment and the comments below are necessarily limited to the documentation that was provided by FEPAFEM and to which LEG has had access. Based on that documentation, we offer the following comments: For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to FEPAFEM the policies and procedures applicable to non-governmental organizations (NGOs). FEPAFEM is a non-profit, international academic and scientific organization constituted in 1962 in Viña del Mar, Chile, with its executive directorate based in Caracas, Venezuela. It functions as a federation of national associations of faculties and schools of medicine across the Americas. Its mission is to promote quality and socially responsible medical education to improve health in the Americas through academic exchange, research, and innovation in medical education. Governance is provided by a General Assembly and an Executive Committee headed by a President (currently Dr. Luis Enrique Podestá Gavilano, Peru). The governance members screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List showed no matches. FEPAFEM declared no links with the tobacco and arms industries on 24 September 2025; no such links were identified by LEG based on the information reviewed. The risk analysis consists of identifying potential risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of the proposed collaboration, with a view to protecting and preserving PAHO’s integrity and reputation and mitigating conflicts of interest. Based solely on the information available to LEG, we found no evidence of financial, ethical, or reputational concerns that would compromise PAHO’s reputation. FEPAFEM’s profile and activities are consistent with PAHO’s Strategic Plan, and its prior collaboration with PAHO has been positive. On that basis, and taking into account the limitations resulting from the absence of the requested financial and donor information, LEG recommends proceeding with the designation, provided that ERP is satisfied that PAHO’s integrity, independence, and reputation will not be affected and that the collaboration promotes PAHO’s objectives. Please do not hesitate to contact LEG should you require further assistance. Sincerely, LEG
Luna, Cristina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 12/23/2025 11:08:00 AM. Comments: ERP recommends proceeding to renew the status as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. However, ERP notices that LEG is conducting the FENSA review and risk analysis and ERP final recommendation is conditional to its completion and clearance. While ERP supports moving forward with the renewal based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.

Por favor, le pedimos revisar las observaciones a continuación y compartir detalles adicionales a través del sistema. Si es necesario, tras su revisión, podemos enviar un pedido de información a través del sistema a FEPAFEM para más aclaraciones. Actividades pasadas (2023–2025) El reporte requiere precisar el rol específico de OPS (por ejemplo, coorganización, asistencia técnica) y ampliar la información para mostrar el carácter colaborativo de las actividades y su alineación con las tres líneas de colaboración aprobadas en el plan de trabajo 2023–2025. Por favor notar que el reporte no está organizado con base al plan aprobado. Le enviaré por correo el documento completo aprobado por los Estados Miembros para referencia. Observaciones puntuales sobre actividades reportadas: 1. El reporte menciona la creación del Programa Panamericano de Alta Gerencia y Liderazgo en Educación Médica (PAG-FEPAFEM), la Plataforma Panamericana de Movilidad Docente-Estudiantil y un grupo de trabajo sobre Diversidad, Inclusión y Equidad. o ¿Podría aclarar cuál fue el rol específico de OPS en cada una de estas acciones? o ¿Qué acciones se desarrollaron de manera colaborativa entre ambas entidades? o ¿Podrían incluir cómo estas actividades se relacionan con las tres líneas aprobadas y si se avanzó en otras áreas/actividades descritas en el plan de trabajo? Plan trienal propuesto 2026–2028 Se identifican aspectos que requieren mayor precisión para evidenciar la naturaleza colaborativa del plan. Por favor puede especificar cómo OPS y FEPAFEM trabajarán juntas en cada línea propuesta (por ejemplo, ¿coorganización, asistencia técnica, apoyo normativo?). Es positivo que las actividades se construya sobre el plan anterior, pero es importante que en el apartado de actividades pasadas se refleje claramente si hubo avances en estas áreas, Le agradecemos por su colaboración y estamos a su disposición en el caso de tener preguntas.

Title: Confederación Latinoamericana de Bioquímica Clínica (COLABIOCLI)
NSAObjectives:
La Confederación Latinoamericana de Bioquímica Clínica (COLABIOCLI) es una
asociación civil de carácter científico, académico y gremial, sin fines de lucro, que
agrupa a entidades nacionales de profesionales que se dedican a la bioquímica clínica
o profesiones similares en América Latina.
Su propósito principal es el constante mejoramiento de la profesión en sus aspectos
éticos, científicos, técnicos y económicos, para servir a los individuos y la sociedad a
través de los siguientes objetivos:
• Reconocimiento en cada país una entidad nacional, procurando su afiliación a la
confederación.
• Promoción de la conciencia gremial y el espíritu de solidaridad entre los
bioquímicos clínicos de todo el mundo, y en particular los de América Latina.
• Colaboración y asesoramiento a autoridades y asociaciones de profesionales en
el estudio y solución de problemas sanitarios, profesionales y universitarios.
• Establecimiento y promoción de vínculos con asociaciones de todo tipo en el
ámbito de la bioquímica clínica y ciencias del laboratorio clínico.
• Difusión de información a través de publicaciones periódicas de temas científicos,
gremiales, profesionales y la actividad de la Institución.
NSAWorkFields:
• La agrupación de las asociaciones nacionales que se dedican al desarrollo de las
ciencias del laboratorio.
• La organización y promoción de eventos científicos y gremiales, así como de
programas de evaluación externa de calidad.
• La aplicación de normas de acreditación y el establecimiento de programas de
posgrado en los países miembros.
NSABoardMembers:
Los socios de COLABIOCLI son entidades afiliadas, académicas y colaboradores. Las
entidades afiliadas son aquellas asociaciones, sociedades y colegios profesionales
nacionales dedicados a la bioquímica o química clínica. Las entidades académicas son
instituciones docentes en educación superior e investigación. Los afiliados
colaboradores son instituciones que realizan su accionar en el ámbito de la bioquímica
clínica y ciencias de laboratorio y deben ser avaladas por la entidad nacional afiliada.
Se afilia una entidad nacional por país miembro. Todas las entidades nacionales tienen
derecho a voto.
Actualmente las asociaciones y colegios profesionales de los 19 países siguientes son
miembros de COLABIOCLI: Argentina, Bolivia, Brasil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica,
Ecuador, España, Guatemala, Honduras, Italia, México, Nicaragua, Panamá, Paraguay,
Portugal, República Dominicana, Uruguay y Venezuela.
NSAOrganizationBodies: Federación Internacional de Química Clínica (IFCC, por su sigla en inglés).

Title: American Heart Association (AHA)
NSAObjectives:
Over our 100-year history, the American Heart Association has been fighting heart disease and stroke and helping families and communities thrive. Building on over 100 years of trusted leadership in cardiovascular and brain 
health, by 2028 the AHA will drive breakthroughs and implement proven solutions in science, policy, and care for healthier people and communities. The greatest discoveries in health must reach people where they are. Our mission is to advance health and hope for everyone, everywhere.
NSAWorkFields:
Access to Care: As a step toward ensuring access to care for all:
• Preserve & expand Medicaid coverage 
• Increase enrollment in ACA marketplace exchange plans

Quality Care: As a step toward improving care wherever it is delivered, expand quality 
improvement in:
• Clinics (ex. Target: BP) 
• Hospitals (ex. Get With the Guidelines) 
• Post-acute facilities (ex. Certification)

Health Factors: As a step toward transformational impact in Cardio Kidney Metabolic Health & Primary Care: 
• Improve blood pressure control through Target: BP 

CPR: As a step toward our Nation of Lifesavers goal to double out of hospital cardiac arrest survival by 2030:
• Improve bystander-initiated CPR through policies and programs
NSABoardMembers:
- 25 governing body members: https://www.heart.org/en/about-us#executive 


Affiliations:
- American Stroke Association is a division of the AHA focuses on stroke prevention and treatment.
- BrightTorch Ventures is a non-profit subsidiary of the AHA that invests in locally-led solutions to health disparities in underrepresented communities.
-Aphelion Capital has partnered with the AHA, UPMC Enterprises, and Philips to form Cardeation Capital. Cardeation is a venture fund formed to invest in innovative healthcare products, services, and technologies that align with the mission of the AHA.
- RQI Partners, a partnership between the AHA and Laerdal Medical, is dedicated to improving survival from cardiac arrest by bringing new solutions to resuscitation education and quality improvement.
- Healthcare Quality and Research Systems, Inc. is a non-profit corporation, formed in Delaware to support the AHA’s mission, including ownership of AHA’s Middle East subsidiaries.
NSAOrganizationBodies:
-United Nations (ECOSOC Status)
- World Heart Federation
- NCD Alliance

Comments: ERP recommends proceeding with the designation as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. While ERP supports moving forward with the designation based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Sophia Nilanont responded with additional information as NSA Originator on 11/14/2025 10:02:05 AM. Comments: Dear AHA colleagues, Thank you for reporting the activities for the 2023–2025 period and for submitting the proposed work plan for 2026–2028. We have a few clarification requests: Report on 2023–2025 Activities

 Q: Activity 1: -Please confirm whether the webinar mentioned refers to “Reduction of sodium in packaged foods: regional workshop on the legislation of regulatory instruments”. If so, kindly include the title and/or objective.
A: No, the webinar was titled the “Program on Social Marketing for Public Health: Best Practices.” It took place on August 30, 2023.

 Q: Could you also confirm the expected date for the launch of the guide on applying Social Marketing to health interventions, as you mention it is “soon to be launched”?

A: First quarter of 2026.

Q: Activity 2: Please clarify whether the responsibility for this activity should reflect AHA (or AHA and PAHO jointly), rather than only PAHO. Please review and adjust accordingly.

A: No, just AHA.

Q: Activity 3: -Similarly, please clarify whether the responsibility should be AHA or AHA and PAHO.
A: It would be just PAHO.

Q: Could you confirm if the regional guidance document mentioned has a title or expected publication date? Also, please indicate whether it is being developed jointly by PAHO and AHA.

A:      Lineamientos regionales sobre políticas de alimentación saludable y actividad física en entornos escolares.
-        Regional guidance on healthy eating and physical activity policies in school environments.
-        First quarter 2026
-  It was developed jointly by PAHO and OAS.

Q: Proposed Work Plan for 2026–2028 - As PAHO is currently listed as the sole responsible entity, please revise to reflect AHA’s role or joint responsibility where applicable. - Where relevant, expand descriptions to clarify the nature of collaboration and the roles of PAHO and AHA (e.g., technical support, advocacy, or co-development of campaign materials and tools).

A: No updates; AHA provides financial support to PAHO to undertake activities, excluding advocacy around maternal health which is just AHA.

Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 11/10/2025 01:27:27 PM. Comments: Dear Leendert Nederveen: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed your FENSA request for the American Heart Association (AHA) concerning the renewal of its status as a non-State actor in official relations with PAHO for the 2026–2028 cycle. LEG recommends proceeding with the renewal, provided that the Focal Point considers that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected and that the collaboration continues to promote the objectives of PAHO. Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO conducts due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-State actors. Based on the documentation provided by AHA and the information available to LEG, we offer the following comments: 1. Category: For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to AHA the policies and procedures applicable to a non-State actor of the non-governmental organization (NGO) category. 2. Legal Status: AHA is a U.S.-registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, incorporated in New York (EIN 13-5613797), with headquarters in Dallas, Texas. It operates as a public charity dedicated to advancing cardiovascular health and scientific research since 1924. 3. Mission and Objectives: AHA’s mission is to be a relentless force for a world of longer, healthier lives. Its objectives include cardiovascular disease prevention, public education, scientific research funding, and advocacy for equitable access to health care. 4. Financial Information: The Association’s main income sources are public donations, grants, and limited corporate sponsorships. Support from the pharmaceutical and medical device sectors represented approximately 3–4% of total revenue for FY2022–FY2024 (based on audited disclosures). 5. Governance: The organization is governed by a national Board of Directors and managed by an executive team led by its Chief Executive Officer. Board members and key officers have been screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List; no matches were identified. 6. Links with Tobacco and Arms: AHA declared no links with the tobacco or arms industries as of September 2025, and no such links were identified by LEG. 7. Affiliations and External Relations: AHA has been in official relations with PAHO since 1964, holds consultative status with the United Nations Economic and Social Council (since 2013), and is a member of the World Heart Federation, which is in official relations with WHO. 8. Engagement with Other Industries: AHA collaborates through its “CEO Roundtable” with private-sector actors including Nestlé, Dow, Chevron, and Amgen. These partnerships are limited to non-normative engagement. Past sponsorships (2011–2015) from Coca-Cola and PepsiCo were publicly disclosed in peer-reviewed literature. Current transparency mechanisms and conflict-of-interest policies mitigate potential reputational risks. LEG’s analysis identifies that AHA maintains extensive multi-sectoral engagement while applying robust transparency and governance mechanisms. No evidence of undue influence from commercial entities was found. Residual risks relate primarily to the perception of association with food and beverage companies; these can be mitigated by ensuring that any joint activity remains non-commercial, transparent, and free from endorsement of private entities. LEG therefore recommends the renewal of AHA’s status as a non-State actor in official relations with PAHO. The Association continues to demonstrate alignment with PAHO’s objectives in the prevention and control of cardiovascular diseases and other noncommunicable conditions. Its activities remain consistent with the principles of FENSA, and no conflicts of interest or reputational risks have been identified. Sincerely, Office of Legal Counsel (LEG)

NSAObjetivesENG: Over our 100-year history, the American Heart Association has been fighting heart disease and stroke and helping families and communities thrive. Building on over 100 years of trusted leadership in cardiovascular and brain health, by 2028 the AHA will drive breakthroughs and implement proven solutions in science, policy, and care for healthier people and communities. The greatest discoveries in health must reach people where they are. Our mission is to advance health and hope for everyone, everywhere.
NSAObjetivesSPA: A lo largo de nuestra historia de 100 años, la Asociación Americana del Corazón ha estado luchando contra las enfermedades del corazón y los accidentes cerebrovasculares, y ayudando a las familias y comunidades a prosperar. Basándose en más de 100 años de liderazgo confiable en salud cardiovascular y cerebral, para 2028 la AHA impulsará avances e implementará soluciones probadas en ciencia, política y atención para personas y comunidades más saludables. Los mayores descubrimientos en salud deben llegar a las personas donde se encuentran. Nuestra misión es promover la salud y la esperanza para todos, en todas partes.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
Access to Care: As a step toward ensuring access to care for all:
• Preserve and expand Medicaid coverage 
• Increase enrollment in ACA marketplace exchange plans

Quality Care: As a step toward improving care wherever it is delivered, expand quality improvement in:
• Clinics (e.g., Target: BP) 
• Hospitals (e.g., Get With the Guidelines) 
• Post-acute facilities (e.g., Certification)

Health Factors: As a step toward transformational impact in Cardio Kidney Metabolic Health and Primary Care: 
• Improve blood pressure control through Target: BP 

CPR: As a step toward our Nation of Lifesavers goal to double out-of-hospital cardiac arrest survival by 2030:
• Improve bystander-initiated CPR through policies and programs
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
Acceso a la Atención: Como un paso hacia garantizar el acceso a la atención para todos:
• Preservar y expandir la cobertura de Medicaid 
• Aumentar la inscripción en los planes de intercambio del mercado de ACA

Atención de Calidad: Como un paso hacia la mejora de la atención donde sea que se brinde, expandir la mejora de la calidad en:
• Clínicas (por ejemplo: Target: BP) 
• Hospitales (por ejemplo: Get With the Guidelines) 
• Instalaciones post-agudas (por ejemplo: Certificación)

Factores de Salud: Como un paso hacia un impacto transformacional en la Salud Cardio Renal Metabólica y la Atención Primaria:
• Mejorar el control de la presión arterial a través de Target: BP 

RCP: Como un paso hacia nuestro objetivo de Nación de Salvavidas de duplicar la supervivencia a paro cardíaco fuera del hospital para 2030:
• Mejorar la RCP iniciada por transeúntes a través de políticas y programas
NSABoardMembersENG:
- 25 governing body members: https://www.heart.org/en/about-us#executive 

Affiliations:
- The American Stroke Association is a division of the AHA that focuses on stroke prevention and treatment.
- BrightTorch Ventures is a non-profit subsidiary of the AHA that invests in locally-led solutions to health disparities in underrepresented communities.
- Aphelion Capital has partnered with the AHA, UPMC Enterprises, and Philips to form Cardeation Capital. Cardeation is a venture fund established to invest in innovative healthcare products, services, and technologies that align with the mission of the AHA.
- RQI Partners, a collaboration between the AHA and Laerdal Medical, is dedicated to improving survival rates from cardiac arrest by introducing new solutions for resuscitation education and quality improvement.
- Healthcare Quality and Research Systems, Inc. is a non-profit corporation established in Delaware to support the AHA’s mission, including the ownership of AHA’s subsidiaries in the Middle East.
NSABoardMembersSPA:
- 25 miembros del órgano de gobierno: https://www.heart.org/en/about-us#executive

Afiliaciones:
- La Asociación Americana del Accidente Cerebrovascular es una división de la AHA que se centra en la prevención y el tratamiento de accidentes cerebrovasculares.
- BrightTorch Ventures es una subsidiaria sin fines de lucro de la AHA que invierte en soluciones lideradas localmente para las disparidades en salud en comunidades subrepresentadas.
- Aphelion Capital se ha asociado con la AHA, UPMC Enterprises y Philips para formar Cardeation Capital. Cardeation es un fondo de inversión creado para invertir en productos, servicios y tecnologías innovadoras en el ámbito de la salud que se alineen con la misión de la AHA.
- RQI Partners, una asociación entre la AHA y Laerdal Medical, está dedicada a mejorar la supervivencia tras un paro cardíaco al ofrecer nuevas soluciones para la educación en resucitación y la mejora de la calidad.
- Healthcare Quality and Research Systems, Inc. es una corporación sin fines de lucro, formada en Delaware para apoyar la misión de la AHA, incluyendo la propiedad de las subsidiarias de la AHA en Oriente Medio.

NSAObjectivesFRE: Au cours de notre histoire de 100 ans, l'American Heart Association a lutté contre les maladies cardiaques et les AVC, aidant les familles et les communautés à prospérer. S'appuyant sur plus de 100 ans de leadership de confiance en matière de santé cardiovasculaire et cérébrale, d'ici 2028, l'AHA fera progresser les découvertes et mettra en œuvre des solutions éprouvées en science, en politique et en soins pour des personnes et des communautés en meilleure santé. Les plus grandes découvertes en matière de santé doivent atteindre les gens là où ils se trouvent. Notre mission est de promouvoir la santé et l'espoir pour tous, partout.
NSAObjectivesPOR: Ao longo de nossa história de 100 anos, a American Heart Association tem lutado contra doenças cardíacas e derrames, ajudando famílias e comunidades a prosperar. Com base em mais de 100 anos de liderança confiável em saúde cardiovascular e cerebral, até 2028, a AHA promoverá avanços e implementará soluções comprovadas em ciência, política e cuidados para pessoas e comunidades mais saudáveis. As maiores descobertas em saúde devem alcançar as pessoas onde elas estão. Nossa missão é promover saúde e esperança para todos, em todos os lugares.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
Accès aux soins : En tant qu'étape vers l'assurance de l'accès aux soins pour tous :
• Préserver et élargir la couverture Medicaid 
• Augmenter l'inscription aux plans d'échange du marché ACA

Qualité des soins : En tant qu'étape vers l'amélioration des soins où qu'ils soient dispensés, élargir l'amélioration de la qualité dans :
• Cliniques (ex. Objectif : BP) 
• Hôpitaux (ex. Get With the Guidelines) 
• Établissements post-aigus (ex. Certification)

Facteurs de santé : En tant qu'étape vers un impact transformationnel dans la santé cardio-rénale métabolique et les soins primaires :
• Améliorer le contrôle de la pression artérielle grâce à l'Objectif : BP 

RCP : En tant qu'étape vers notre objectif Nation de Sauveteurs de doubler le taux de survie après un arrêt cardiaque hors hôpital d'ici 2030 :
• Améliorer la RCP initiée par des témoins grâce à des politiques et des programmes
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
Acesso aos Cuidados: Como um passo para garantir o acesso aos cuidados para todos:
• Preservar e expandir a cobertura do Medicaid 
• Aumentar a adesão aos planos de troca do mercado ACA

Cuidados de Qualidade: Como um passo para melhorar os cuidados onde quer que sejam prestados, expandir a melhoria da qualidade em:
• Clínicas (ex. Meta: BP) 
• Hospitais (ex. Get With the Guidelines) 
• Instalações pós-agudas (ex. Certificação)

Fatores de Saúde: Como um passo para um impacto transformacional na Saúde Cardio Renal Metabólica e Cuidados Primários:
• Melhorar o controle da pressão arterial através da Meta: BP 

RCP: Como um passo em direção ao nosso objetivo de Nação de Salvadores de Dobrar a sobrevivência de paradas cardíacas fora do hospital até 2030:
• Melhorar a RCP iniciada por transeuntes através de políticas e programas
NSABoardMembersFRE:
### Traduction

- 25 membres de l'organe directeur : https://www.heart.org/en/about-us#executive

Affiliations :
- L'American Stroke Association est une division de l'AHA qui se concentre sur la prévention et le traitement des AVC.
- BrightTorch Ventures est une filiale à but non lucratif de l'AHA qui investit dans des solutions dirigées localement pour réduire les disparités en matière de santé dans les communautés sous-représentées.
- Aphelion Capital a établi un partenariat avec l'AHA, UPMC Enterprises et Philips pour former Cardeation Capital. Cardeation est un fonds de capital-risque créé pour investir dans des produits, services et technologies de santé innovants qui s'alignent sur la mission de l'AHA.
- RQI Partners, un partenariat entre l'AHA et Laerdal Medical, est dédié à l'amélioration de la survie après un arrêt cardiaque en apportant de nouvelles solutions à l'éducation à la réanimation et à l'amélioration de la qualité.
- Healthcare Quality and Research Systems, Inc. est une corporation à but non lucratif, formée dans le Delaware pour soutenir la mission de l'AHA, y compris la propriété des filiales de l'AHA au Moyen-Orient.

Afiliações:

- A BrightTorch Ventures é uma subsidiária sem fins lucrativos da AHA que investe em soluções lideradas localmente para disparidades de saúde em comunidades sub-representadas.
- A Aphelion Capital se associou à AHA, UPMC Enterprises e Philips para formar a Cardeation Capital. A Cardeation é um fundo de investimento criado para investir em produtos, serviços e tecnologias inovadoras em saúde que estejam alinhados com a missão da AHA.
- A RQI Partners, uma parceria entre a AHA e a Laerdal Medical, é dedicada a melhorar a sobrevivência em casos de parada cardíaca, trazendo novas soluções para a educação em ressuscitação e melhoria da qualidade.
- A Healthcare Quality and Research Systems, Inc. é uma corporação sem fins lucrativos, formada em Delaware para apoiar a missão da AHA, incluindo a propriedade das subsidiárias da AHA no Oriente Médio.

NSAObjetivesENG: Over our 100-year history, the American Heart Association has been fighting heart disease and stroke and helping families and communities thrive. Building on over 100 years of trusted leadership in cardiovascular and brain health, by 2028 the AHA will drive breakthroughs and implement proven solutions in science, policy, and care for healthier people and communities. The greatest discoveries in health must reach people where they are. Our mission is to advance health and hope for everyone, everywhere.
NSAObjetivesSPA: A lo largo de nuestra historia de 100 años, la Asociación Americana del Corazón ha estado luchando contra las enfermedades del corazón y los accidentes cerebrovasculares, y ayudando a las familias y comunidades a prosperar. Basándose en más de 100 años de liderazgo confiable en salud cardiovascular y cerebral, para 2028 la AHA impulsará avances e implementará soluciones probadas en ciencia, política y atención para personas y comunidades más saludables. Los mayores descubrimientos en salud deben llegar a las personas donde se encuentran. Nuestra misión es promover la salud y la esperanza para todos, en todas partes.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
Access to Care: As a step toward ensuring access to care for all:
• Preserve and expand Medicaid coverage 
• Increase enrollment in ACA marketplace exchange plans

Quality Care: As a step toward improving care wherever it is delivered, expand quality improvement in:
• Clinics (e.g., Target: BP) 
• Hospitals (e.g., Get With the Guidelines) 
• Post-acute facilities (e.g., Certification)

Health Factors: As a step toward transformational impact in Cardio Kidney Metabolic Health and Primary Care: 
• Improve blood pressure control through Target: BP 

CPR: As a step toward our Nation of Lifesavers goal to double out-of-hospital cardiac arrest survival by 2030:
• Improve bystander-initiated CPR through policies and programs
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
Acceso a la Atención: Como un paso hacia garantizar el acceso a la atención para todos:
• Preservar y expandir la cobertura de Medicaid 
• Aumentar la inscripción en los planes de intercambio del mercado de ACA

Atención de Calidad: Como un paso hacia la mejora de la atención donde sea que se brinde, expandir la mejora de la calidad en:
• Clínicas (por ejemplo: Target: BP) 
• Hospitales (por ejemplo: Get With the Guidelines) 
• Instalaciones post-agudas (por ejemplo: Certificación)

Factores de Salud: Como un paso hacia un impacto transformacional en la Salud Cardio Renal Metabólica y la Atención Primaria:
• Mejorar el control de la presión arterial a través de Target: BP 

RCP: Como un paso hacia nuestro objetivo de Nación de Salvavidas de duplicar la supervivencia a paro cardíaco fuera del hospital para 2030:
• Mejorar la RCP iniciada por transeúntes a través de políticas y programas
NSABoardMembersENG:
- 25 governing body members: https://www.heart.org/en/about-us#executive 

Affiliations:
- The American Stroke Association is a division of the AHA that focuses on stroke prevention and treatment.
- BrightTorch Ventures is a non-profit subsidiary of the AHA that invests in locally-led solutions to health disparities in underrepresented communities.
- Aphelion Capital has partnered with the AHA, UPMC Enterprises, and Philips to form Cardeation Capital. Cardeation is a venture fund established to invest in innovative healthcare products, services, and technologies that align with the mission of the AHA.
- RQI Partners, a collaboration between the AHA and Laerdal Medical, is dedicated to improving survival rates from cardiac arrest by introducing new solutions for resuscitation education and quality improvement.
- Healthcare Quality and Research Systems, Inc. is a non-profit corporation established in Delaware to support the AHA’s mission, including the ownership of AHA’s subsidiaries in the Middle East.
NSABoardMembersSPA:
- 25 miembros del órgano de gobierno: https://www.heart.org/en/about-us#executive

Afiliaciones:
- La Asociación Americana del Accidente Cerebrovascular es una división de la AHA que se centra en la prevención y el tratamiento de accidentes cerebrovasculares.
- BrightTorch Ventures es una subsidiaria sin fines de lucro de la AHA que invierte en soluciones lideradas localmente para las disparidades en salud en comunidades subrepresentadas.
- Aphelion Capital se ha asociado con la AHA, UPMC Enterprises y Philips para formar Cardeation Capital. Cardeation es un fondo de inversión creado para invertir en productos, servicios y tecnologías innovadoras en el ámbito de la salud que se alineen con la misión de la AHA.
- RQI Partners, una asociación entre la AHA y Laerdal Medical, está dedicada a mejorar la supervivencia tras un paro cardíaco al ofrecer nuevas soluciones para la educación en resucitación y la mejora de la calidad.
- Healthcare Quality and Research Systems, Inc. es una corporación sin fines de lucro, formada en Delaware para apoyar la misión de la AHA, incluyendo la propiedad de las subsidiarias de la AHA en Oriente Medio.

NSAObjectivesFRE: Au cours de notre histoire de 100 ans, l'American Heart Association a lutté contre les maladies cardiaques et les AVC, aidant les familles et les communautés à prospérer. S'appuyant sur plus de 100 ans de leadership de confiance en matière de santé cardiovasculaire et cérébrale, d'ici 2028, l'AHA fera progresser les découvertes et mettra en œuvre des solutions éprouvées en science, en politique et en soins pour des personnes et des communautés en meilleure santé. Les plus grandes découvertes en matière de santé doivent atteindre les gens là où ils se trouvent. Notre mission est de promouvoir la santé et l'espoir pour tous, partout.
NSAObjectivesPOR: Ao longo de nossa história de 100 anos, a American Heart Association tem lutado contra doenças cardíacas e derrames, ajudando famílias e comunidades a prosperar. Com base em mais de 100 anos de liderança confiável em saúde cardiovascular e cerebral, até 2028, a AHA promoverá avanços e implementará soluções comprovadas em ciência, política e cuidados para pessoas e comunidades mais saudáveis. As maiores descobertas em saúde devem alcançar as pessoas onde elas estão. Nossa missão é promover saúde e esperança para todos, em todos os lugares.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE:
Accès aux soins : En tant qu'étape vers l'assurance de l'accès aux soins pour tous :
• Préserver et élargir la couverture Medicaid 
• Augmenter l'inscription aux plans d'échange du marché ACA

Qualité des soins : En tant qu'étape vers l'amélioration des soins où qu'ils soient dispensés, élargir l'amélioration de la qualité dans :
• Cliniques (ex. Objectif : BP) 
• Hôpitaux (ex. Get With the Guidelines) 
• Établissements post-aigus (ex. Certification)

Facteurs de santé : En tant qu'étape vers un impact transformationnel dans la santé cardio-rénale métabolique et les soins primaires :
• Améliorer le contrôle de la pression artérielle grâce à l'Objectif : BP 

RCP : En tant qu'étape vers notre objectif Nation de Sauveteurs de doubler le taux de survie après un arrêt cardiaque hors hôpital d'ici 2030 :
• Améliorer la RCP initiée par des témoins grâce à des politiques et des programmes
NSAWorkFieldsPOR:
Acesso aos Cuidados: Como um passo para garantir o acesso aos cuidados para todos:
• Preservar e expandir a cobertura do Medicaid 
• Aumentar a adesão aos planos de troca do mercado ACA

Cuidados de Qualidade: Como um passo para melhorar os cuidados onde quer que sejam prestados, expandir a melhoria da qualidade em:
• Clínicas (ex. Meta: BP) 
• Hospitais (ex. Get With the Guidelines) 
• Instalações pós-agudas (ex. Certificação)

Fatores de Saúde: Como um passo para um impacto transformacional na Saúde Cardio Renal Metabólica e Cuidados Primários:
• Melhorar o controle da pressão arterial através da Meta: BP 

RCP: Como um passo em direção ao nosso objetivo de Nação de Salvadores de Dobrar a sobrevivência de paradas cardíacas fora do hospital até 2030:
• Melhorar a RCP iniciada por transeuntes através de políticas e programas

Title: Basic Health International (BHI)
NSAObjectives: The Corporation is incorporated under the Nonprofit Corporation Law of 1988, as amended, of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, and shall be organized and operated exclusively for charitable, scientific, literary and educational purposes permitted within the scope of Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended ("Code"), including without limitation to provide essential primary care and to conduct public health research in developing countries as well as to provide cervical cancer screening and treatment to women. In furtherance of these purposes, the Corporation may exercise all rights and powers conferred by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania upon nonprofit corporations.
NSAWorkFields:
1. Clinical research for the prevention and treatment of cervical cancer.
2. Implementation of validated cervical cancer prevention technologies or processes.
3. Technical education of medical staff.
