History:
Luna, Cristina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 12/19/2025 04:00:59 PM. Comments: ERP recommends proceeding to renew the status as NSA in Official Relations, taking into consideration that the workplan and reporting arrangements have been reviewed and approved by the relevant technical focal point. In addition, the FENSA review and risk analysis conducted by LEG has also been carefully considered. While ERP supports moving forward with the renewal based on these established reviews and assessments, it is important to note that safeguarding the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO is a shared institutional responsibility, informed by the roles and due diligence of all involved entities.
Maza, Mauricio Antonio (WDC) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 12/16/2025 04:07:25 PM. Comments: Estimada Florencia, gracias por tu recomendación. En esta fase consideramos que no es conveniente separar las actividades en componentes independientes. El detalle de apoyo específico por país y las reuniones se ajusta continuamente y, en el contexto actual, no es posible desagregar. Proponemos mantener un paquete único de trabajo con resultados esperados a nivel regional y reportar avances por ejes temáticos y por país conforme se confirmen agendas y compromisos.
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 12/16/2025 02:44:25 PM. Comments: Dear colleagues: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed your FENSA request for Basic Health International Inc. (BHI) to renew their status as non-state actor in official relations with PAHO. Subject to the considerations indicated below, including LEG’s recommended edits to the workplan, LEG recommendation is to proceed with the renewal of the status, as long as the Focal Point believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO carries out due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-state actors. Based on the documentation provided by Basic Health International Inc. and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments: For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to BHI the policies and procedures applicable to nongovernmental organizations (NGOs). BHI is a United States–registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, incorporated in Ohio, USA, in 1997. It holds tax-exempt status and operates as an independent public charity. The entity’s stated mission is to eliminate cervical cancer worldwide through prevention, screening, and treatment initiatives, with a particular focus on underserved populations in Latin America and the Caribbean. Its objectives align with WHO/PAHO’s strategic goals on noncommunicable disease prevention and women’s health. BHI’s principal income sources include grants, charitable donations, and partnerships with health ministries and public institutions. No funding or affiliations were identified with commercial or high-risk industries. Governance: BHI’s Board of Directors is composed of medical and public health professionals. All members were screened against the UN Security Council Consolidated List — no matches were found. BHI declared no links with tobacco and arms industries on 1 October 2025; and no links were uncovered by LEG either. The organization’s work is health-oriented and includes collaboration with public entities and civil society, with no evidence of conflict of interest or undue industry influence. LEG did not uncover any significant links with other potentially problematic industries. The risk analysis consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the potential benefits of participating in this collaboration, while protecting and preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. In our searches, we found no material reputational, financial, or ethical risks associated with Basic Health International Inc. The organization maintains a clear record of compliance, transparency, and positive engagement with WHO and PAHO. It has been in official relations with PAHO for several years, and the engagement has been consistent with PAHO’s Strategic Plan. As LEG previously indicated, in order to ensure the presentation of a robust work plan and effective reporting of progress, we recommend that the proposed activities be separated into distinct lines of collaboration. Beyond this point, LEG has no objections to renewing BHI as a non-State Actor in Official Relations with PAHO for the upcoming cycle. Sincerely, Office of Legal Counsel (LEG)
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as LEG Reviewer on 12/10/2025 03:05:01 PM. Comments: Estimado Mauricio, gracias por las aclaraciones respecto del plan de trabajo. Teniendo en cuenta las distintas actividades incluidas en el plan, la recomendación de LEG es que las mismas se separen como distintas actividades. Esto permitira reflejar un plan de trabajo robusto, y un reporte de los avances efectivo. Siguiendo en esta línea, recomendamos que para cada una de las actividades se identifiquen los resultados esperados concretos. Saludos,
Maza, Mauricio Antonio (WDC) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 12/8/2025 02:04:51 PM. Comments: Dear Cristina I have reviewed the document, and I would rephrase the expected results: Support the implementation of a national cervical cancer elimination program across various countries in the Americas, including Antigua and Barbuda, St Vincent and the Grenadines, among others. • Support PAHO to execute the Unitaid grant, which implements new HPV genotyping testing in 5 member states. • BHI will continue to participate in PAHO meetings and workshops to bring in our expertise and network to help implement the plan of action for cervical cancer elimination 2018-30. • Support PAHO in other activities as needed, such as in-country assessment of local cervical cancer prevention programs, training of healthcare professionals to strengthen cervical cancer screening and treatment programs.

Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) requested additional information from "NSA Originator" as LEG Reviewer on 12/8/2025 11:30:48 AM. emumenthaler@basichealth.org responded with additional information as NSA Originator on 12/7/2025 07:07:04 AM. We are grateful for the opportunity to contribute to PAHO's important work!

NSAObjetivesENG: The Corporation is incorporated under the Nonprofit Corporation Law of 1988, as amended, of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, and shall be organized and operated exclusively for charitable, scientific, literary, and educational purposes permitted within the scope of Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended ("Code"), including, without limitation, providing essential primary care and conducting public health research in developing countries, as well as providing cervical cancer screening and treatment to women. In furtherance of these purposes, the Corporation may exercise all rights and powers conferred by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania upon nonprofit corporations.
NSAObjetivesSPA: La Corporación está constituida bajo la Ley de Corporaciones Sin Fines de Lucro de 1988, según enmendada, del Commonwealth de Pennsylvania, y se organizará y operará exclusivamente con fines benéficos, científicos, literarios y educativos permitidos dentro del alcance de la Sección 501(c)(3) del Código de Rentas Internas de 1986, según enmendado ("Código"), incluyendo, sin limitación, proporcionar atención primaria esencial y llevar a cabo investigaciones en salud pública en países en desarrollo, así como proporcionar exámenes y tratamientos para el cáncer cervical a mujeres. En cumplimiento de estos propósitos, la Corporación podrá ejercer todos los derechos y poderes conferidos por las leyes del Commonwealth de Pennsylvania a las corporaciones sin fines de lucro.
NSAWorkFieldsENG:
1. Clinical research for the prevention and treatment of cervical cancer.  
2. Implementation of validated cervical cancer prevention technologies or processes.  
3. Technical education of medical staff.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA:
1. Investigación clínica para la prevención y tratamiento del cáncer de cuello uterino.  
2. Implementación de tecnologías o procesos validados para la prevención del cáncer de cuello uterino.  
3. Educación técnica del personal médico.

NSAObjectivesFRE: La Corporation est constituée en vertu de la Loi sur les Corporations à But Non Lucratif de 1988, telle que modifiée, du Commonwealth de Pennsylvanie, et sera organisée et exploitée exclusivement à des fins caritatives, scientifiques, littéraires et éducatives autorisées dans le cadre de l'article 501(c)(3) du Code des impôts de 1986, tel que modifié ("Code"), y compris, sans s'y limiter, la fourniture de soins primaires essentiels et la réalisation de recherches en santé publique dans les pays en développement, ainsi que la fourniture de dépistage et de traitement du cancer du col de l'utérus aux femmes. Title: Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute (CLSI)
NSAObjectives:
The mission of Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute (CLSI) is to develop clinical and laboratory practices and promote their use worldwide. Working together, CLSI staff and volunteer experts guide the development and promote the use of voluntary laboratory standards and guidelines within the U.S. and international health care communities. 

CLSI facilitates a unique process of developing these testing standards based on input from and consensus among industry, government, and health care professionals. Improving the quality of patient care by fostering excellence in laboratory medicine is the driving force behind the creation of every CLSI product and program.
NSAWorkFields: CLSI provides standards, guidelines, education materials, training, and informational webinars in 11 main focus areas relevant to medical laboratories and device developers including automation, clinical chemistry and technology, pre-examination, hematology, microbiology, molecular methods, and newborn screening, among others.

History:
Campa Sole, Regina (WDC) has completed the ERP review on 1/9/2026 04:22:35 PM. Comments:
Abregu, Maria Florencia (WDC) has completed the LEG review on 1/9/2026 04:17:54 PM. Comments: Dear colleagues: Thank you for contacting LEG. We have reviewed your FENSA request for the Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute (CLSI) to renew their status as non-state actor in official relations with PAHO. Subject to the considerations indicated below, including LEG’s observations regarding the financial information provided, LEG’s recommendation is to proceed with the review, as long as the responsible Focal Point believes that the integrity, independence, and reputation of PAHO will not be affected, and that the collaboration promotes the objectives of PAHO. Within the framework of FENSA, PAHO carries out due diligence and a risk assessment of proposed collaborations with non-state actors. Based on the documentation provided by CLSI and to which LEG has had access, we offer the following comments: For the purposes of FENSA, LEG applies to CLSI the policies and procedures applicable to non-governmental organizations. CLSI is a not-for-profit organization incorporated in the United States of America, legally constituted under U.S. law, and operating as an international standards-developing organization in the field of clinical and laboratory medicine. CLSI’s mission is to develop and promote consensus-based clinical and laboratory standards and guidelines aimed at improving the quality of health care and laboratory practices globally. With regard to financial information, LEG notes that it had access only to CLSI’s 2023 IRS Form 990. At the time of this review, audited financial statements were not available to LEG. CLSI’s main sources of income include membership fees, sales of standards and publications, grants, and contributions from philanthropic and corporate partners. CLSI is governed by a Board of Directors composed of representatives from government, academia, and the diagnostics and pharmaceutical sectors. Board members and key administrators were screened against the United Nations Security Council Consolidated List, and no matches were identified. CLSI declared no links with the tobacco and arms industries on September 2025; and no links were uncovered by LEG either. LEG notes that CLSI maintains relationships with pharmaceutical and diagnostics companies through philanthropic contributions and participation in its governance structures. The risk analysis consists of identifying possible risks and weighing them against the possible benefits of participating in this collaboration, while protecting and preserving the integrity and reputation of PAHO and mitigating any type of conflict of interest. In our searches, we found that CLSI is a well-established and reputable technical organization whose mission and activities are aligned with PAHO’s public health objectives, particularly in laboratory quality management and standards strengthening. In relation to the participation of the pharmaceutical sector on the board of CLSI, it should be noted that such ties are openly disclosed and form part of CLSI’s model of including government, academia, and industry experts in a balanced way. There is no indication of any negative incident (e.g., scandal or undue influence) arising from these pharmaceutical or diagnostics industry connections; rather, they are part of CLSI’s standard collaborations. Therefore, LEG considers that this is not an impediment to advance a joint work plan. Subject to the considerations indicated above, CLSI’s renewal may proceed, as long as the collaborations with PAHO are not earmarked or directly financed by private sector (including but not limited to diagnostics, pharmaceutical, alcohol, or food and beverage sectors) and do not create a perceived or actual conflict of interest. bEST REGARDS,

Ramon-Pardo, Pilar (WDC) responded with additional information as PAHO Focal Point on 11/25/2025 04:24:18 PM. Comments: Dear Cristina, It is important to acknowledge that some of the planned activities for 2022–2024 were not implemented due to various factors. Nevertheless, collaboration has continued through efforts to provide and ensure access to microbiology laboratory standards across Latin America and the Caribbean via ReLAVRA+. The 2026–2028 work plan remains broad at this stage, as specific activities will be defined once a CLSI focal point is designated for PAHO. In the meantime, PAHO will maintain its participation in relevant committees and continue facilitating access to CLSI standards. We hope this helps. With thanks, Pilar
Luna, Cristina (Consultant WDC) requested additional information from "PAHO Focal Point" as ERP Reviewer on 11/11/2025 11:31:14 AM. Comments: Dear Pilar, Thank you for clearing the CLSI renewal form. After reviewing it in ERP, we identified points that need clarification to ensure consistency with the 2022–2024 approved work plan and to clarify the proposed 2026–2028 plan. Please review the observations below and share any additional details through the system. If needed, we can consult with the NSA for further clarification. Past Activities (2022–2024) The approved plan included three lines of collaboration (I am sending the 2022 background paper with the full plan by email): 1. Collaborate to strengthen the capacities of the national laboratories in the PAHO region to monitor and mitigate the spread of antimicrobial resistance in bacterial pathogens. This included the development of materials and resources written for the PAHO community, strengthening national policies and guidelines, training and workshops, and strengthening of surveillance infrastructure. 2. Collaborate to strengthen the capacities of the national laboratories in the PAHO region to monitor and mitigate the spread of antifungal resistance, in particular for those causing invasive fungal infections. This included the development of materials and resources written for the PAHO community, strengthening national policies and guidelines, training and workshops, and strengthening of surveillance infrastructure. 3. Collaborate to strengthen the capacities of the national and clinical laboratories in the Region of the Americas to monitor the spread of antimicrobial resistance and assess the impact of interventions. This included the development of materials and resources written for the PAHO community, strengthening national policies and guidelines, training and workshops, and strengthening of surveillance data management, as well as contributing to identify new standards, guidelines, and documents to be developed based on the Latin American epidemiology. Reported activities are aligned with the overall objective, but do not clearly indicate progress on several components of the approved plan. For example: • The report does not mention whether materials and resources were developed for the PAHO community. • There is no reference to national policy support or guidelines. • Activities related to antifungal resistance are only partially addressed (adaptation of tables), without details on capacity-building or surveillance infrastructure. • There is no information on evaluation of interventions or identification of new standards based on regional epidemiology. Could you please expand the information on what has been implemented and ensure consistency with what was proposed and approved? Proposed Work Plan (2026–2028) The proposed plan outlines important objectives but remains very general. Could you please: • Provide more details on the planned collaborative activities, including specific actions and the expected contributions from CLSI and PAHO to be implemented during the period. • If appropriate, indicate whether activities will include capacity-building components, policy support, or adaptation of standards for regional needs, as these were part of the previous plan and could help build on past achievements. Thank you for your collaboration and support.

TitleENGSPA: Instituto de Normas Clínicas y de Laboratorio
NSAObjetivesENG:
The mission of the Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute (CLSI) is to develop clinical and laboratory practices and promote their use worldwide. Working together, CLSI staff and volunteer experts guide the development and promote the use of voluntary laboratory standards and guidelines within the U.S. and international health care communities.

CLSI facilitates a unique process of developing these testing standards based on input from and consensus among industry, government, and health care professionals. Improving the quality of patient care by fostering excellence in laboratory medicine is the driving force behind the creation of every CLSI product and program.
NSAObjetivesSPA:
La misión del Instituto de Normas Clínicas y de Laboratorio (CLSI) es desarrollar prácticas clínicas y de laboratorio y promover su uso en todo el mundo. Trabajando juntos, el personal de CLSI y expertos voluntarios guían el desarrollo y promueven el uso de normas y directrices voluntarias de laboratorio dentro de las comunidades de atención médica de EE. UU. y a nivel internacional.

CLSI facilita un proceso único para desarrollar estas normas de prueba basado en la opinión y el consenso de la industria, el gobierno y los profesionales de la salud. Mejorar la calidad de la atención al paciente fomentando la excelencia en la medicina de laboratorio es la fuerza impulsora detrás de la creación de cada producto y programa de CLSI.
NSAWorkFieldsENG: CLSI provides standards, guidelines, educational materials, training, and informational webinars in 11 main focus areas relevant to medical laboratories and device developers, including automation, clinical chemistry and technology, pre-examination, hematology, microbiology, molecular methods, and newborn screening, among others.
NSAWorkFieldsSPA: CLSI proporciona estándares, directrices, materiales educativos, capacitación y seminarios web informativos en 11 áreas principales de enfoque relevantes para laboratorios médicos y desarrolladores de dispositivos, incluyendo automatización, química clínica y tecnología, preexamen, hematología, microbiología, métodos moleculares y cribado neonatal, entre otros.

TitleFRE: Institut des normes cliniques et de laboratoire (CLSI)
TitlePOR: Instituto de Padrões Clínicos e de Laboratório (CLSI)
NSAObjectivesFRE:
La mission de l'Institut des normes cliniques et de laboratoire (CLSI) est de développer des pratiques cliniques et de laboratoire et de promouvoir leur utilisation dans le monde entier. En travaillant ensemble, le personnel du CLSI et des experts bénévoles guident le développement et promeuvent l'utilisation de normes et de directives volontaires pour les laboratoires au sein des communautés de soins de santé aux États-Unis et à l'international.

Le CLSI facilite un processus unique de développement de ces normes de test basé sur les contributions et le consensus de l'industrie, du gouvernement et des professionnels de la santé. Améliorer la qualité des soins aux patients en favorisant l'excellence en médecine de laboratoire est la force motrice derrière la création de chaque produit et programme du CLSI.
NSAObjectivesPOR:
A missão do Instituto de Padrões Clínicos e de Laboratório (CLSI) é desenvolver práticas clínicas e laboratoriais e promover seu uso em todo o mundo. Trabalhando juntos, a equipe do CLSI e especialistas voluntários orientam o desenvolvimento e promovem o uso de padrões e diretrizes laboratoriais voluntários dentro das comunidades de saúde dos EUA e internacionais.

O CLSI facilita um processo único de desenvolvimento desses padrões de teste com base nas contribuições e no consenso entre a indústria, o governo e os profissionais de saúde. Melhorar a qualidade do atendimento ao paciente, promovendo a excelência na medicina laboratorial, é a força motriz por trás da criação de cada produto e programa do CLSI.
NSAWorkFieldsFRE: CLSI fournit des normes, des directives, des matériaux éducatifs, des formations et des webinaires d'information dans 11 domaines principaux pertinents pour les laboratoires médicaux et les développeurs de dispositifs, y compris l'automatisation, la chimie clinique et la technologie, la pré-examen, l'hématologie, la microbiologie, les méthodes moléculaires et le dépistage néonatal, entre autres.
NSAWorkFieldsPOR: A CLSI fornece padrões, diretrizes, materiais educacionais, treinamento e webinars informativos em 11 áreas principais de foco relevantes para laboratórios médicos e desenvolvedores de dispositivos, incluindo automação, química clínica e tecnologia, pré-exame, hematologia, microbiologia, métodos moleculares e triagem de recém-nascidos, entre outros.
